Genesis1:5 God called the light day
Genesis1:6 Then God said

Genesis1:8 God called the expanse sky. Evening came
Genesis1:9 Then God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry land earth
Genesis1:11 Then God said

Exodus23:1 You must not spread a false report. Do not join the wicked to be a malicious witness.
Exodus23:2 You must not follow a crowd in wrongdoing. Do not testify in a lawsuit and go along with a crowd to pervert [justice].
Exodus23:3 Do not show favoritism to a poor person in his lawsuit.
Exodus23:4 If you come across your enemy's stray ox or donkey
Exodus23:5 If you see the donkey of someone who hates you lying [helpless] under its load
Exodus23:6 You must not deny justice to the poor among you in his lawsuit.
Exodus23:7 Stay far away from a false accusation. Do not kill the innocent and the just
Exodus23:8 You must not take a bribe
Exodus23:9 You must not oppress a foreign resident; you yourselves know how it feels to be a foreigner because you were foreigners in the land of Egypt.
Exodus23:10 Sow your land for six years and gather its produce.
Exodus23:11 But during the seventh year you are to let it rest and leave it uncultivated
Exodus23:12 Do your work for six days but rest on the seventh day so that your ox and your donkey may rest
Exodus23:13 Pay strict attention to everything I have said to you. You must not invoke the names of other gods; they must not be heard on your lips.
Exodus23:14 Celebrate a festival in My honor three times a year.
Exodus23:15 Observe the Festival of Unleavened Bread. As I commanded you
Exodus23:16 Also [observe] the Festival of Harvest with the firstfruits of your produce from what you sow in the field
Exodus23:17 Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord God.
Exodus23:18 You must not offer the blood of My sacrifices with anything leavened. The fat of My festival offering must not remain until morning.
Exodus23:19 Bring the best of the firstfruits of your land to the house of the LORD your God. You must not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
Exodus23:20 I am going to send an Angel before you to protect you on the way and bring you to the place I have prepared.
Exodus23:21 Be attentive to Him and listen to His voice. Do not defy Him
Exodus23:22 But if you will carefully obey Him and do everything I say
Exodus23:23 For My Angel will go before you and bring you to [the land of] the Amorites
Exodus23:24 You must not bow down to their gods or worship them. Do not imitate their practices. Instead
Exodus23:25 Worship the LORD your God
Exodus23:26 No woman will miscarry or be barren in your land. I will give [you] the full number of your days.
Exodus23:27 I will cause the people ahead of you to feel terror and throw into confusion all the nations you come to. I will make all your enemies turn their backs to you in retreat.
Exodus23:28 I will send the hornet in front of you
Exodus23:29 I will not drive them out ahead of you in a single year; otherwise
Exodus23:30 I will drive them out little by little ahead of you until you have become numerous and take possession of the land.
Exodus23:31 I will set your borders from the Red Sea to the Mediterranean Sea
Exodus23:32 You must not make a covenant with them or their gods.
Exodus23:33 They must not remain in your land
Exodus24:1 Then He said to Moses
Exodus24:2 Moses alone is to approach the LORD
Exodus24:3 Moses came and told the people all the commands of the LORD and all the ordinances. Then all the people responded with a single voice
Exodus24:4 And Moses wrote down all the words of the LORD. He rose early the next morning and set up an altar and 12 pillars for the 12 tribes of Israel at the base of the mountain.
Exodus24:5 Then he sent out young Israelite men
Exodus24:6 Moses took half the blood and set it in basins; the [other] half of the blood he sprinkled on the altar.
Exodus24:7 He then took the covenant scroll and read [it] aloud to the people. They responded
Exodus24:8 Moses took the blood
Exodus24:9 Then Moses went up with Aaron
Exodus24:10 and they saw the God of Israel. Beneath His feet was something like a pavement made of sapphire stone
Exodus24:11 God did not harm the Israelite nobles; they saw Him
Exodus24:12 The LORD said to Moses
Exodus24:13 So Moses arose with his assistant Joshua
Exodus24:14 He told the elders
Exodus24:15 When Moses went up the mountain
Exodus24:16 The glory of the LORD settled on Mount Sinai
Exodus24:17 The appearance of the LORD's glory to the Israelites was like a consuming fire on the mountaintop.
Exodus24:18 Moses entered the cloud as he went up the mountain
Exodus25:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus25:2 Tell the Israelites to take an offering for Me. You are to take My offering from everyone whose heart stirs him [to give].
Exodus25:3 This is the offering you are to receive from them: gold
Exodus25:4 blue
Exodus25:5 ram skins dyed red and manatee skins; acacia wood;
Exodus25:6 oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense;
Exodus25:7 and onyx along with [other] gemstones for mounting on the ephod and breastpiece.
Exodus25:8 They are to make a sanctuary for Me so that I may dwell among them.
Exodus25:9 You must make [it] according to all that I show you-- the design of the tabernacle as well as the design of all its furnishings.
Exodus25:10 They are to make an ark of acacia wood
Exodus25:11 Overlay it with pure gold; overlay it both inside and out. Also make a gold molding all around it.
Exodus25:12 Cast four gold rings for it and place [them] on its four feet
Exodus25:13 Make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Exodus25:14 Insert the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark in order to carry the ark with them.
Exodus25:15 The poles are to remain in the rings of the ark; they must not be removed from it.
Exodus25:16 Put the [tablets of the] testimony that I will give you into the ark.
Exodus25:17 Make a mercy seat of pure gold
Exodus25:18 Make two cherubim of gold; make them of hammered work at the two ends of the mercy seat.
Exodus25:19 Make one cherub at one end and one cherub at the other end. Make the cherubim of one piece with the mercy seat at its two ends.
Exodus25:20 The cherubim are to have wings spread out above
Exodus25:21 Set the mercy seat on top of the ark and put the testimony that I will give you into the ark.
Exodus25:22 I will meet with you there above the mercy seat
Exodus25:23 You are to construct a table of acacia wood
Exodus25:24 Overlay it with pure gold and make a gold molding all around it.
Exodus25:25 Make a three-inch frame all around it and make a gold molding for it all around its frame.
Exodus25:26 Make four gold rings for it
Exodus25:27 The rings should be next to the frame as holders for the poles to carry the table.
Exodus25:28 Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold so that the table can be carried by them.
Exodus25:29 You are also to make its plates and cups
Exodus25:30 Put the bread of the Presence on the table before Me at all times.
Exodus25:31 You are to make a lampstand out of pure
Exodus25:32 Six branches are to extend from its sides
Exodus25:33 There are to be three cups shaped like almond blossoms
Exodus25:34 There are to be four cups shaped like almond blossoms on the lampstand [shaft] along with its calyxes and petals.
Exodus25:35 For the six branches that extend from the lampstand
Exodus25:36 Their calyxes and branches are to be of one piece. All of it is to be a single hammered piece of pure gold.
Exodus25:37 Make seven lamps on it. Its lamps are to be set up so they illuminate the area in front of it.
Exodus25:38 Its snuffers and firepans must be of pure gold.
Exodus25:39 The lampstand with all these utensils is to be made from 75 pounds of pure gold.
Exodus25:40 Be careful to make [everything] according to the model of them you have been shown on the mountain.
Exodus26:1 You are to construct the tabernacle itself with 10 curtains. You must make them of finely spun linen
Exodus26:2 The length of each curtain should be 42 feet
Exodus26:3 Five of the curtains should be joined together
Exodus26:4 Make loops of blue yarn on the edge of the last curtain in the [first] set
Exodus26:5 Make 50 loops on the one curtain and make 50 loops on the edge of the curtain in the second set
Exodus26:6 Also make 50 gold clasps and join the curtains together with the clasps
Exodus26:7 You are to make curtains of goat hair for a tent over the tabernacle; make 11 of these curtains.
Exodus26:8 The length of each curtain should be 45 feet and the width of each curtain six feet. All 11 curtains are to have the same measurements.
Exodus26:9 Join five of the curtains by themselves
Exodus26:10 Make 50 loops on the edge of the one curtain
Exodus26:11 Make 50 bronze clasps; put the clasps through the loops and join the tent together so that it is a single unit.
Exodus26:12 As for the flap that is left over from the tent curtains
Exodus26:13 The half yard on one side and the half yard on the other of what is left over along the length of the tent curtains should be hanging down over the sides of the tabernacle on either side to cover it.
Exodus26:14 Make a covering for the tent from ram skins dyed red
Exodus26:15 You are to make upright planks of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
Exodus26:16 The length of each plank is to be 15 feet
Exodus26:17 Each plank must be connected together with two tenons. Do the same for all the planks of the tabernacle.
Exodus26:18 Make the planks for the tabernacle as follows: 20 planks for the south side
Exodus26:19 and make 40 silver bases under the 20 planks
Exodus26:20 20 planks for the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:21 along with their 40 silver bases
Exodus26:22 and make six planks for the west side of the tabernacle.
Exodus26:23 Make two additional planks for the two back corners of the tabernacle.
Exodus26:24 They are to be paired at the bottom
Exodus26:25 There are to be eight planks with their silver bases: 16 bases; two bases under the first plank and two bases under each plank.
Exodus26:26 You are to make five crossbars of acacia wood for the planks on one side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:27 five crossbars for the planks on the other side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:28 The central crossbar is to run through the middle of the planks from one end to the other.
Exodus26:29 Then overlay the planks with gold
Exodus26:30 You are to set up the tabernacle according to the plan for it that you have been shown on the mountain.
Exodus26:31 You are to make a veil of blue
Exodus26:32 Hang it on four gold-plated posts of acacia wood that have gold hooks [and that stand] on four silver bases.
Exodus26:33 Hang the veil under the clasps and bring the ark of the testimony there behind the veil
Exodus26:34 Put the mercy seat on the ark of the testimony in the most holy place.
Exodus26:35 Place the table outside the veil and the lampstand on the south side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:36 For the entrance to the tent you are to make a screen embroidered with blue
Exodus26:37 Make five posts of acacia wood for the screen and overlay them with gold; their hooks are to be gold
Exodus27:1 You are to construct the altar of acacia wood. The altar must be square
Exodus27:2 Make horns for it on its four corners; the horns are to be of one piece. Overlay it with bronze.
Exodus27:3 Make its pots for removing ashes
Exodus27:4 Construct a grate for it of bronze mesh
Exodus27:5 Set it below
Exodus27:6 Then make poles for the altar
Exodus27:7 The poles are to be inserted into the rings
Exodus27:8 Construct the altar with boards so that it is hollow. They are to make it just as it was shown to you on the mountain.
Exodus27:9 You are to make the courtyard for the tabernacle. [Make the hangings] on the south of the courtyard out of finely spun linen
Exodus27:10 There are to be 20 posts and 20 bronze bases. The hooks and bands of the posts must be silver.
Exodus27:11 Then [make the hangings] on the north side 150 [feet] long. There are to be 20 posts and 20 bronze bases. The hooks and bands of the posts must be silver.
Exodus27:12 [Make] the hangings of the courtyard on the west side 75 feet long
Exodus27:13 Make the hangings of the courtyard on the east side toward the sunrise 75 feet.
Exodus27:14 [Make] the hangings on one side [of the gate] 22 and a half feet
Exodus27:15 And make the hangings on the other side 22 and a half [feet]
Exodus27:16 The gate of the courtyard is to have a thirty-foot screen embroidered with blue
Exodus27:17 All the posts around the courtyard are to be banded with silver and have silver hooks and bronze bases.
Exodus27:18 The length of the courtyard is to be 150 feet
Exodus27:19 All the tools of the tabernacle for every use and all its tent pegs as well as all the tent pegs of the courtyard are to be made of bronze.
Exodus27:20 You are to command the Israelites to bring you pure oil from crushed olives for the light
Exodus27:21 In the tent of meeting outside the veil that is in front of the testimony
Exodus28:1 Have your brother Aaron
Exodus28:2 Make holy garments for your brother Aaron
Exodus28:3 You are to instruct all the skilled craftsmen
Exodus28:4 These are the garments that they must make: a breastpiece
Exodus28:5 They should use gold; blue
Exodus28:6 They are to make the ephod of finely spun linen embroidered with gold
Exodus28:7 It must have two shoulder pieces attached to its two edges so that it can be joined together.
Exodus28:8 The artistically woven waistband that is on the ephod must be of one piece
Exodus28:9 Take two onyx stones and engrave on them the names of Israel's sons:
Exodus28:10 six of their names on the first stone and the remaining six names on the second stone
Exodus28:11 Engrave the two stones with the names of Israel's sons as a gem cutter engraves a seal. Mount them
Exodus28:12 Fasten both stones on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the Israelites. Aaron will carry their names on his two shoulders before the LORD as a reminder.
Exodus28:13 Fashion gold filigree settings
Exodus28:14 and two chains of pure gold; you will make them of braided cord work
Exodus28:15 You are to make an embroidered breastpiece for decisions. Make it with the same workmanship as the ephod; make it of gold
Exodus28:16 It must be square and folded double
Exodus28:17 Place a setting of gemstones on it
Exodus28:18 the second row
Exodus28:19 the third row
Exodus28:20 and the fourth row
Exodus28:21 The 12 stones are to correspond to the names of Israel's sons. Each stone must be engraved like a seal
Exodus28:22 You are to make braided chains of pure gold cord work for the breastpiece.
Exodus28:23 Fashion two gold rings for the breastpiece and attach them to its two corners.
Exodus28:24 Then attach the two gold cords to the two gold rings at the corners of the breastpiece.
Exodus28:25 Attach the other ends of the two cords to the two filigree settings and in this way attach [them] to the ephod's shoulder pieces in the front.
Exodus28:26 Make two [other] gold rings and put them at the two other corners of the breastpiece on the edge that is next to the inner border of the ephod.
Exodus28:27 Make two [more] gold rings and attach them to the bottom of the ephod's two shoulder pieces on its front
Exodus28:28 The craftsmen are to tie the breastpiece from its rings to the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue yarn
Exodus28:29 Whenever he enters the sanctuary
Exodus28:30 Place the Urim and Thummim in the breastpiece for decisions
Exodus28:31 You are to make the robe of the ephod entirely of blue yarn.
Exodus28:32 There should be an opening at its top in the center of it. Around the opening
Exodus28:33 Make pomegranates of blue
Exodus28:34 [so that] gold bells and pomegranates alternate around the lower hem of the robe.
Exodus28:35 The robe must be [worn by] Aaron whenever he ministers
Exodus28:36 You are to make a plate of pure gold and engrave it
Exodus28:37 Fasten it to a cord of blue yarn so it can be placed on the turban; the plate is to be on the front of the turban.
Exodus28:38 It will be on Aaron's forehead so that Aaron may bear the guilt connected with the holy offerings that the Israelites consecrate as all their holy gifts. It is always to be on his forehead
Exodus28:39 You are to weave the tunic from fine linen
Exodus28:40 Make tunics
Exodus28:41 Put these on your brother Aaron and his sons; then anoint
Exodus28:42 Make them linen undergarments to cover [their] naked bodies; they must extend from the waist to the thighs.
Exodus28:43 These must be [worn by] Aaron and his sons whenever they enter the tent of meeting or approach the altar to minister in the sanctuary [area]
Exodus29:1 This is what you are to do for them to consecrate them to serve Me as priests. Take a young bull and two unblemished rams
Exodus29:2 with unleavened bread
Exodus29:3 put them in a basket
Exodus29:4 Bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance to the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus29:5 Then take the garments and clothe Aaron with the tunic
Exodus29:6 Put the turban on his head and place the holy diadem on the turban.
Exodus29:7 Take the anointing oil
Exodus29:8 You must also bring his sons
Exodus29:9 fasten headbands on them
Exodus29:10 You are to bring the bull to the front of the tent of meeting
Exodus29:11 Slaughter the bull before the LORD at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Exodus29:12 Take some of the bull's blood and apply [it] to the horns of the altar with your finger; then pour out all the [rest] of the blood at the base of the altar.
Exodus29:13 Take all the fat that covers the entrails
Exodus29:14 But burn up the bull's flesh
Exodus29:15 Take one ram
Exodus29:16 You are to slaughter the ram
Exodus29:17 Cut the ram into pieces. Wash its entrails and shanks
Exodus29:18 Then burn the whole ram on the altar; it is a burnt offering to the LORD. It is a pleasing aroma
Exodus29:19 You are to take the second ram
Exodus29:20 Slaughter the ram
Exodus29:21 Take some of the blood that is on the altar and some of the anointing oil
Exodus29:22 Take the fat from the ram
Exodus29:23 take one loaf of bread
Exodus29:24 and put all of them in the hands of Aaron and his sons and wave them as a presentation offering before the LORD.
Exodus29:25 Take them from their hands and burn [them] on the altar on top of the burnt offering
Exodus29:26 Take the breast from the ram of Aaron's ordination and wave it as a presentation offering before the LORD; it is to be your portion.
Exodus29:27 Consecrate for Aaron and his sons the breast of the presentation offering that is waved and the thigh of the contribution that is lifted up from the ram of ordination.
Exodus29:28 This will belong to Aaron and his sons as a regular portion from the Israelites
Exodus29:29 The holy garments that belong to Aaron are to belong to his sons after him
Exodus29:30 Any priest who is one of his sons and who succeeds him and enters the tent of meeting to minister in the sanctuary must wear them for seven days.
Exodus29:31 You are to take the ram of ordination and boil its flesh in a holy place.
Exodus29:32 Aaron and his sons are to eat the meat of the ram and the bread that is in the basket at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Exodus29:33 They must eat those things by which atonement was made at the [time of] their ordination and consecration. An unauthorized person must not eat [them]
Exodus29:34 If any of the meat of ordination or any of the bread is left until morning
Exodus29:35 This is what you are to do for Aaron and his sons based on all I have commanded you. Ordain them for seven days.
Exodus29:36 Sacrifice a bull as a sin offering each day for atonement. Purify the altar when you make atonement for it
Exodus29:37 For seven days you must make atonement for the altar and consecrate it. The altar will become especially holy; whatever touches the altar will become holy.
Exodus29:38 This is what you are to offer regularly on the altar every day: two year-old lambs.
Exodus29:39 In the morning offer one lamb
Exodus29:40 With the first lamb offer two quarts of fine flour mixed with one quart of crushed olive oil
Exodus29:41 You are to offer the second lamb at twilight. Offer a grain offering and a drink offering with it
Exodus29:42 This will be a regular burnt offering throughout your generations at the entrance to the tent of meeting before the LORD
Exodus29:43 I will also meet with the Israelites there
Exodus29:44 I will consecrate the tent of meeting and the altar; I will also consecrate Aaron and his sons to serve Me as priests.
Exodus29:45 I will dwell among the Israelites and be their God.
Exodus29:46 And they will know that I am the LORD their God
Exodus30:1 You are to make an altar for the burning of incense; make it of acacia wood.
Exodus30:2 It must be square
Exodus30:3 Overlay its top
Exodus30:4 Make two gold rings for it under the molding on two of its sides; put these on opposite sides of it to be holders for the poles to carry it with.
Exodus30:5 Make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold.
Exodus30:6 You are to place the altar in front of the veil by the ark of the testimony-- in front of the mercy seat that is over the testimony-- where I will meet with you.
Exodus30:7 Aaron must burn fragrant incense on it; he must burn it every morning when he tends the lamps.
Exodus30:8 When Aaron sets up the lamps at twilight
Exodus30:9 You must not offer unauthorized incense on it
Exodus30:10 Once a year Aaron is to perform the purification rite on the horns of the altar. Throughout your generations he is to perform the purification rite for it once a year
Exodus30:11 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus30:12 When you take a census of the Israelites to register them
Exodus30:13 Everyone who is registered must pay half a shekel according to the sanctuary shekel
Exodus30:14 Each man who is registered
Exodus30:15 The wealthy may not give more
Exodus30:16 Take the atonement money from the Israelites and use it for the service of the tent of meeting. It will serve as a reminder for the Israelites before the LORD to atone for your lives.
Exodus30:17 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus30:18 Make a bronze basin for washing and a bronze stand for it. Set it between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus30:19 Aaron and his sons must wash their hands and feet from the basin.
Exodus30:20 Whenever they enter the tent of meeting or approach the altar to minister by burning up an offering to the LORD
Exodus30:21 They must wash their hands and feet so that they will not die; this is to be a permanent statute for them
Exodus30:22 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus30:23 Take for yourself the finest spices: 12 and a half pounds of liquid myrrh
Exodus30:24 12 and a half pounds of cassia (by the sanctuary shekel)
Exodus30:25 Prepare from these a holy anointing oil
Exodus30:26 With it you are to anoint the tent of meeting
Exodus30:27 the table with all its utensils
Exodus30:28 the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils
Exodus30:29 Consecrate them and they will be especially holy. Whatever touches them will be consecrated.
Exodus30:30 Anoint Aaron and his sons and consecrate them to serve Me as priests.
Exodus30:31 Tell the Israelites: This will be My holy anointing oil throughout your generations.
Exodus30:32 It must not be used for [ordinary] anointing on a person's body
Exodus30:33 Anyone who blends something like it or puts some of it on an unauthorized person must be cut off from his people.
Exodus30:34 The LORD said to Moses: Take fragrant spices: stacte
Exodus30:35 Prepare expertly blended incense from these; it is to be seasoned with salt
Exodus30:36 Grind some of it into a fine powder and put some in front of the testimony in the tent of meeting
Exodus30:37 As for the incense you are making
Exodus30:38 Anyone who makes something like it to smell its fragrance must be cut off from his people.

Exodus32:10 Now leave Me alone

Exodus32:12 Why should the Egyptians say

Exodus32:17 When Joshua heard the sound of the people as they shouted

Exodus33:2 I will send an angel ahead of you and will drive out the Canaanites
Exodus33:3 [Go up] to a land flowing with milk and honey. But I will not go with you because you are a stiff-necked people; otherwise
Exodus33:4 When the people heard this bad news
Exodus33:5 For the LORD said to Moses: Tell the Israelites: You are a stiff-necked people. If I went with you for a single moment

Exodus34:1 The LORD said to Moses
Exodus34:2 Be prepared by morning. Come up Mount Sinai in the morning and stand before Me on the mountaintop.
Exodus34:3 No one may go up with you; in fact
Exodus34:4 Moses cut two stone tablets like the first ones. He got up early in the morning
Exodus34:5 The LORD came down in a cloud
Exodus34:6 Then the LORD passed in front of him and proclaimed: Yahweh-- Yahweh is a compassionate and gracious God
Exodus34:7 maintaining faithful love to a thousand [generations]
Exodus34:8 Moses immediately bowed down to the ground and worshiped.
Exodus34:9 Then he said
Exodus34:10 And the LORD responded: Look
Exodus34:11 Observe what I command you today. I am going to drive out before you the Amorites
Exodus34:12 Be careful not to make a treaty with the inhabitants of the land that you are going to enter; otherwise
Exodus34:13 Instead
Exodus34:14 You are to never bow down to another god because the LORD
Exodus34:15 Do not make a treaty with the inhabitants of the land
Exodus34:16 Then you will take some of their daughters [as brides] for your sons. Their daughters will prostitute themselves with their gods and cause your sons to prostitute themselves with their gods.
Exodus34:17 Do not make cast images of gods for yourselves.
Exodus34:18 Observe the Festival of Unleavened Bread. You are to eat unleavened bread for seven days at the appointed time in the month of Abib as I commanded you. For you came out of Egypt in the month of Abib.
Exodus34:19 The firstborn male from every womb belongs to Me
Exodus34:20 You must redeem the firstborn of a donkey with a sheep
Exodus34:21 You are to labor six days but you must rest on the seventh day; you must even rest during plowing and harvesting times.
Exodus34:22 Observe the Festival of Weeks with the firstfruits of the wheat harvest
Exodus34:23 Three times a year all your males are to appear before the Lord God
Exodus34:24 For I will drive out nations before you and enlarge your territory. No one will covet your land when you go up three times a year to appear before the LORD your God.
Exodus34:25 Do not present the blood for My sacrifice with anything leavened. The sacrifice of the Passover Festival must not remain until morning.
Exodus34:26 Bring the best firstfruits of your land to the house of the LORD your God. You must not boil a young goat in its mother's milk.
Exodus34:27 The LORD also said to Moses
Exodus34:28 Moses was there with the LORD 40 days and 40 nights; he did not eat bread or drink water. He wrote down on the tablets the words of the covenant-- the Ten Commandments.
Exodus34:29 As Moses descended from Mount Sinai-- with the two tablets of the testimony in his hands as he descended the mountain-- he did not realize that the skin of his face shone as a result of his speaking with the LORD.
Exodus34:30 When Aaron and all the Israelites saw Moses
Exodus34:31 But Moses called out to them
Exodus34:32 Afterwards all the Israelites came near
Exodus34:33 When Moses had finished speaking with them
Exodus34:34 But whenever Moses went before the LORD to speak with Him
Exodus34:35 and the Israelites would see that Moses' face was radiant. Then Moses would put the veil over his face again until he went to speak with the LORD.
Exodus35:1 Moses assembled the entire Israelite community and said to them
Exodus35:2 For six days work is to be done
Exodus35:3 Do not light a fire in any of your homes on the Sabbath day.
Exodus35:4 Then Moses said to the entire Israelite community
Exodus35:5 Take up an offering for the LORD among you. Let everyone whose heart is willing bring this as the LORD's offering: gold
Exodus35:6 blue
Exodus35:7 ram skins dyed red and manatee skins; acacia wood;
Exodus35:8 oil for the light; spices for the anointing oil and for the fragrant incense;
Exodus35:9 and onyx with gemstones to mount on the ephod and breastpiece.
Exodus35:10 Let all the skilled craftsmen among you come and make everything that the LORD has commanded:
Exodus35:11 the tabernacle-- its tent and covering
Exodus35:12 the ark with its poles
Exodus35:13 the table with its poles
Exodus35:14 the lampstand for light with its utensils and lamps as well as the oil for the light;
Exodus35:15 the altar of incense with its poles; the anointing oil and the fragrant incense; the entryway screen for the entrance to the tabernacle;
Exodus35:16 the altar of burnt offering with its bronze grate
Exodus35:17 the hangings of the courtyard
Exodus35:18 the tent pegs for the tabernacle and the tent pegs for the courtyard
Exodus35:19 and the specially woven garments for ministering in the sanctuary-- the holy garments for Aaron the priest and the garments for his sons to serve as priests.
Exodus35:20 Then the entire Israelite community left Moses' presence.
Exodus35:21 Everyone whose heart was moved and whose spirit prompted him came and brought an offering to the LORD to construct the tent of meeting for every use
Exodus35:22 Both men and women came; all who had willing hearts brought brooches
Exodus35:23 Everyone who had in his possession blue
Exodus35:24 Everyone who offered a contribution of silver or bronze brought it to the LORD. Everyone who possessed acacia wood useful for any task in the work brought [it].
Exodus35:25 Every skilled woman spun [yarn] with her hands and brought it: blue
Exodus35:26 And all the women whose hearts were moved spun the goat hair by virtue of [their] skill.
Exodus35:27 The leaders brought onyx and gemstones to mount on the ephod and breastpiece
Exodus35:28 as well as the spice and oil for the light
Exodus35:29 So the Israelites brought a freewill offering to the LORD
Exodus35:30 Moses then said to the Israelites: Look
Exodus35:31 He has filled him with God's Spirit
Exodus35:32 to design artistic works in gold
Exodus35:33 to cut gemstones for mounting
Exodus35:34 He has also given both him and Oholiab son of Ahisamach
Exodus35:35 He has filled them with skill to do all the work of a gem cutter; a designer; an embroiderer in blue
Exodus36:1 Bezalel
Exodus36:2 So Moses summoned Bezalel
Exodus36:3 They took from Moses' presence all the contributions that the Israelites had brought for the task of making the sanctuary. Meanwhile
Exodus36:4 Then all the craftsmen who were doing all the work for the sanctuary came one by one from the work they were doing
Exodus36:5 and said to Moses
Exodus36:6 After Moses gave an order
Exodus36:7 The materials were sufficient for them to do all the work. There was more than enough.
Exodus36:8 All the skilled craftsmen among those doing the work made the tabernacle with 10 curtains. Bezalel made them of finely spun linen
Exodus36:9 The length of each curtain was 42 feet
Exodus36:10 He joined five of the curtains to each other
Exodus36:11 He made loops of blue yarn on the edge of the last curtain in the first set and did the same on the edge of the outermost curtain in the second set.
Exodus36:12 He made 50 loops on the one curtain and 50 loops on the edge of the curtain in the second set
Exodus36:13 He also made 50 gold clasps and joined the curtains to each other
Exodus36:14 He made curtains of goat hair for a tent over the tabernacle; he also made 11 of them.
Exodus36:15 The length of each curtain was 45 feet
Exodus36:16 He joined five of the curtains together
Exodus36:17 He made 50 loops on the edge of the outermost curtain in the [first] set and 50 loops on the edge of the [corresponding] curtain in the second set.
Exodus36:18 He made 50 bronze clasps to join the tent together as a single unit.
Exodus36:19 He also made a covering for the tent from ram skins dyed red and a covering of manatee skins on top of it.
Exodus36:20 He made upright planks of acacia wood for the tabernacle.
Exodus36:21 The length of each plank was 15 feet
Exodus36:22 There were two tenons connected to each other for each plank. He did the same for all the planks of the tabernacle.
Exodus36:23 He made planks for the tabernacle as follows: 20 for the south side
Exodus36:24 and he made 40 silver bases to put under the 20 planks
Exodus36:25 for the second side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:26 with their 40 silver bases
Exodus36:27 and for the west side of the tabernacle he made six planks.
Exodus36:28 He also made two additional planks for the two back corners of the tabernacle.
Exodus36:29 They were paired at the bottom and joined together at the top in a single ring. This is what he did with both of them for the two corners.
Exodus36:30 So there were eight planks with their 16 silver bases
Exodus36:31 He made five crossbars of acacia wood for the planks on one side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:32 five crossbars for the planks on the other side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:33 He made the central crossbar run through the middle of the planks from one end to the other.
Exodus36:34 He overlaid them with gold and made their rings and holders for the crossbars out of gold. He also overlaid the crossbars with gold.
Exodus36:35 Then he made the veil with blue
Exodus36:36 For it he made four posts of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold; their hooks were of gold. And he cast four silver bases for the posts.
Exodus36:37 He made a screen embroidered with blue
Exodus36:38 together with its five posts and their hooks. He overlaid the tops of the posts and their bands with gold
Exodus37:1 Bezalel made the ark of acacia wood
Exodus37:2 He overlaid it with pure gold inside and out and made a gold molding all around it.
Exodus37:3 He cast four gold rings for it to be on its four feet
Exodus37:4 He made poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:5 He inserted the poles into the rings on the sides of the ark for carrying the ark.
Exodus37:6 He made a mercy seat of pure gold
Exodus37:7 He made two cherubim of gold; he made them of hammered work at the two ends of the mercy seat
Exodus37:8 one cherub at one end and one cherub at the other end. He made the cherubim [of one piece] with the mercy seat
Exodus37:9 They had wings spread out
Exodus37:10 He constructed the table of acacia wood
Exodus37:11 He overlaid it with pure gold and made a gold molding all around it.
Exodus37:12 He made a three-inch frame all around it and made a gold molding all around its frame.
Exodus37:13 He cast four gold rings for it and attached the rings to the four corners at its four legs.
Exodus37:14 The rings were next to the frame as holders for the poles to carry the table.
Exodus37:15 He made the poles for carrying the table from acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:16 He also made the utensils that would be on the table out of pure gold: its plates and cups
Exodus37:17 Then he made the lampstand out of pure hammered gold. He made it [all] of one piece: its base and shaft
Exodus37:18 Six branches extended from its sides
Exodus37:19 There were three cups shaped like almond blossoms
Exodus37:20 On the lampstand shaft there were four cups shaped like almond blossoms with its calyxes and petals.
Exodus37:21 For the six branches that extended from it
Exodus37:22 Their calyxes and branches were of one piece. All of it was a single hammered piece of pure gold.
Exodus37:23 He also made its seven lamps
Exodus37:24 He made it and all its utensils of 75 pounds of pure gold.
Exodus37:25 He made the altar of incense out of acacia wood. It was square
Exodus37:26 He overlaid it
Exodus37:27 He made two gold rings for it under the molding on two of its sides; [he put these] on opposite sides of it to be holders for the poles to carry it with.
Exodus37:28 He made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with gold.
Exodus37:29 He also made the holy anointing oil and the pure
Exodus38:1 Bezalel constructed the altar of burnt offering from acacia wood. It was square
Exodus38:2 He made horns for it on its four corners; the horns were of one piece. Then he overlaid it with bronze.
Exodus38:3 He made all the altar's utensils: the pots
Exodus38:4 He constructed for the altar a grate of bronze mesh under its ledge
Exodus38:5 At the four corners of the bronze grate he cast four rings as holders for the poles.
Exodus38:6 He also made the poles of acacia wood and overlaid them with bronze.
Exodus38:7 Then he inserted the poles into the rings on the sides of the altar in order to carry it with them. He constructed the altar with boards so that it was hollow.
Exodus38:8 He made the bronze basin and its stand from the [bronze] mirrors of the women who served at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Exodus38:9 Then he made the courtyard. The hangings on the south side of the courtyard were of finely spun linen
Exodus38:10 including their 20 posts and 20 bronze bases. The hooks and bands of the posts were silver.
Exodus38:11 [The hangings] on the north side were also 150 feet in length
Exodus38:12 The hangings on the west side were 75 feet in length
Exodus38:13 [The hangings] on the east toward the sunrise were also 75 feet in length.
Exodus38:14 The hangings on one side [of the gate] were 22 and a half feet
Exodus38:15 It was the same for the other side. The hangings were 22 and a half feet
Exodus38:16 All the hangings around the courtyard were of finely spun linen.
Exodus38:17 The bases for the posts were bronze; the hooks and bands of the posts were silver; and the plating for the tops of the posts was silver. All the posts of the courtyard were banded with silver.
Exodus38:18 The screen for the gate of the courtyard was embroidered with blue
Exodus38:19 It had four posts
Exodus38:20 All the tent pegs for the tabernacle and for the surrounding courtyard were bronze.
Exodus38:21 This is the inventory for the tabernacle
Exodus38:22 Bezalel son of Uri
Exodus38:23 With him was Oholiab son of Ahisamach
Exodus38:24 All the gold of the presentation offering that was used for the project in all the work on the sanctuary
Exodus38:25 The silver from those of the community who were registered was 7
Exodus38:26 two-fifths of an ounce per man
Exodus38:27 There were 7
Exodus38:28 With the [remaining] 44 pounds he made the hooks for the posts
Exodus38:29 The bronze of the presentation offering totaled 5
Exodus38:30 He made with it the bases for the entrance to the tent of meeting
Exodus38:31 the bases for the surrounding courtyard
Exodus39:1 They made specially woven garments for ministry in the sanctuary
Exodus39:2 Bezalel made the ephod of gold
Exodus39:3 They hammered out thin sheets of gold
Exodus39:4 They made shoulder pieces for attaching it; it was joined together at its two edges.
Exodus39:5 The artistically woven waistband that was on the ephod was of one piece with the ephod
Exodus39:6 Then they mounted the onyx stones surrounded with gold filigree settings
Exodus39:7 He fastened them on the shoulder pieces of the ephod as memorial stones for the Israelites
Exodus39:8 He also made the embroidered breastpiece with the same workmanship as the ephod of gold
Exodus39:9 They made the breastpiece square and folded double
Exodus39:10 They mounted four rows of gemstones on it. The first row was a row of carnelian
Exodus39:11 the second row
Exodus39:12 the third row
Exodus39:13 and the fourth row
Exodus39:14 The 12 stones corresponded to the names of Israel's sons. Each stone was engraved like a seal with one of the names of the 12 tribes.
Exodus39:15 They made braided chains of pure gold cord for the breastpiece.
Exodus39:16 They also fashioned two gold filigree settings and two gold rings and attached the two rings to its two corners.
Exodus39:17 Then they attached the two gold cords to the two gold rings on the corners of the breastpiece.
Exodus39:18 They attached the other ends of the two cords to the two filigree settings and
Exodus39:19 They made two [other] gold rings and put [them] at the two other corners of the breastpiece on the edge that is next to the inner border of the ephod.
Exodus39:20 They made two [more] gold rings and attached them to the bottom of the ephod's two shoulder pieces on its front
Exodus39:21 Then they tied the breastpiece from its rings to the rings of the ephod with a cord of blue yarn
Exodus39:22 They made the woven robe of the ephod entirely of blue yarn.
Exodus39:23 There was an opening in the center of the robe like that for body armor with a collar around the opening so that it would not tear.
Exodus39:24 They made pomegranates of finely spun blue
Exodus39:25 They made bells of pure gold and attached the bells between the pomegranates
Exodus39:26 a bell and a pomegranate alternating all around the lower hem of the robe. It is to be used for ministry
Exodus39:27 They made the tunics of fine woven linen for Aaron and his sons.
Exodus39:28 [They also made] the turban and the ornate headbands of fine linen
Exodus39:29 and the sash of finely spun linen of embroidered blue
Exodus39:30 They also made a plate
Exodus39:31 Then they attached a cord of blue yarn to it in order to mount [it] on the turban
Exodus39:32 So all the work for the tabernacle
Exodus39:33 Then they brought the tabernacle to Moses: the tent with all its furnishings
Exodus39:34 the covering of ram skins dyed red and the covering of manatee skins; the veil for the screen;
Exodus39:35 the ark of the testimony with its poles and the mercy seat;
Exodus39:36 the table
Exodus39:37 the pure [gold] lampstand
Exodus39:38 the gold altar; the anointing oil; the fragrant incense; the screen for the entrance to the tent;
Exodus39:39 the bronze altar with its bronze grate
Exodus39:40 the hangings of the courtyard
Exodus39:41 and the specially woven garments for ministering in the sanctuary
Exodus39:42 The Israelites had done all the work according to everything the LORD had commanded Moses.
Exodus39:43 Moses inspected all the work they had accomplished. They had done just as the LORD commanded. Then Moses blessed them.
Exodus40:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Exodus40:2 You are to set up the tabernacle
Exodus40:3 Put the ark of the testimony there
Exodus40:4 Then bring in the table and lay out its arrangement; also bring in the lampstand and set up its lamps.
Exodus40:5 Place the gold altar for incense in front of the ark of the testimony. Put up the screen for the entrance to the tabernacle.
Exodus40:6 Position the altar of burnt offering in front of the entrance to the tabernacle
Exodus40:7 Place the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar
Exodus40:8 Assemble the surrounding courtyard and hang the screen for the gate of the courtyard.
Exodus40:9 Take the anointing oil
Exodus40:10 Anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils; consecrate the altar so that it will be especially holy.
Exodus40:11 Anoint the basin and its stand
Exodus40:12 Then bring Aaron and his sons to the entrance to the tent of meeting and wash them with water.
Exodus40:13 Clothe Aaron with the holy garments
Exodus40:14 Have his sons come forward and clothe them in tunics.
Exodus40:15 Anoint them just as you anointed their father
Exodus40:16 Moses did everything just as the LORD had commanded him.
Exodus40:17 The tabernacle was set up in the first month of the second year
Exodus40:18 Moses set up the tabernacle: he laid its bases
Exodus40:19 Then he spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent on top of it
Exodus40:20 Moses took the testimony and placed [it] in the ark
Exodus40:21 He brought the ark into the tabernacle
Exodus40:22 Moses placed the table in the tent of meeting on the north side of the tabernacle
Exodus40:23 He arranged the bread on it before the LORD
Exodus40:24 He also put the lampstand in the tent of meeting opposite the table on the south side of the tabernacle
Exodus40:25 and set up the lamps before the LORD
Exodus40:26 Moses also installed the gold altar in the tent of meeting
Exodus40:27 and burned fragrant incense on it
Exodus40:28 He put up the screen at the entrance to the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 Then he placed the altar of burnt offering at the entrance to the tabernacle
Exodus40:30 He set the basin between the tent of meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
Exodus40:31 Moses
Exodus40:32 They washed whenever they came to the tent of meeting and approached the altar
Exodus40:33 Next Moses set up the surrounding courtyard for the tabernacle and the altar and hung a screen for the gate of the courtyard. So Moses finished the work.
Exodus40:34 The cloud covered the tent of meeting
Exodus40:35 Moses was unable to enter the tent of meeting because the cloud rested on it
Exodus40:36 The Israelites set out whenever the cloud was taken up from the tabernacle throughout all the stages of their journey.
Exodus40:37 If the cloud was not taken up
Exodus40:38 For the cloud of the LORD was over the tabernacle by day
Leviticus1:1 Then the LORD summoned Moses and spoke to him from the tent of meeting:
Leviticus1:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When any of you brings an offering to the LORD from the livestock
Leviticus1:3 If his gift is a burnt offering from the herd
Leviticus1:4 He is to lay his hand on the head of the burnt offering so it can be accepted on his behalf to make atonement for him.
Leviticus1:5 He is to slaughter the bull before the LORD; Aaron's sons the priests are to present the blood and sprinkle it on all sides of the altar that is at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus1:6 Then he must skin the burnt offering and cut it into pieces.
Leviticus1:7 The sons of Aaron the priest will prepare a fire on the altar and arrange wood on the fire.
Leviticus1:8 Aaron's sons the priests are to arrange the pieces
Leviticus1:9 The offerer must wash its entrails and shanks with water. Then the priest will burn all of it on the altar as a burnt offering
Leviticus1:10 But if his gift for a burnt offering is from the flock
Leviticus1:11 He will slaughter it on the north side of the altar before the LORD. Aaron's sons the priests will sprinkle its blood against the altar on all sides.
Leviticus1:12 He will cut it into pieces with its head and its suet
Leviticus1:13 But he is to wash the entrails and shanks with water. The priest will then present all of it and burn [it] on the altar; it is a burnt offering
Leviticus1:14 If his gift to the LORD is a burnt offering of birds
Leviticus1:15 Then the priest must bring it to the altar
Leviticus1:16 He will remove its digestive tract
Leviticus1:17 He will tear it open by its wings without dividing [the bird]. Then the priest is to burn it on the altar on top of the burning wood. It is a burnt offering
Leviticus2:1 When anyone presents a grain offering as a gift to the LORD
Leviticus2:2 and bring it to Aaron's sons the priests. The priest will take a handful of fine flour and oil from it
Leviticus2:3 But the rest of the grain offering will belong to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus2:4 When you present a grain offering baked in an oven
Leviticus2:5 If your gift is a grain offering prepared on the griddle
Leviticus2:6 Break it into pieces and pour oil on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:7 If your gift is a grain offering [prepared] in a pan
Leviticus2:8 When you bring to the LORD the grain offering made in any of these ways
Leviticus2:9 The priest will remove the memorial portion from the grain offering and burn it on the altar
Leviticus2:10 But the rest of the grain offering will belong to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus2:11 No grain offering that you present to the LORD is to be made with yeast
Leviticus2:12 You may present them to the LORD as an offering of firstfruits
Leviticus2:13 You are to season each of your grain offerings with salt; you must not omit from your grain offering the salt of the covenant with your God. You are to present salt with each of your offerings.
Leviticus2:14 If you present a grain offering of firstfruits to the LORD
Leviticus2:15 You are to put oil and frankincense on it; it is a grain offering.
Leviticus2:16 The priest will then burn some of its crushed kernels and oil with all its frankincense as a fire offering to the LORD.
Leviticus3:1 If his offering is a fellowship sacrifice
Leviticus3:2 He is to lay his hand on the head of his offering and slaughter it at the entrance to the tent of meeting. Then Aaron's sons the priests will sprinkle the blood on all sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:3 He will present part of the fellowship sacrifice as a fire offering to the LORD: the fat surrounding the entrails
Leviticus3:4 and the two kidneys with the fat on them at the loins; he will also remove the fatty lobe of the liver with the kidneys.
Leviticus3:5 Aaron's sons will burn it on the altar along with the burnt offering that is on the burning wood
Leviticus3:6 If his offering as a fellowship sacrifice to the LORD is from the flock
Leviticus3:7 If he is presenting a lamb for his offering
Leviticus3:8 He must lay his hand on the head of his offering
Leviticus3:9 He will then present part of the fellowship sacrifice as a fire offering to the LORD [consisting of] its fat and the entire fat tail
Leviticus3:10 the two kidneys with the fat on them at the loins
Leviticus3:11 Then the priest will burn it on the altar as food
Leviticus3:12 If his offering is a goat
Leviticus3:13 He must lay his hand on its head and slaughter it before the tent of meeting. Aaron's sons will sprinkle its blood on all sides of the altar.
Leviticus3:14 He will present part of his offering as a fire offering to the LORD: the fat surrounding the entrails
Leviticus3:15 and the two kidneys with the fat on them at the loins; he will also remove the fatty lobe of the liver with the kidneys.
Leviticus3:16 Then the priest will burn them on the altar as food
Leviticus3:17 This is a permanent statute throughout your generations
Leviticus4:1 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus4:2 Tell the Israelites: When someone sins unintentionally against any of the LORD's commands and does anything prohibited by them--
Leviticus4:3 If the anointed priest sins
Leviticus4:4 He must bring the bull to the entrance to the tent of meeting before the LORD
Leviticus4:5 The anointed priest must then take some of the bull's blood and bring it into the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:6 The priest is to dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of it seven times before the LORD in front of the veil of the sanctuary.
Leviticus4:7 The priest must apply some of the blood to the horns of the altar of fragrant incense that is before the LORD in the tent of meeting. He must pour out the rest of the bull's blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering that is at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:8 He is to remove all the fat from the bull of the sin offering: the fat surrounding the entrails; all the fat that is on the entrails;
Leviticus4:9 and the two kidneys with the fat on them at the loins. He will also remove the fatty lobe of the liver with the kidneys
Leviticus4:10 just as the fat is removed from the ox of the fellowship sacrifice. The priest is to burn them on the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:11 But the hide of the bull and all its flesh
Leviticus4:12 all [the rest] of the bull-- he must bring to a ceremonially clean place outside the camp to the ash heap
Leviticus4:13 Now if the whole community of Israel errs
Leviticus4:14 then the assembly must present a young bull as a sin offering. When the sin they have committed in regard to the command becomes known
Leviticus4:15 The elders of the assembly are to lay their hands on the bull's head before the LORD and it is to be slaughtered before the LORD.
Leviticus4:16 The anointed priest will bring some of the bull's blood into the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:17 The priest is to dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle [it] seven times before the LORD in front of the veil.
Leviticus4:18 He is to apply some of the blood to the horns of the altar that is before the LORD in the tent of meeting. He must pour out the rest of the blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering that is at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus4:19 He is to remove all the fat from it and burn it on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 He is to offer this bull just as he did with the bull in the sin offering; he will offer it the same way. So the priest will make atonement on their behalf
Leviticus4:21 Then he will bring the bull outside the camp and burn it just as he burned the first bull. It is the sin offering for the assembly.
Leviticus4:22 When a leader sins and unintentionally violates any of the commands of the LORD his God by doing what is prohibited
Leviticus4:23 or someone informs him about the sin he has committed
Leviticus4:24 He is to lay his hand on the head of the goat and slaughter it at the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered before the LORD. It is a sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 Then the priest must take some of the blood from the sin offering with his finger and apply it to the horns of the altar of burnt offering. The rest of its blood he must pour out at the base of the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:26 He must burn all its fat on the altar
Leviticus4:27 Now if any of the common people sins unintentionally by violating one of the LORD's commands
Leviticus4:28 or if someone informs him about the sin he has committed
Leviticus4:29 He is to lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it at the place of the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:30 Then the priest must take some of its blood with his finger and apply it to the horns of the altar of burnt offering. He must pour out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:31 He is to remove all its fat just as the fat is removed from the fellowship sacrifice. The priest is to burn [it] on the altar as a pleasing aroma to the LORD. In this way the priest will make atonement on his behalf
Leviticus4:32 Or if the offering that he brings as a sin offering is a lamb
Leviticus4:33 He is to lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and slaughter it as a sin offering at the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered.
Leviticus4:34 Then the priest must take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and apply it to the horns of the altar of burnt offering. He must pour out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:35 He is to remove all its fat just as the fat of the lamb is removed from the fellowship sacrifice. The priest will burn it on the altar along with the fire offerings to the LORD. In this way the priest will make atonement on his behalf for the sin he has committed
Leviticus5:1 When someone sins [in any of these ways]: [If] he has seen
Leviticus5:2 Or [if] someone touches anything unclean-- a carcass of an unclean wild animal
Leviticus5:3 Or [if] he touches human uncleanness-- any uncleanness by which one can become defiled-- without being aware of it
Leviticus5:4 Or [if] someone swears rashly to do what is good or evil-- concerning anything a person may speak rashly in an oath-- without being aware of it
Leviticus5:5 If someone incurs guilt in one of these cases
Leviticus5:6 He must bring his restitution for the sin he has committed to the LORD: a female lamb or goat from the flock as a sin offering. In this way the priest will make atonement on his behalf for his sin.
Leviticus5:7 But if he cannot afford an animal from the flock
Leviticus5:8 He is to bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 Then he will sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 He must prepare the second [bird] as a burnt offering according to the regulation. In this way the priest will make atonement on his behalf for the sin he has committed
Leviticus5:11 But if he cannot afford two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus5:12 He is to bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 In this way the priest will make atonement on his behalf concerning the sin he has committed in any of these cases
Leviticus5:14 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus5:15 If someone offends by sinning unintentionally in regard to any of the LORD's holy things
Leviticus5:16 He must make restitution for his sin regarding any holy thing
Leviticus5:17 If someone sins and without knowing [it] violates any of the LORD's commands concerning anything prohibited
Leviticus5:18 He must bring an unblemished ram from the flock according to your valuation as a restitution offering to the priest. Then the priest will make atonement on his behalf for the error he has committed unintentionally
Leviticus5:19 It is a restitution offering; he is indeed guilty before the LORD.
Leviticus6:1  The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus6:2 When someone sins and offends the LORD by deceiving his neighbor in regard to a deposit
Leviticus6:3 or finds something lost and lies about it; or swears falsely about any of the sinful things a person may do--
Leviticus6:4 once he has sinned and acknowledged [his] guilt-- he must return what he stole or defrauded
Leviticus6:5 or anything else about which he swore falsely. He must make full restitution for it and add a fifth of its value to it. He is to pay it to its owner on the day he acknowledges [his] guilt.
Leviticus6:6 Then he must bring his restitution offering to the LORD: an unblemished ram from the flock
Leviticus6:7 In this way the priest will make atonement on his behalf before the LORD
Leviticus6:8  The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus6:9 Command Aaron and his sons: This is the law of the burnt offering; the burnt offering itself must remain on the altar's hearth all night until morning
Leviticus6:10 The priest is to put on his linen robe and linen undergarments. He is to remove the ashes of the burnt offering the fire has consumed on the altar
Leviticus6:11 Then he must take off his garments
Leviticus6:12 The fire on the altar is to be kept burning; it must not go out. Every morning the priest will burn wood on the fire. He is to arrange the burnt offering on the fire and burn the fat portions from the fellowship offerings on it.
Leviticus6:13 Fire must be kept burning on the altar continually; it must not go out.
Leviticus6:14 Now this is the law of the grain offering: Aaron's sons will present it before the LORD in front of the altar.
Leviticus6:15 The priest is to remove a handful of fine flour and olive oil from the grain offering
Leviticus6:16 Aaron and his sons may eat the rest of it. It is to be eaten as unleavened bread in a holy place; they are to eat it in the courtyard of the tent of meeting.
Leviticus6:17 It must not be baked with yeast; I have assigned it as their portion from My fire offerings. It is especially holy
Leviticus6:18 Any male among Aaron's descendants may eat it. It is a permanent portion throughout your generations from the fire offerings to the Lord. Anything that touches the offerings will become holy.
Leviticus6:19 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus6:20 This is the offering that Aaron and his sons must present to the LORD on the day that he is anointed: two quarts of fine flour as a regular grain offering
Leviticus6:21 It is to be prepared with oil on a griddle; you are to bring it well-kneaded. You must present it as a grain offering of baked pieces
Leviticus6:22 The priest
Leviticus6:23 Every grain offering for a priest will be a whole burnt offering; it is not to be eaten.
Leviticus6:24 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus6:25 Tell Aaron and his sons: This is the law of the sin offering. The sin offering is most holy and must be slaughtered before the LORD at the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered.
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers it as a sin offering is to eat it. It must be eaten in a holy place
Leviticus6:27 Anything that touches its flesh will become holy
Leviticus6:28 A clay pot in which the sin offering is boiled must be broken; if it is boiled in a bronze vessel
Leviticus6:29 Any male among the priests may eat it; it is especially holy.
Leviticus6:30 But no sin offering may be eaten if its blood has been brought into the tent of meeting to make atonement in the holy place; it must be burned up.
Leviticus7:1 Now this is the law of the restitution offering; it is especially holy.
Leviticus7:2 The restitution offering must be slaughtered at the place where the burnt offering is slaughtered
Leviticus7:3 The offerer must present all the fat from it: the fat tail
Leviticus7:4 and the two kidneys with the fat on them at the loins; he will also remove the fatty lobe of the liver with the kidneys.
Leviticus7:5 The priest will burn them on the altar as a fire offering to the LORD; it is a restitution offering.
Leviticus7:6 Any male among the priests may eat it. It is to be eaten in a holy place; it is especially holy.
Leviticus7:7 The restitution offering is like the sin offering; the law is the same for both. It belongs to the priest who makes atonement with it.
Leviticus7:8 As for the priest who presents someone's burnt offering
Leviticus7:9 Any grain offering that is baked in an oven
Leviticus7:10 But any grain offering
Leviticus7:11 Now this is the law of the fellowship sacrifice that someone may present to the LORD:
Leviticus7:12 If he presents it for thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 He is to present as his offering cakes of leavened bread
Leviticus7:14 From the cakes he must present one [portion] of each offering as a contribution to the LORD. It will belong to the priest who sprinkles the blood of the fellowship offering; it is his.
Leviticus7:15 The meat of his thanksgiving sacrifice of fellowship must be eaten on the day he offers it; he may not leave any of it until morning.
Leviticus7:16 If the sacrifice he offers is a vow or a freewill offering
Leviticus7:17 But what remains of the sacrificial meat by the third day must be burned up.
Leviticus7:18 If any of the meat of his fellowship sacrifice is eaten on the third day
Leviticus7:19 Meat that touches anything unclean must not be eaten; it is to be burned up. Everyone who is clean may eat any [other] meat.
Leviticus7:20 But the one who eats meat from the LORD's fellowship sacrifice while he is unclean
Leviticus7:21 If someone touches anything unclean
Leviticus7:22 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus7:23 Tell the Israelites: You are not to eat any fat of an ox
Leviticus7:24 The fat of an animal that dies naturally or is mauled by wild beasts may be used for any purpose
Leviticus7:25 If anyone eats animal fat from a fire offering presented to the LORD
Leviticus7:26 Wherever you live
Leviticus7:27 Whoever eats any blood
Leviticus7:28 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus7:29 Tell the Israelites: The one who presents a fellowship sacrifice to the LORD must bring an offering to the LORD from his sacrifice.
Leviticus7:30 His own hands will bring the fire offerings to the LORD. He will bring the fat together with the breast. The breast is to be waved as a presentation offering before the LORD.
Leviticus7:31 The priest is to burn the fat on the altar
Leviticus7:32 You are to give the right thigh to the priest as a contribution from your fellowship sacrifices.
Leviticus7:33 The son of Aaron who presents the blood of the fellowship offering and the fat will have the right thigh as a portion.
Leviticus7:34 I have taken from the Israelites the breast of the presentation offering and the thigh of the contribution from their fellowship sacrifices
Leviticus7:35 This is the portion from the fire offerings to the LORD for Aaron and his sons since the day they were presented to serve the LORD as priests.
Leviticus7:36 The LORD commanded this to be given to them by the Israelites on the day He anointed them. It is a permanent portion throughout their generations.
Leviticus7:37 This is the law for the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 which the LORD commanded Moses on Mount Sinai on the day He commanded the Israelites to present their offerings to the LORD in the Wilderness of Sinai.
Leviticus8:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus8:2 Take Aaron
Leviticus8:3 and assemble the whole community at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus8:4 So Moses did as the LORD commanded him
Leviticus8:5 Moses said to them
Leviticus8:6 Then Moses presented Aaron and his sons and washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 He put the tunic on Aaron
Leviticus8:8 Then he put the breastpiece on him and placed the Urim and Thummim into the breastpiece.
Leviticus8:9 He also put the turban on his head and placed the plate of gold
Leviticus8:10 Then Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and everything in it to consecrate them.
Leviticus8:11 He sprinkled some of the oil on the altar seven times
Leviticus8:12 He poured some of the anointing oil on Aaron's head and anointed and consecrated him.
Leviticus8:13 Then Moses presented Aaron's sons
Leviticus8:14 Then he brought the bull near for the sin offering
Leviticus8:15 Then Moses slaughtered [it]
Leviticus8:16 Moses took all the fat that was on the entrails
Leviticus8:17 He burned up the bull with its hide
Leviticus8:18 Then he presented the ram for the burnt offering
Leviticus8:19 Moses slaughtered it and sprinkled the blood on all sides of the altar.
Leviticus8:20 Moses cut the ram into pieces and burned the head
Leviticus8:21 but he washed the entrails and shanks with water. He then burned the entire ram on the altar. It was a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma
Leviticus8:22 Next he presented the second ram
Leviticus8:23 Moses slaughtered [it]
Leviticus8:24 Moses also presented Aaron's sons and put some of the blood on their right earlobes
Leviticus8:25 He took the fat-- the fat tail
Leviticus8:26 From the basket of unleavened bread that was before the LORD he took one cake of unleavened bread
Leviticus8:27 He put all [these] in the hands of Aaron and his sons and waved them before the LORD as a presentation offering.
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took them from their hands and burned [them] on the altar with the burnt offering. This was an ordination offering for a pleasing aroma
Leviticus8:29 He also took the breast and waved it before the LORD as a presentation offering; it was Moses' portion of the ordination ram as the LORD had commanded him.
Leviticus8:30 Then Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood that was on the altar and sprinkled [them] on Aaron and his garments
Leviticus8:31 Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 You must burn up what remains of the meat and bread.
Leviticus8:33 You must not go outside the entrance to the tent of meeting for seven days
Leviticus8:34 The LORD commanded what has been done today in order to make atonement for you.
Leviticus8:35 You must remain at the entrance to the tent of meeting day and night for seven days and keep the LORD's charge so that you will not die
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did everything the LORD had commanded through Moses.
Leviticus9:1 On the eighth day Moses summoned Aaron
Leviticus9:2 He said to Aaron
Leviticus9:3 And tell the Israelites: 'Take a male goat for a sin offering; a calf and a lamb
Leviticus9:4 an ox and a ram for a fellowship offering to sacrifice before the LORD; and a grain offering mixed with oil. For today the LORD is going to appear to you.'

Leviticus9:6 Moses said
Leviticus9:7 Then Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus9:8 So Aaron approached the altar and slaughtered the calf as a sin offering for himself.
Leviticus9:9 Aaron's sons brought the blood to him
Leviticus9:10 He burned the fat
Leviticus9:11 He burned up the flesh and the hide outside the camp.

Leviticus9:13 They brought him the burnt offering piece by piece
Leviticus9:14 He washed the entrails and the shanks and burned them with the burnt offering on the altar.
Leviticus9:15 Aaron presented the people's offering. He took the male goat for the people's sin offering
Leviticus9:16 He presented the burnt offering and sacrificed it according to the regulation.
Leviticus9:17 Next he presented the grain offering
Leviticus9:18 Finally

Leviticus10:1 Aaron's sons Nadab and Abihu each took his own firepan
Leviticus10:2 Then flames leaped from the LORD's presence and burned them to death before the LORD.
Leviticus10:3 So Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus10:4 Moses summoned Mishael and Elzaphan
Leviticus10:5 So they came forward and carried them in their tunics outside the camp
Leviticus10:6 Then Moses said to Aaron and his sons Eleazar and Ithamar
Leviticus10:7 You must not go outside the entrance to the tent of meeting or you will die
Leviticus10:8 The LORD spoke to Aaron:
Leviticus10:9 You and your sons are not to drink wine or beer when you enter the tent of meeting
Leviticus10:10 You must distinguish between the holy and the common
Leviticus10:11 and teach the Israelites all the statutes that the LORD has given to them through Moses.
Leviticus10:12 Moses spoke to Aaron and his remaining sons
Leviticus10:13 You must eat it in a holy place because it is your portion and your sons' from the fire offerings to the LORD
Leviticus10:14 But you and your sons and your daughters may eat the breast of the presentation offering and the thigh of the contribution in any ceremonially clean place
Leviticus10:15 They are to bring the thigh of the contribution and the breast of the presentation offering
Leviticus10:16 Later

Leviticus10:18 Since its blood was not brought inside the sanctuary
