Leviticus11:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Leviticus11:2 Tell the Israelites: You may eat all these [kinds] of land animals.
Leviticus11:3 You may eat any animal with divided hooves and that chews the cud.
Leviticus11:4 But among the ones that chew the cud or have hooves you are not to eat [these]: the camel
Leviticus11:5 the hyrax
Leviticus11:6 the hare
Leviticus11:7 the pig
Leviticus11:8 Do not eat any of their meat or touch their carcasses-- they are unclean for you.
Leviticus11:9 This [is what] you may eat from all that is in the water: You may eat everything in the water that has fins and scales
Leviticus11:10 But these are to be detestable to you: everything that does not have fins and scales in the seas or streams
Leviticus11:11 They are to remain detestable to you; you must not eat any of their meat
Leviticus11:12 Everything in the water that does not have fins and scales will be detestable to you.
Leviticus11:13 You are to detest these birds. They must not be eaten because they are detestable: the eagle
Leviticus11:14 the kite
Leviticus11:15 every kind of raven
Leviticus11:16 the ostrich
Leviticus11:17 the little owl
Leviticus11:18 the white owl
Leviticus11:19 the stork
Leviticus11:20 All winged insects that walk on all fours are to be detestable to you.
Leviticus11:21 But you may eat these kinds of all the winged insects that walk on all fours: those that have jointed legs above their feet for hopping on the ground.
Leviticus11:22 You may eat these: the various kinds of locust
Leviticus11:23 All [other] winged insects that have four feet are to be detestable to you.
Leviticus11:24 These will make you unclean. Whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean until evening
Leviticus11:25 and whoever carries any of their carcasses must wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:26 All animals that have hooves but do not have a divided hoof and do not chew the cud are unclean for you. Whoever touches them becomes unclean.
Leviticus11:27 All the four-footed animals that walk on their paws are unclean for you. Whoever touches their carcasses will be unclean until evening
Leviticus11:28 and anyone who carries their carcasses must wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening. They are unclean for you.
Leviticus11:29 These creatures that swarm on the ground are unclean for you: the weasel
Leviticus11:30 the gecko
Leviticus11:31 These are unclean for you among all the swarming creatures. Whoever touches them when they are dead will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:32 When any one of them dies and falls on anything it becomes unclean-- any item of wood
Leviticus11:33 If any of them falls into any clay pot
Leviticus11:34 Any edible food coming into contact with [that unclean] water will become unclean
Leviticus11:35 Anything one of their carcasses falls on will become unclean. If it is an oven or stove
Leviticus11:36 A spring or cistern containing water will remain clean
Leviticus11:37 If one of their carcasses falls on any seed that is to be sown
Leviticus11:38 but if water has been put on the seed and one of their carcasses falls on it
Leviticus11:39 If one of the animals that you use for food dies
Leviticus11:40 Anyone who eats some of its carcass must wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening. Anyone who carries its carcass must wash his clothes and will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus11:41 All the creatures that swarm on the earth are detestable; they must not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Do not eat any of the creatures that swarm on the earth
Leviticus11:43 Do not become contaminated by any creature that swarms; do not become unclean or defiled by them.
Leviticus11:44 For I am the LORD your God
Leviticus11:45 For I am the Lord
Leviticus11:46 This is the law concerning animals
Leviticus11:47 in order to distinguish between the unclean and the clean
Leviticus12:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus12:2 Tell the Israelites: When a woman becomes pregnant and gives birth to a male child
Leviticus12:3 The flesh of his foreskin must be circumcised on the eighth day.
Leviticus12:4 She will continue in purification from her bleeding for 33 days. She must not touch any holy thing or go into the sanctuary until completing her days of purification.
Leviticus12:5 But if she gives birth to a female child
Leviticus12:6 When her days of purification are complete
Leviticus12:7 He will present them before the LORD and make atonement on her behalf; she will be clean from her discharge of blood. This is the law for a woman giving birth
Leviticus12:8 But if she doesn't have sufficient means for a sheep
Leviticus13:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Leviticus13:2 When a person has a swelling
Leviticus13:3 The priest will examine the infection on the skin of his body. If the hair in the infection has turned white and the infection appears to be deeper than the skin of his body
Leviticus13:4 But if the spot on the skin of his body is white and does not appear to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:5 The priest will then reexamine him on the seventh day. If the infection remains unchanged in his sight and has not spread on the skin
Leviticus13:6 The priest will examine him again on the seventh day. If the infection has faded and has not spread on the skin
Leviticus13:7 But if the scab spreads further on his skin after he has presented himself to the priest for his cleansing
Leviticus13:8 The priest will examine him
Leviticus13:9 When a skin disease develops on a person
Leviticus13:10 The priest will examine him. If there is a white swelling on the skin that has turned the hair white
Leviticus13:11 it is a chronic disease on the skin of his body
Leviticus13:12 But if the skin disease breaks out completely over the skin so that it covers all the skin of the infected person from his head to his feet so far as the priest can see
Leviticus13:13 the priest will look
Leviticus13:14 But whenever raw flesh appears on him
Leviticus13:15 When the priest examines the raw flesh
Leviticus13:16 But if the raw flesh changes and turns white
Leviticus13:17 The priest will examine him
Leviticus13:18 When a boil appears on the skin of one's body and it heals
Leviticus13:19 and a white swelling or a reddish-white spot develops where the boil was
Leviticus13:20 The priest will make an examination
Leviticus13:21 But when the priest examines it
Leviticus13:22 If it spreads further on the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the spot remains where it is and does not spread
Leviticus13:24 When there is a burn on the skin of one's body produced by fire
Leviticus13:25 the priest is to examine it. If the hair in the spot has turned white and the spot appears to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:26 But when the priest examines it
Leviticus13:27 The priest will reexamine him on the seventh day. If it has spread further on the skin
Leviticus13:28 But if the spot has remained where it was and has not spread on the skin but is faded
Leviticus13:29 When a man or woman has an infection on the head or chin
Leviticus13:30 the priest must examine the infection. If it appears to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:31 When the priest examines the scaly infection
Leviticus13:32 The priest will reexamine the infection on the seventh day. If the scaly outbreak has not spread and there is no yellow hair in it and it does not appear to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:33 the person must shave himself but not shave the scaly area. Then the priest must quarantine the person who has the scaly outbreak for another seven days.
Leviticus13:34 The priest will examine the scaly outbreak on the seventh day
Leviticus13:35 But if the scaly outbreak spreads further on the skin after his cleansing
Leviticus13:36 the priest is to examine the person. If the scaly outbreak has spread on the skin
Leviticus13:37 But if as far as he can see
Leviticus13:38 When a man or a woman has white spots on the skin of the body
Leviticus13:39 the priest is to make an examination. If the spots on the skin of the body are dull white
Leviticus13:40 If a man loses the hair of his head
Leviticus13:41 Or if he loses the hair at his hairline
Leviticus13:42 But if there is a reddish-white infection on the bald head or forehead
Leviticus13:43 The priest is to examine him
Leviticus13:44 the man is afflicted with a skin disease; he is unclean. The priest must pronounce him unclean; the infection is on his head.
Leviticus13:45 The person afflicted with an infectious skin disease is to have his clothes torn and his hair hanging loose
Leviticus13:46 He will remain unclean as long as he has the infection; he is unclean. He must live alone in a place outside the camp.
Leviticus13:47 If a fabric is contaminated with mildew-- in wool or linen fabric
Leviticus13:48 in the warp or woof of linen or wool
Leviticus13:49 and if the contamination is green or red in the fabric
Leviticus13:50 The priest is to examine the contamination and quarantine the contaminated fabric for seven days.
Leviticus13:51 The priest is to reexamine the contamination on the seventh day. If it has spread in the fabric
Leviticus13:52 He is to burn the fabric
Leviticus13:53 When the priest examines [it]
Leviticus13:54 the priest is to order whatever is contaminated to be washed and quarantined for another seven days.
Leviticus13:55 After it has been washed
Leviticus13:56 If the priest examines [it]
Leviticus13:57 But if it reappears in the fabric
Leviticus13:58 But if the contamination disappears from the fabric
Leviticus13:59 This is the law concerning a mildew contamination in wool or linen fabric
Leviticus14:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus14:2 This is the law concerning the person afflicted with a skin disease on the day of his cleansing. He is to be brought to the priest
Leviticus14:3 who will go outside the camp and examine [him]. If the skin disease has disappeared from the afflicted person
Leviticus14:4 the priest will order that two live clean birds
Leviticus14:5 Then the priest will order that one of the birds be slaughtered over fresh water in a clay pot.
Leviticus14:6 He is to take the live bird together with the cedar wood
Leviticus14:7 He will then sprinkle [the blood] seven times on the one who is to be cleansed from the skin disease. He is to pronounce him clean and release the live bird over the open countryside.
Leviticus14:8 The one who is to be cleansed must wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 He is to shave off all his hair [again] on the seventh day: his head
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day he must take two unblemished male lambs
Leviticus14:11 The priest who performs the cleansing will place the person who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:12 The priest is to take one male lamb and present it as a restitution offering
Leviticus14:13 He is to slaughter the male lamb at the place in the sanctuary area where the sin offering and burnt offering are slaughtered
Leviticus14:14 The priest is to take some of the blood from the restitution offering and put [it] on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:15 Then the priest will take some of the one-third of a quart of olive oil and pour it into his left palm.
Leviticus14:16 The priest will dip his right finger into the oil in his left palm and sprinkle some of the oil with his finger seven times before the LORD.
Leviticus14:17 From the oil remaining in his palm the priest will put some on the lobe of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:18 What is left of the oil in the priest's palm he is to put on the head of the one to be cleansed. In this way the priest will make atonement for him before the LORD.
Leviticus14:19 The priest must sacrifice the sin offering and make atonement for the one to be purified from his uncleanness. Afterwards he will slaughter the burnt offering.
Leviticus14:20 The priest is to offer the burnt offering and the grain offering on the altar. The priest will make atonement for him
Leviticus14:21 But if he is poor and cannot afford [these]
Leviticus14:22 and two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus14:23 On the eighth day he is to bring these things for his cleansing to the priest at the entrance to the tent of meeting before the LORD.
Leviticus14:24 The priest will take the male lamb for the restitution offering and the one-third of a quart of olive oil
Leviticus14:25 After he slaughters the male lamb for the restitution offering
Leviticus14:26 Then the priest will pour some of the oil into his left palm.
Leviticus14:27 With his right finger the priest will sprinkle some of the oil in his left palm seven times before the LORD.
Leviticus14:28 The priest will also put some of the oil in his palm on the right earlobe of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:29 What is left of the oil in the priest's palm he is to put on the head of the one to be cleansed to make atonement for him before the LORD.
Leviticus14:30 He must then sacrifice one type of what he can afford
Leviticus14:31 one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering
Leviticus14:32 This is the law for someone who has a skin disease and cannot afford the cost of his cleansing.
Leviticus14:33 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Leviticus14:34 When you enter the land of Canaan that I am giving you as a possession
Leviticus14:35 the owner of the house is to come and tell the priest: Something like mildew contamination has appeared in my house.
Leviticus14:36 The priest must order them to clear the house before he enters to examine the contamination
Leviticus14:37 He will examine it
Leviticus14:38 the priest is to go outside the house to its doorway and quarantine the house for seven days.
Leviticus14:39 The priest is to return on the seventh day and examine it. If the contamination has spread on the walls of the house
Leviticus14:40 the priest must order that the stones with the contamination be pulled out and thrown into an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:41 He is to have the inside of the house completely scraped
Leviticus14:42 Then they must take different stones to replace the [former] ones and take additional plaster to replaster the house.
Leviticus14:43 If the contamination reappears in the house after the stones have been pulled out
Leviticus14:44 the priest must come and examine it. If the contamination has spread in the house
Leviticus14:45 It must be torn down with its stones
Leviticus14:46 Whoever enters the house during any of the days the priest quarantines it will be unclean until evening.
Leviticus14:47 Whoever lies down in the house is to wash his clothes
Leviticus14:48 But when the priest comes and examines it
Leviticus14:49 He is to take two birds
Leviticus14:50 and he is to slaughter one of the birds over a clay pot containing fresh water.
Leviticus14:51 He will take the cedar wood
Leviticus14:52 He will purify the house with the blood of the bird
Leviticus14:53 Then he is to release the live bird into the open countryside outside the city. In this way he will make atonement for the house
Leviticus14:54 This is the law for any skin disease or mildew
Leviticus14:55 for mildew in clothing or on a house
Leviticus14:56 and for a swelling
Leviticus14:57 to determine when something is unclean or clean. This is the law regarding skin disease and mildew.
Leviticus15:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Leviticus15:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When any man has a discharge from his body
Leviticus15:3 This is uncleanness of his discharge: Whether his body secretes the discharge or retains it
Leviticus15:4 Any bed the man with the discharge lies on will be unclean
Leviticus15:5 Anyone who touches his bed is to wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:6 Whoever sits on furniture that the man with the discharge was sitting on is to wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:7 Whoever touches the body of the man with a discharge is to wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:8 If the man with the discharge spits on anyone who is clean
Leviticus15:9 Any saddle the man with the discharge rides on will be unclean.
Leviticus15:10 Whoever touches anything that was under him will be unclean until evening
Leviticus15:11 If the man with the discharge touches anyone without [first] rinsing his hands in water
Leviticus15:12 Any clay pot that the man with the discharge touches must be broken
Leviticus15:13 When the man with the discharge has been cured of it
Leviticus15:14 He must take two turtledoves or two young pigeons on the eighth day
Leviticus15:15 The priest is to sacrifice them
Leviticus15:16 When a man has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:17 Any clothing or leather on which there is an emission of semen must be washed with water
Leviticus15:18 If a man sleeps with a woman and has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:19 When a woman has a discharge
Leviticus15:20 Anything she lies on during her menstruation will become unclean
Leviticus15:21 Everyone who touches her bed is to wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:22 Everyone who touches any furniture she was sitting on is to wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:23 If discharge is on the bed or the furniture she was sitting on
Leviticus15:24 If a man sleeps with her
Leviticus15:25 When a woman has a discharge of her blood for many days
Leviticus15:26 Any bed she lies on during the days of her discharge will be like her bed during menstrual impurity; any furniture she sits on will be unclean as in her menstrual period.
Leviticus15:27 Everyone who touches them will be unclean; he must wash his clothes and bathe with water
Leviticus15:28 When she is cured of her discharge
Leviticus15:29 On the eighth day she must take two turtledoves or two young pigeons and bring them to the priest at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus15:30 The priest is to sacrifice one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering. In this way the priest will make atonement for her before the LORD because of her unclean discharge.
Leviticus15:31 You must keep the Israelites from their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the law for someone with a discharge: a man who has an emission of semen
Leviticus15:33 a woman who is in her menstrual period; anyone who has a discharge
Leviticus16:1 The LORD spoke to Moses after the death of two of Aaron's sons when they approached the presence of the LORD and died.
Leviticus16:2 The LORD said to Moses: Tell your brother Aaron that he may not come whenever he wants into the holy place behind the veil in front of the mercy seat on the ark or else he will die
Leviticus16:3 Aaron is to enter the [most] holy place in this way: with a young bull for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:4 He is to wear a holy linen tunic
Leviticus16:5 He is to take from the Israelite community two male goats for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 Aaron will present the bull for his sin offering and make atonement for himself and his household.
Leviticus16:7 Next he will take the two goats and place them before the LORD at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Leviticus16:8 After Aaron casts lots for the two goats
Leviticus16:9 he is to present the goat chosen by lot for the LORD and sacrifice it as a sin offering.
Leviticus16:10 But the goat chosen by lot for Azazel is to be presented alive before the LORD to make purification with it by sending it into the wilderness for Azazel.
Leviticus16:11 When Aaron presents the bull for his sin offering and makes atonement for himself and his household
Leviticus16:12 Then he must take a firepan full of fiery coals from the altar before the LORD and two handfuls of finely ground fragrant incense
Leviticus16:13 He is to put the incense on the fire before the LORD
Leviticus16:14 He is to take some of the bull's blood and sprinkle [it] with his finger against the east side of the mercy seat; then he will sprinkle some of the blood with his finger before the mercy seat seven times.
Leviticus16:15 When he slaughters the male goat for the people's sin offering and brings its blood inside the veil
Leviticus16:16 He will purify the [most] holy place in this way for all their sins because of the Israelites' impurities and rebellious acts. He will do the same for the tent of meeting that remains among them
Leviticus16:17 No one may be in the tent of meeting from the time he enters to make atonement in the [most] holy place until he leaves after he has made atonement for himself
Leviticus16:18 Then he will go out to the altar that is before the LORD and make atonement for it. He is to take some of the bull's blood and some of the goat's blood and put [it] on the horns on all sides of the altar.
Leviticus16:19 He is to sprinkle some of the blood on it with his finger seven times to cleanse and set it apart from the Israelites' impurities.
Leviticus16:20 When he has finished purifying the [most] holy place
Leviticus16:21 Aaron will lay both his hands on the head of the live goat and confess over it all the Israelites' wrongdoings and rebellious acts-- all their sins. He is to put them on the goat's head and send [it] away into the wilderness by the man appointed for the task.
Leviticus16:22 The goat will carry on it all their wrongdoings into a desolate land
Leviticus16:23 Then Aaron is to enter the tent of meeting
Leviticus16:24 He will bathe his body with water in a holy place and put on his clothes. Then he must go out and sacrifice his burnt offering and the people's burnt offering; he will make atonement for himself and for the people.
Leviticus16:25 He is to burn the fat of the sin offering on the altar.
Leviticus16:26 The man who released the goat for Azazel is to wash his clothes and bathe his body with water; afterwards he may reenter the camp.
Leviticus16:27 The bull for the sin offering and the goat for the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 The one who burns them is to wash his clothes and bathe himself with water; afterwards he may reenter the camp.
Leviticus16:29 This is to be a permanent statute for you: In the seventh month
Leviticus16:30 Atonement will be made for you on this day to cleanse you
Leviticus16:31 It is a Sabbath of complete rest for you
Leviticus16:32 The priest who is anointed and ordained to serve as [high] priest in place of his father will make atonement. He will put on the linen garments
Leviticus16:33 and purify the most holy place. He will purify the tent of meeting and the altar and will make atonement for the priests and all the people of the assembly.
Leviticus16:34 This is to be a permanent statute for you
Leviticus17:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus17:2 Speak to Aaron
Leviticus17:3 Anyone from the house of Israel who slaughters an ox
Leviticus17:4 instead of bringing it to the entrance to the tent of meeting to present [it] as an offering to the LORD before His tabernacle-- that person will be charged with murder. He has shed blood and must be cut off from his people.
Leviticus17:5 This is so the Israelites will bring to the LORD the sacrifices they have been offering in the open country. They are to bring them to the priest at the entrance to the tent of meeting and offer them as fellowship sacrifices to the LORD.
Leviticus17:6 The priest will then sprinkle the blood on the LORD's altar at the entrance to the tent of meeting and burn the fat as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Leviticus17:7 They must no longer offer their sacrifices to the goat-demons that they have prostituted themselves with. This will be a permanent statute for them throughout their generations.
Leviticus17:8 Say to them: Anyone from the house of Israel or from the foreigners who live among them who offers a burnt offering or a sacrifice
Leviticus17:9 but does not bring it to the entrance to the tent of meeting to sacrifice it to the LORD
Leviticus17:10 Anyone from the house of Israel or from the foreigners who live among them who eats any blood
Leviticus17:11 For the life of a creature is in the blood
Leviticus17:12 Therefore I say to the Israelites: None of you and no foreigner who lives among you may eat blood.
Leviticus17:13 Any Israelite or foreigner living among them
Leviticus17:14 Since the life of every creature is its blood
Leviticus17:15 Every person
Leviticus17:16 But if he does not wash [his clothes] and bathe himself
Leviticus18:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus18:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus18:3 Do not follow the practices of the land of Egypt
Leviticus18:4 You are to practice My ordinances and you are to keep My statutes by following them; I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus18:5 Keep My statutes and ordinances; a person will live if he does them. I am the LORD.
Leviticus18:6 You are not to come near any close relative for sexual intercourse; I am the LORD.
Leviticus18:7 You are not to shame your father by having sex with your mother. She is your mother; you must not have sexual intercourse with her.
Leviticus18:8 You are not to have sex with your father's wife; it will shame your father.
Leviticus18:9 You are not to have sexual intercourse with your sister
Leviticus18:10 You are not to have sexual intercourse with your son's daughter or your daughter's daughter
Leviticus18:11 You are not to have sexual intercourse with your father's wife's daughter
Leviticus18:12 You are not to have sexual intercourse with your father's sister; she is your father's close relative.
Leviticus18:13 You are not to have sexual intercourse with your mother's sister
Leviticus18:14 You are not to shame your father's brother by coming near his wife to have sexual intercourse; she is your aunt.
Leviticus18:15 You are not to have sexual intercourse with your daughter-in-law. She is your son's wife; you are not to have sex with her.
Leviticus18:16 You are not to have sexual intercourse with your brother's wife; it will shame your brother.
Leviticus18:17 You are not to have sexual intercourse with a woman and her daughter. You are not to marry her son's daughter or her daughter's daughter and have sex with her. They are close relatives; it is depraved.
Leviticus18:18 You are not to marry a woman as a rival to her sister and have sexual intercourse with her during her [sister's] lifetime.
Leviticus18:19 You are not to come near a woman during her menstrual impurity to have sexual intercourse with her.
Leviticus18:20 You are not to have sexual intercourse with your neighbor's wife
Leviticus18:21 You are not to make any of your children pass through [the fire] to Molech. Do not profane the name of your God; I am the LORD.
Leviticus18:22 You are not to sleep with a man as with a woman; it is detestable.
Leviticus18:23 You are not to have sexual intercourse with any animal
Leviticus18:24 Do not defile yourselves by any of these [practices]
Leviticus18:25 The land has become defiled
Leviticus18:26 But you are to keep My statutes and ordinances. You must not commit any of these abominations-- not the native or the foreigner who lives among you.
Leviticus18:27 For the men who were in the land prior to you have committed all these abominations
Leviticus18:28 If you defile the land
Leviticus18:29 Any person who does any of these abominations must be cut off from his people.
Leviticus18:30 You must keep My instruction to not do any of the detestable customs that were practiced before you
Leviticus19:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus19:2 Speak to the entire Israelite community and tell them: Be holy because I
Leviticus19:3 Each of you is to respect his mother and father. You are to keep My Sabbaths; I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn to idols or make cast images of gods for yourselves; I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:5 When you offer a fellowship sacrifice to the LORD
Leviticus19:6 It is to be eaten on the day you sacrifice [it] or on the next day
Leviticus19:7 If any is eaten on the third day
Leviticus19:8 Anyone who eats it will bear his punishment
Leviticus19:9 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 You must not strip your vineyard bare or gather its fallen grapes. Leave them for the poor and the foreign resident; I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:11 You must not steal. You must not act deceptively or lie to one another.
Leviticus19:12 You must not swear falsely by My name
Leviticus19:13 You must not oppress your neighbor or rob [him]. The wages due a hired hand must not remain with you until morning.
Leviticus19:14 You must not curse the deaf or put a stumbling block in front of the blind
Leviticus19:15 You must not act unjustly when rendering judgment. Do not be partial to the poor or give preference to the rich; judge your neighbor fairly.
Leviticus19:16 You must not go about spreading slander among your people; you must not jeopardize your neighbor's life; I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:17 You must not hate your brother in your heart. Rebuke your neighbor directly
Leviticus19:18 Do not take revenge or bear a grudge against members of your community
Leviticus19:19 You are to keep My statutes. You must not crossbreed two different kinds of your livestock
Leviticus19:20 If a man has sexual intercourse with a woman who is a slave designated for [another] man
Leviticus19:21 However
Leviticus19:22 The priest will make atonement on his behalf before the LORD with the ram of the restitution offering for the sin he has committed
Leviticus19:23 When you come into the land and plant any kind of tree for food
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year all its fruit must be consecrated as a praise offering to the LORD.
Leviticus19:25 But in the fifth year you may eat its fruit. In this way its yield will increase for you; I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus19:26 You are not to eat [anything] with blood [in it]. You are not to practice divination or sorcery.
Leviticus19:27 You are not to cut off the hair at the sides of your head or mar the edge of your beard.
Leviticus19:28 You are not to make gashes on your bodies for the dead or put tattoo marks on yourselves; I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:29 Do not debase your daughter by making her a prostitute
Leviticus19:30 You must keep My Sabbaths and revere My sanctuary; I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:31 Do not turn to mediums or consult spiritists
Leviticus19:32 You are to rise in the presence of the elderly and honor the old. Fear your God; I am the LORD.
Leviticus19:33 When a foreigner lives with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 You must regard the foreigner who lives with you as the native-born among you. You are to love him as yourself
Leviticus19:35 You must not act unfairly in measurements of length
Leviticus19:36 You are to have honest balances
Leviticus19:37 You must keep all My statutes and all My ordinances and do them; I am the LORD.
Leviticus20:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus20:2 Say to the Israelites: Any Israelite or foreigner living in Israel who gives any of his children to Molech must be put to death; the people of the country are to stone him.
Leviticus20:3 I will turn against that man and cut him off from his people
Leviticus20:4 But if the people of the country look the other way when that man gives any of his children to Molech
Leviticus20:5 then I will turn against that man and his family
Leviticus20:6 Whoever turns to mediums or spiritists and prostitutes himself with them
Leviticus20:7 Consecrate yourselves and be holy
Leviticus20:8 Keep My statutes and do them; I am the LORD who sets you apart.
Leviticus20:9 If anyone curses his father or mother
Leviticus20:10 If a man commits adultery with a married woman-- if he commits adultery with his neighbor's wife-- both the adulterer and the adulteress must be put to death.
Leviticus20:11 If a man sleeps with his father's wife
Leviticus20:12 If a man sleeps with his daughter-in-law
Leviticus20:13 If a man sleeps with a man as with a woman
Leviticus20:14 If a man marries a woman and her mother
Leviticus20:15 If a man has sexual intercourse with an animal
Leviticus20:16 If a woman comes near any animal and mates with it
Leviticus20:17 If a man marries his sister
Leviticus20:18 If a man sleeps with a menstruating woman and has sexual intercourse with her
Leviticus20:19 You must not have sexual intercourse with your mother's sister or your father's sister
Leviticus20:20 If a man sleeps with his aunt
Leviticus20:21 If a man marries his brother's wife
Leviticus20:22 You are to keep all My statutes and all My ordinances
Leviticus20:23 You must not follow the statutes of the nations I am driving out before you
Leviticus20:24 And I promised you: You will inherit their land
Leviticus20:25 Therefore you must distinguish the clean animal from the unclean one
Leviticus20:26 You are to be holy to Me because I
Leviticus20:27 A man or a woman who is a medium or a spiritist must be put to death. They are to be stoned; their blood is on their own hands.
Leviticus21:1 The LORD said to Moses: Speak to Aaron's sons
Leviticus21:2 except for his immediate family: his mother
Leviticus21:3 He may make himself unclean for his young unmarried sister in his immediate family.
Leviticus21:4 He is not to make himself unclean for those related to him by marriage and so defile himself.
Leviticus21:5 Priests may not make bald spots on their heads
Leviticus21:6 They are to be holy to their God and not profane the name of their God
Leviticus21:7 They are not to marry a woman defiled by prostitution or divorced by her husband
Leviticus21:8 You are to consider him holy since he presents the food of your God. He will be holy to you because I
Leviticus21:9 If a priest's daughter defiles herself by promiscuity
Leviticus21:10 The priest who is highest among his brothers
Leviticus21:11 He must not go near any dead person or make himself unclean [even] for his father or mother.
Leviticus21:12 He must not leave the sanctuary or he will desecrate the sanctuary of his God
Leviticus21:13 He is to marry a woman who is a virgin.
Leviticus21:14 He is not to marry a widow
Leviticus21:15 so that he does not corrupt his bloodline among his people
Leviticus21:16 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus21:17 Tell Aaron: None of your descendants throughout your generations who has a physical defect is to come near to present the food of his God.
Leviticus21:18 No man who has any defect is to come near: no man who is blind
Leviticus21:19 no man who has a broken foot or hand
Leviticus21:20 or who is a hunchback or a dwarf
Leviticus21:21 No descendant of Aaron the priest who has a defect is to come near to present the fire offerings to the LORD. He has a defect and is not to come near to present the food of his God.
Leviticus21:22 He may eat the food of his God from what is especially holy as well as from what is holy.
Leviticus21:23 But because he has a defect
Leviticus21:24 Moses said [this] to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites.
Leviticus22:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus22:2 Tell Aaron and his sons to deal respectfully with the holy offerings of the Israelites that they have consecrated to Me
Leviticus22:3 Say to them: If any man from any of your descendants throughout your generations is in a state of uncleanness yet approaches the holy offerings that the Israelites consecrate to the LORD
Leviticus22:4 No man of Aaron's descendants who has a skin disease or a discharge is to eat from the holy offerings until he is clean. Whoever touches anything made unclean by a dead person or by a man who has an emission of semen
Leviticus22:5 or whoever touches any swarming creature that makes him unclean or any person who makes him unclean-- whatever his uncleanness--
Leviticus22:6 the man who touches any of these will remain unclean until evening and is not to eat from the holy offerings unless he has bathed his body with water.
Leviticus22:7 When the sun has set
Leviticus22:8 He must not eat an animal that died naturally or was mauled by wild beasts
Leviticus22:9 They must keep My instruction
Leviticus22:10 No one outside a priest's family is to eat the holy offering. A foreigner staying with a priest or a hired hand is not to eat the holy offering.
Leviticus22:11 But if a priest purchases someone with his money
Leviticus22:12 If the priest's daughter is married to a man outside a priest's family
Leviticus22:13 But if the priest's daughter becomes widowed or divorced
Leviticus22:14 If anyone eats a holy offering in error
Leviticus22:15 The priests must not profane the holy offerings the Israelites give to the LORD
Leviticus22:16 and have them bear the penalty of restitution if the people eat their holy offerings. For I am the LORD who sets them apart.

Leviticus22:18 Speak to Aaron
Leviticus22:19 must offer an unblemished male from the cattle
Leviticus22:20 You are not to present anything that has a defect
Leviticus22:21 When a man presents a fellowship sacrifice to the LORD to fulfill a vow or as a freewill offering from the herd or flock
Leviticus22:22 You are not to present any [animal] to the LORD that is blind
Leviticus22:23 You may sacrifice as a freewill offering any animal from the herd or flock that has an elongated or stunted limb
Leviticus22:24 You are not to present to the LORD anything that has bruised
Leviticus22:25 Neither you nor a foreigner are to present food to your God from any of these animals. They will not be accepted for you because they are deformed and have a defect.
Leviticus22:26 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus22:27 When an ox
Leviticus22:28 But you are not to slaughter an animal from the herd or flock on the same day as its young.
Leviticus22:29 When you sacrifice a thank offering to the LORD
Leviticus22:30 It is to be eaten on the same day. Do not let any of it remain until morning; I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:31 You are to keep My commands and do them; I am the LORD.
Leviticus22:32 You must not profane My holy name; I must be treated as holy among the Israelites. I am the LORD who sets you apart
Leviticus22:33 the One who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God; I am the LORD.
Leviticus23:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: These are My appointed times
Leviticus23:3 For six days work may be done
Leviticus23:4 These are the LORD's appointed times
Leviticus23:5 The Passover to the LORD comes in the first month
Leviticus23:6 The Festival of Unleavened Bread to the LORD is on the fifteenth day of the same month. For seven days you must eat unleavened bread.
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you are to hold a sacred assembly; you are not to do any daily work.
Leviticus23:8 You are to present a fire offering to the LORD for seven days. On the seventh day there will be a sacred assembly; you must not do any daily work.
Leviticus23:9 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:10 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land I am giving you and reap its harvest
Leviticus23:11 He will wave the sheaf before the LORD so that you may be accepted; the priest is to wave it on the day after the Sabbath.
Leviticus23:12 On the day you wave the sheaf
Leviticus23:13 Its grain offering is to be four quarts of fine flour mixed with oil as a fire offering to the LORD
Leviticus23:14 You must not eat bread
Leviticus23:15 You are to count seven complete weeks starting from the day after the Sabbath
Leviticus23:16 You are to count 50 days until the day after the seventh Sabbath and then present an offering of new grain to the LORD.
Leviticus23:17 Bring two loaves of bread from your settlements as a presentation offering
Leviticus23:18 You are to present with the bread seven unblemished male lambs a year old
Leviticus23:19 You are also to prepare one male goat as a sin offering
Leviticus23:20 The priest will wave the lambs with the bread of firstfruits as a presentation offering before the LORD; the bread and the two lambs will be holy to the LORD for the priest.
Leviticus23:21 On that same day you are to make a proclamation and hold a sacred assembly. You are not to do any daily work. This is to be a permanent statute wherever you live throughout your generations.
Leviticus23:22 When you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:24 Tell the Israelites: In the seventh month
Leviticus23:25 You must not do any daily work
Leviticus23:26 The LORD again spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:27 The tenth [day] of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement. You are to hold a sacred assembly and practice self-denial; you are to present a fire offering to the LORD.
Leviticus23:28 On this particular day you are not to do any work
Leviticus23:29 If any person does not practice self-denial on this particular day
Leviticus23:30 I will destroy among his people anyone who does any work on this same day.
Leviticus23:31 You are not to do any work. This is a permanent statute throughout your generations wherever you live.
Leviticus23:32 It will be a Sabbath of complete rest for you
Leviticus23:33 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus23:34 Tell the Israelites: The Festival of Booths to the LORD begins on the fifteenth day of this seventh month and continues for seven days.
Leviticus23:35 There is to be a sacred assembly on the first day; you are not to do any daily work.
Leviticus23:36 You are to present a fire offering to the LORD for seven days. On the eighth day you are to hold a sacred assembly and present a fire offering to the LORD. It is a solemn gathering; you are not to do any daily work.
Leviticus23:37 These are the LORD's appointed times that you are to proclaim as sacred assemblies for presenting fire offerings to the LORD
Leviticus23:38 These are in addition to the offerings for the LORD's Sabbaths
Leviticus23:39 You are to celebrate the LORD's festival on the fifteenth day of the seventh month for seven days after you have gathered the produce of the land. There will be complete rest on the first day and complete rest on the eighth day.
Leviticus23:40 On the first day you are to take the product of majestic trees-- palm fronds
Leviticus23:41 You are to celebrate it as a festival to the LORD seven days each year. This is a permanent statute for you throughout your generations; you must celebrate it in the seventh month.
Leviticus23:42 You are to live in booths for seven days. All the native-born of Israel must live in booths
Leviticus23:43 so that your generations may know that I made the Israelites live in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt; I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus23:44 So Moses declared the LORD's appointed times to the Israelites.
Leviticus24:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus24:2 Command the Israelites to bring you pure oil of beaten olives for the light
Leviticus24:3 Aaron is to tend it regularly from evening until morning before the LORD outside the veil of the testimony in the tent of meeting. This is a permanent statute throughout your generations.
Leviticus24:4 He must regularly tend the lamps on the pure [gold] lampstand in the LORD's presence.
Leviticus24:5 Take fine flour and bake it into 12 loaves; each loaf is to be made with four quarts.
Leviticus24:6 Arrange them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 Place pure frankincense near each row
Leviticus24:8 The bread is to be set out before the LORD every Sabbath day as a perpetual covenant obligation on the part of the Israelites.
Leviticus24:9 It belongs to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus24:10 Now the son of an Israelite mother and an Egyptian father was among the Israelites. A fight broke out in the camp between the Israelite woman's son and an Israelite man.
Leviticus24:11 Her son cursed and blasphemed the Name
Leviticus24:12 They put him in custody until the LORD's decision could be made clear to them.
Leviticus24:13 Then the LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus24:14 Bring the one who has cursed to the outside of the camp and have all who heard [him] lay their hands on his head; then have the whole community stone him.
Leviticus24:15 And tell the Israelites: If anyone curses his God
Leviticus24:16 Whoever blasphemes the name of the LORD is to be put to death; the whole community must stone him. If he blasphemes the Name
Leviticus24:17 If a man kills anyone
Leviticus24:18 Whoever kills an animal is to make restitution for it
Leviticus24:19 If any man inflicts a permanent injury on his neighbor
Leviticus24:20 fracture for fracture
Leviticus24:21 Whoever kills an animal is to make restitution for it
Leviticus24:22 You are to have the same law for the foreign resident and the native
Leviticus24:23 After Moses spoke to the Israelites
Leviticus25:1 The LORD spoke to Moses on Mount Sinai:
Leviticus25:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land I am giving you
Leviticus25:3 You may sow your field for six years
Leviticus25:4 But there will be a Sabbath of complete rest for the land in the seventh year
Leviticus25:5 You are not to reap what grows by itself from your crop
Leviticus25:6 [Whatever] the land [produces during] the Sabbath year can be food for you; for yourself
Leviticus25:7 All of its growth may serve as food for your livestock and the wild animals in your land.
Leviticus25:8 You are to count seven sabbatic years
Leviticus25:9 Then you are to sound a trumpet loudly in the seventh month
Leviticus25:10 You are to consecrate the fiftieth year and proclaim freedom in the land for all its inhabitants. It will be your Jubilee
Leviticus25:11 The fiftieth year will be your Jubilee; you are not to sow
Leviticus25:12 It is to be holy to you because it is the Jubilee; you may [only] eat its produce [directly] from the field.
Leviticus25:13 In this Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:14 If you make a sale to your neighbor or a purchase from him
Leviticus25:15 You are to make the purchase from your neighbor based on the number of years since the last Jubilee. He is to sell to you based on the number of [remaining] harvest years.
Leviticus25:16 You are to increase its price in proportion to a greater amount of years
Leviticus25:17 You are not to cheat one another
Leviticus25:18 You are to observe My statutes and ordinances and carefully observe them
Leviticus25:19 Then the land will yield its fruit
Leviticus25:20 If you wonder: 'What will we eat in the seventh year if we don't sow or gather our produce?'
Leviticus25:21 I will appoint My blessing for you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 When you sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 The land is not to be permanently sold because it is Mine
Leviticus25:24 You are to allow the redemption of any land you occupy.
Leviticus25:25 If your brother becomes destitute and sells part of his property
Leviticus25:26 If a man has no family redeemer
Leviticus25:27 he may calculate the years since its sale
Leviticus25:28 But if he cannot obtain enough to repay him
Leviticus25:29 If a man sells a residence in a walled city
Leviticus25:30 If it is not redeemed by the end of a full year
Leviticus25:31 But houses in villages that have no walls around them are to be classified as open fields. The right to redeem [such] houses stays in effect
Leviticus25:32 Concerning the Levitical cities
Leviticus25:33 Whatever [property] one of the Levites can redeem-- a house sold in a city they possess-- must be released at the Jubilee
Leviticus25:34 The open pastureland around their cities may not be sold
Leviticus25:35 If your brother becomes destitute and cannot sustain himself among you
Leviticus25:36 Do not profit or take interest from him
Leviticus25:37 You are not to lend him your silver with interest or sell [him] your food for profit.
Leviticus25:38 I am the LORD your God
Leviticus25:39 If your brother among you becomes destitute and sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 Let him stay with you as a hired hand or temporary resident; he may work for you until the Year of Jubilee.
Leviticus25:41 Then he and his children are to be released from you
Leviticus25:42 They are not to be sold as slaves
Leviticus25:43 You are not to rule over them harshly but fear your God.
Leviticus25:44 Your male and female slaves are to be from the nations around you; you may purchase male and female slaves.
Leviticus25:45 You may also purchase them from the foreigners staying with you
Leviticus25:46 You may leave them to your sons after you to inherit as property; you can make them slaves for life. But concerning your brothers
Leviticus25:47 If a foreigner or temporary resident [living] among you prospers
Leviticus25:48 he has the right of redemption after he has been sold. One of his brothers may redeem him.
Leviticus25:49 His uncle or cousin may redeem him
Leviticus25:50 The one who purchased him is to calculate [the time] from the year he sold himself to him until the Year of Jubilee. The price of his sale will be [determined] by the number of years. It will be [set] for him like the daily wages of a hired hand.
Leviticus25:51 If many years are still left
Leviticus25:52 If only a few years remain until the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:53 He will stay with him like a man hired year by year. A foreign owner is not to rule over him harshly in your sight.
Leviticus25:54 If he is not redeemed in any of these [ways]
Leviticus25:55 For the Israelites are My slaves. They are My slaves I brought out of the land of Egypt; I am the LORD your God.
Leviticus26:1 Do not make idols for yourselves
Leviticus26:2 You must keep My Sabbaths and honor My sanctuary; I am the LORD.
Leviticus26:3 If you follow My statutes and faithfully observe My commands
Leviticus26:4 I will give you rain at the right time
Leviticus26:5 Your threshing will continue until grape harvest
Leviticus26:6 I will give peace to the land
Leviticus26:7 You will pursue your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you will pursue 100
Leviticus26:9 I will turn to you
Leviticus26:10 You will eat the old grain of the previous year and will clear out the old to make room for the new.
Leviticus26:11 I will place My residence among you
Leviticus26:12 I will walk among you and be your God
Leviticus26:13 I am the LORD your God
Leviticus26:14 But if you do not obey Me and observe all these commands--
Leviticus26:15 if you reject My statutes and despise My ordinances
Leviticus26:16 then I will do this to you: I will bring terror on you-- wasting disease and fever that will cause your eyes to fail and your life to ebb away. You will sow your seed in vain because your enemies will eat it.
Leviticus26:17 I will turn against you
Leviticus26:18 But if after these things you will not obey Me
Leviticus26:19 I will break down your strong pride. I will make your sky like iron and your land like bronze
Leviticus26:20 and your strength will be used up for nothing. Your land will not yield its produce
Leviticus26:21 If you act with hostility toward Me and are unwilling to obey Me
Leviticus26:22 I will send wild animals against you that will deprive you of your children
Leviticus26:23 If in spite of these things you do not accept My discipline
Leviticus26:24 then I will act with hostility toward you; I also will strike you seven times for your sins.
Leviticus26:25 I will bring a sword against you to execute the vengeance of the covenant. Though you withdraw into your cities
Leviticus26:26 When I cut off your supply of bread
Leviticus26:27 And if in spite of this you do not obey Me but act with hostility toward Me
Leviticus26:28 I will act with furious hostility toward you; I will also discipline you seven times for your sins.
Leviticus26:29 You will eat the flesh of your sons; you will eat the flesh of your daughters.
Leviticus26:30 I will destroy your high places
Leviticus26:31 I will reduce your cities to ruins and devastate your sanctuaries. I will not smell the pleasing aroma of your [sacrifices].
Leviticus26:32 I also will devastate the land
Leviticus26:33 But I will scatter you among the nations
Leviticus26:34 Then the land will make up for its Sabbath [years] during the time it lies desolate
Leviticus26:35 As long as it lies desolate
Leviticus26:36 I will put anxiety in the hearts of those of you who survive in the lands of their enemies. The sound of a wind-driven leaf will put them to flight
Leviticus26:37 They will stumble over one another as if [fleeing] from a sword though no one is pursuing [them]. You will not be able to stand against your enemies.
Leviticus26:38 You will perish among the nations; the land of your enemies will devour you.
Leviticus26:39 Those who survive in the lands of your enemies will waste away because of their sin; they will also waste away because of their fathers' sins along with theirs.
Leviticus26:40 But if they will confess their sin and the sin of their fathers-- their unfaithfulness that they practiced against Me
Leviticus26:41 and I acted with hostility toward them and brought them into the land of their enemies-- and if their uncircumcised hearts will be humbled
Leviticus26:42 then I will remember My covenant with Jacob. I will also remember My covenant with Isaac and My covenant with Abraham
Leviticus26:43 For the land abandoned by them will make up for its Sabbaths by lying desolate without the people
Leviticus26:44 Yet in spite of this
Leviticus26:45 For their sake I will remember the covenant with their fathers
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes
Leviticus27:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Leviticus27:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When someone makes a special vow to the LORD that involves the valuation of people
Leviticus27:3 if the valuation concerns a male from 20 to 60 years old
Leviticus27:4 If the person is a female
Leviticus27:5 If the person is from five to 20 years old
Leviticus27:6 If the person is from one month to five years old
Leviticus27:7 If the person is 60 years or more
Leviticus27:8 But if one is too poor to pay the valuation
Leviticus27:9 If the vow involves one of the animals that may be brought as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus27:10 He may not replace it or make a substitution for it
Leviticus27:11 If the vow involves any of the unclean animals that may not be brought as an offering to the LORD
Leviticus27:12 The priest will set its value
Leviticus27:13 If the one who brought it decides to redeem it
Leviticus27:14 When a man consecrates his house as holy to the LORD
Leviticus27:15 But if the one who consecrated his house redeems [it]
Leviticus27:16 If a man consecrates to the LORD any part of a field that he possesses
Leviticus27:17 If he consecrates his field during the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:18 But if he consecrates his field after the Jubilee
Leviticus27:19 If the one who consecrated the field decides to redeem it
Leviticus27:20 But if he does not redeem the field or if he has sold it to another man
Leviticus27:21 When the field is released in the Jubilee
Leviticus27:22 If a person consecrates to the LORD a field he has purchased that is not part of his inherited landholding
Leviticus27:23 then the priest will calculate for him the amount of the valuation up to the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:24 In the Year of Jubilee the field will return to the one he bought it from
Leviticus27:25 All your valuations will be [measured] by the standard sanctuary shekel
Leviticus27:26 But no one can consecrate a firstborn of the livestock
Leviticus27:27 If it is one of the unclean livestock
Leviticus27:28 Nothing that a man permanently sets apart to the LORD from all he owns
Leviticus27:29 No person who has been set apart [for destruction] is to be ransomed; he must be put to death.
Leviticus27:30 Every tenth of the land's produce
Leviticus27:31 If a man decides to redeem any part of this tenth
Leviticus27:32 Every tenth animal from the herd or flock
Leviticus27:33 He is not to inspect whether it is good or bad
Leviticus27:34 These are the commands the LORD gave Moses for the Israelites on Mount Sinai.
Numbers1:1 The LORD spoke to Moses in the tent of meeting in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers1:2 Take a census of the entire Israelite community by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:3 You and Aaron are to register those who are 20 years old or more by their military divisions-- everyone who can serve in Israel's army.
Numbers1:4 A man from each tribe is to be with you
Numbers1:5 These are the names of the men who are to assist you: Elizur son of Shedeur from Reuben;
Numbers1:6 Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai from Simeon;
Numbers1:7 Nahshon son of Amminadab from Judah;
Numbers1:8 Nethanel son of Zuar from Issachar;
Numbers1:9 Eliab son of Helon from Zebulun;
Numbers1:10 from the sons of Joseph: Elishama son of Ammihud from Ephraim
Numbers1:11 Abidan son of Gideoni from Benjamin;
Numbers1:12 Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai from Dan;
Numbers1:13 Pagiel son of Ochran from Asher;
Numbers1:14 Eliasaph son of Deuel from Gad;
Numbers1:15 Ahira son of Enan from Naphtali.
Numbers1:16 These are the men called from the community; they are leaders of their ancestral tribes
Numbers1:17 So Moses and Aaron took these men who had been designated by name
Numbers1:18 and they assembled the whole community on the first day of the second month. They recorded their ancestry by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:19 just as the LORD commanded Moses. He registered them in the Wilderness of Sinai:
Numbers1:20 The descendants of Reuben
Numbers1:21 those registered for the tribe of Reuben numbered 46
Numbers1:22 The descendants of Simeon: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:23 those registered for the tribe of Simeon numbered 59
Numbers1:24 The descendants of Gad: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:25 those registered for the tribe of Gad numbered 45
Numbers1:26 The descendants of Judah: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:27 those registered for the tribe of Judah numbered 74
Numbers1:28 The descendants of Issachar: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:29 those registered for the tribe of Issachar numbered 54
Numbers1:30 The descendants of Zebulun: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:31 those registered for the tribe of Zebulun numbered 57
Numbers1:32 The descendants of Joseph: The descendants of Ephraim: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:33 those registered for the tribe of Ephraim numbered 40
Numbers1:34 The descendants of Manasseh: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:35 those registered for the tribe of Manasseh numbered 32
Numbers1:36 The descendants of Benjamin: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:37 those registered for the tribe of Benjamin numbered 35
Numbers1:38 The descendants of Dan: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:39 those registered for the tribe of Dan numbered 62
Numbers1:40 The descendants of Asher: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:41 those registered for the tribe of Asher numbered 41
Numbers1:42 The descendants of Naphtali: according to their family records by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers1:43 those registered for the tribe of Naphtali numbered 53
Numbers1:44 These are the men Moses and Aaron registered
Numbers1:45 So all the Israelites 20 years old or more
Numbers1:46 All those registered numbered 603
Numbers1:47 But the Levites were not registered with them by their ancestral tribe.
Numbers1:48 For the LORD had told Moses:
Numbers1:49 Do not register or take a census of the tribe of Levi with the [other] Israelites.
Numbers1:50 Appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the testimony
Numbers1:51 Whenever the tabernacle is to move
Numbers1:52 The Israelites are to camp by their military divisions
Numbers1:53 The Levites are to camp around the tabernacle of the testimony and watch over it
Numbers1:54 The Israelites did everything just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Numbers2:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers2:2 The Israelites are to camp under their respective banners beside the flags of their ancestral houses. They are to camp around the tent of meeting at a distance [from it]:
Numbers2:3 Judah's military divisions will camp on the east side toward the sunrise under their banner. The leader of the descendants of Judah is Nahshon son of Amminadab.
Numbers2:4 His military division numbers 74
Numbers2:5 The tribe of Issachar will camp next to it. The leader of the Issacharites is Nethanel son of Zuar.
Numbers2:6 His military division numbers 54
Numbers2:7 The tribe of Zebulun [will be next]. The leader of the Zebulunites is Eliab son of Helon.
Numbers2:8 His military division numbers 57
Numbers2:9 The total number in their military divisions who belong to Judah's encampment is 186
Numbers2:10 Reuben's military divisions will camp on the south side under their banner. The leader of the Reubenites is Elizur son of Shedeur.
Numbers2:11 His military division numbers 46
Numbers2:12 The tribe of Simeon will camp next to it. The leader of the Simeonites is Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai.
Numbers2:13 His military division numbers 59
Numbers2:14 The tribe of Gad [will be next]. The leader of the Gadites is Eliasaph son of Deuel.
Numbers2:15 His military division numbers 45
Numbers2:16 The total number in their military divisions who belong to Reuben's encampment is 151
Numbers2:17 The tent of meeting is to move out with the Levites' camp
Numbers2:18 Ephraim's military divisions will camp on the west side under their banner. The leader of the Ephraimites is Elishama son of Ammihud.
Numbers2:19 His military division numbers 40
Numbers2:20 The tribe of Manasseh will be next to it. The leader of the Manassites is Gamaliel son of Pedahzur.
Numbers2:21 His military division numbers 32
Numbers2:22 The tribe of Benjamin [will be next]. The leader of the Benjaminites is Abidan son of Gideoni.
Numbers2:23 His military division numbers 35
Numbers2:24 The total in their military divisions who belong to Ephraim's encampment number 108
Numbers2:25 Dan's military divisions will camp on the north side under their banner. The leader of the Danites is Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
Numbers2:26 His military division numbers 62
Numbers2:27 The tribe of Asher will camp next to it. The leader of the Asherites is Pagiel son of Ochran.
Numbers2:28 His military division numbers 41
Numbers2:29 The tribe of Naphtali [will be next]. The leader of the Naphtalites is Ahira son of Enan.
Numbers2:30 His military division numbers 53
Numbers2:31 The total number who belong to Dan's encampment is 157
Numbers2:32 These are the Israelites registered by their ancestral houses. The total number in the camps by their military divisions is 603
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not registered among the Israelites
Numbers2:34 The Israelites did everything the LORD commanded Moses; they camped by their banners in this way and moved out the same way
Numbers3:1 These are the family records of Aaron and Moses at the time the LORD spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These are the names of Aaron's sons: Nadab
Numbers3:3 These are the names of Aaron's sons
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu died in the LORD's presence when they presented unauthorized fire before the LORD in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:5 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers3:6 Bring the tribe of Levi near and present them to Aaron the priest to assist him.
Numbers3:7 They are to perform duties for him and the entire community before the tent of meeting by attending to the service of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:8 They are to take care of all the furnishings of the tent of meeting and perform duties for the Israelites by attending to the service of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:9 Assign the Levites to Aaron and his sons; they have been assigned exclusively to him from the Israelites.
Numbers3:10 You are to appoint Aaron and his sons to carry out their priestly responsibilities
Numbers3:11 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers3:12 See
Numbers3:13 because every firstborn belongs to Me. At the time I struck down every firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 The LORD spoke to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai:
Numbers3:15 Register the Levites by their ancestral houses and their clans. You are to register every male one month old or more.
Numbers3:16 So Moses registered them in obedience to the LORD as he had been commanded:
Numbers3:17 These were Levi's sons by name: Gershon
Numbers3:18 These were the names of Gershon's sons by their clans: Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 Kohath's sons by their clans were Amram
Numbers3:20 Merari's sons by their clans were Mahli and Mushi. These were the Levite clans by their ancestral houses.
Numbers3:21 The Libnite clan and the Shimeite clan came from Gershon; these were the Gershonite clans.
Numbers3:22 Those registered
Numbers3:23 The Gershonite clans camped behind the tabernacle on the west side
Numbers3:24 and the leader of the Gershonite family was Eliasaph son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 The Gershonites' duties at the tent of meeting involved the tabernacle
Numbers3:26 the hangings of the courtyard
Numbers3:27 The Amramite clan
Numbers3:28 Counting every male one month old or more
Numbers3:29 The clans of the Kohathites camped on the south side of the tabernacle
Numbers3:30 and the leader of the family of the Kohathite clans was Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Their duties involved the ark
Numbers3:32 The chief of the Levite leaders was Eleazar son of Aaron the priest; he had oversight of those responsible for the duties of the sanctuary.
Numbers3:33 The Mahlite clan and the Mushite clan came from Merari; these were the Merarite clans.
Numbers3:34 Those registered
Numbers3:35 The leader of the family of the Merarite clans was Zuriel son of Abihail; they camped on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 The assigned duties of Merari's descendants involved the tabernacle's supports
Numbers3:37 in addition to the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their bases
Numbers3:38 Moses
Numbers3:39 The total number of all the Levite males one month old or more that Moses and Aaron registered by their clans at the LORD's command was 22
Numbers3:40 The LORD told Moses: Register every firstborn male of the Israelites one month old or more
Numbers3:41 You are to take the Levites for Me-- I am the Lord-- in place of every firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:42 So Moses registered every firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:43 The total number of the firstborn males one month old or more listed by name was 22
Numbers3:44 The LORD spoke to Moses again:
Numbers3:45 Take the Levites in place of every firstborn among the Israelites
Numbers3:46 As the redemption price for the 273 firstborn Israelites who outnumber the Levites
Numbers3:47 collect five shekels for each person
Numbers3:48 Give the money to Aaron and his sons as the redemption price for those who are in excess among the Israelites.
Numbers3:49 So Moses collected the redemption money from those in excess of the ones redeemed by the Levites.
Numbers3:50 He collected the money from the firstborn Israelites: 1
Numbers3:51 He gave the redemption money to Aaron and his sons in obedience to the LORD
Numbers4:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers4:2 Among the Levites
Numbers4:3 men from 30 years old to 50 years old-- everyone who is qualified to do work at the tent of meeting.
Numbers4:4 The service of the Kohathites at the tent of meeting concerns the most holy objects.
Numbers4:5 Whenever the camp is about to move on
Numbers4:6 They are to place over this a covering made of manatee skin
Numbers4:7 They are to spread a blue cloth over the table of the Presence and place the plates and cups on it
Numbers4:8 They are to spread a scarlet cloth over them
Numbers4:9 They are to take a blue cloth and cover the lampstand used for light
Numbers4:10 Then they must place it with all its utensils inside a covering made of manatee skin and put [them] on the carrying frame.
Numbers4:11 They are to spread a blue cloth over the gold altar
Numbers4:12 They are to take all the serving utensils they use in the sanctuary
Numbers4:13 They are to remove the ashes from the [bronze] altar
Numbers4:14 and place all the equipment on it that they use in serving: the firepans
Numbers4:15 Aaron and his sons are to finish covering the holy objects and all their equipment whenever the camp is to move on. The Kohathites will come and carry them
Numbers4:16 Eleazar

Numbers4:18 Do not allow the Kohathite tribal clans to be wiped out from the Levites.
Numbers4:19 Do this for them so that they may live and not die when they come near the most holy objects: Aaron and his sons are to go in and assign each man his task and transportation duty.
Numbers4:20 The Kohathites are not to go in and look at the holy objects

Numbers4:26 the hangings of the courtyard
Numbers4:27 All the service of the Gershonites

Numbers4:29 As for the Merarites

Numbers4:32 the posts of the surrounding courtyard with their bases

Numbers4:42 The men of the Merarite clans were registered by their clans and their ancestral houses
Numbers4:43 those from 30 years old to 50 years old

Numbers5:1 The LORD instructed Moses:
Numbers5:2 Command the Israelites to send away anyone from the camp who is afflicted with a skin disease
Numbers5:3 You must send away both male or female; send them outside the camp
Numbers5:4 The Israelites did this
Numbers5:5 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers5:6 Tell the Israelites: When a man or woman commits any sin against another
Numbers5:7 The person is to confess the sin he has committed. He is to pay full compensation
Numbers5:8 But if that individual has no relative to receive compensation
Numbers5:9 Every holy contribution the Israelites present to the priest will be his.
Numbers5:10 Each one's holy contribution is his [to give]; what each one gives to the priest will be his.
Numbers5:11 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers5:12 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: If any man's wife goes astray
Numbers5:13 and sleeps with another
Numbers5:14 and if a feeling of jealousy comes over the husband and he becomes jealous because of his wife who has defiled herself-- or if a feeling of jealousy comes over him and he becomes jealous of her though she has not defiled herself--
Numbers5:15 then the man is to bring his wife to the priest. He is also to bring an offering for her of two quarts of barley flour. He is not to pour oil over it or put frankincense on it because it is a grain offering of jealousy
Numbers5:16 The priest is to bring her forward and have her stand before the LORD.
Numbers5:17 Then the priest is to take holy water in a clay bowl
Numbers5:18 After the priest has the woman stand before the Lord
Numbers5:19 The priest will require the woman to take an oath and will say to her
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray while under your husband's authority
Numbers5:21 at this point the priest must make the woman take the oath with the sworn curse
Numbers5:22 May this water that brings a curse enter your stomach
Numbers5:23 Then the priest is to write these curses on a scroll and wash [them] off into the bitter water.
Numbers5:24 He will require the woman to drink the bitter water that brings a curse
Numbers5:25 The priest is to take the grain offering of jealousy from the woman's hand
Numbers5:26 The priest is to take a handful of the grain offering as a memorial portion and burn it on the altar. Then he will require the woman to drink the water.
Numbers5:27 When he makes her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman has not defiled herself and is pure
Numbers5:29 This is the law regarding jealousy when a wife goes astray and defiles herself while under her husband's authority
Numbers5:30 or when a feeling of jealousy comes over a husband and he becomes jealous of his wife. He is to have the woman stand before the LORD
Numbers5:31 The husband will be free of guilt
Numbers6:1 The LORD instructed Moses:
Numbers6:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When a man or woman makes a special vow
Numbers6:3 he is to abstain from wine and beer. He must not drink vinegar made from wine or from beer. He must not drink any grape juice or eat fresh grapes or raisins.
Numbers6:4 He is not to eat anything produced by the grapevine
Numbers6:5 You must not cut his hair throughout the time of his vow of consecration. He must be holy until the time is completed during which he consecrates himself to the LORD; he is to let the hair of his head grow long.
Numbers6:6 He must not go near a dead body during the time he consecrates himself to the LORD.
Numbers6:7 He is not to defile himself for his father or mother
Numbers6:8 He is holy to the LORD during the time of consecration.
Numbers6:9 If someone suddenly dies near him
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he is to bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
Numbers6:11 The priest is to offer one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to make atonement on behalf of the Nazirite
Numbers6:12 He is to rededicate his time of consecration to the LORD and to bring a year-old male lamb as a restitution offering. But do not count the previous period
Numbers6:13 This is the law of the Nazirite: On the day his time of consecration is completed
Numbers6:14 He is to present an offering to the LORD of one unblemished year-old male lamb as a burnt offering
Numbers6:15 along with their grain offerings and drink offerings
Numbers6:16 The priest is to present [these] before the LORD and sacrifice the Nazirite's sin offering and burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 He will also offer the ram as a fellowship sacrifice to the LORD
Numbers6:18 The Nazirite is to shave his consecrated head at the entrance to the tent of meeting
Numbers6:19 The priest is to take the boiled shoulder from the ram
Numbers6:20 The priest is to wave them as a presentation offering before the LORD. It is a holy portion for the priest
Numbers6:21 This is the ritual of the Nazirite who vows his offering to the LORD for his consecration
Numbers6:22 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers6:23 Tell Aaron and his sons how you are to bless the Israelites. Say to them:
Numbers6:24 The LORD bless you and protect you;
Numbers6:25 the LORD make His face shine on you
Numbers6:26 the LORD look with favor on you and give you peace.
Numbers6:27 In this way they will put My name on the Israelites
Numbers7:1 On the day Moses finished setting up the tabernacle
Numbers7:2 the leaders of Israel
Numbers7:3 They brought as their offering before the LORD six covered carts and 12 oxen
Numbers7:4 The LORD said to Moses
Numbers7:5 Accept [these] from them to be used in the work of the tent of meeting
Numbers7:6 So Moses took the carts and oxen and gave them to the Levites.
Numbers7:7 He gave the Gershonites two carts and four oxen corresponding to their service
Numbers7:8 and gave the Merarites four carts and eight oxen corresponding to their service
Numbers7:9 But he did not give [any] to the Kohathites
Numbers7:10 The leaders also presented the dedication gift for the altar when it was anointed. The leaders presented their offerings in front of the altar.
Numbers7:11 The LORD told Moses
Numbers7:12 The one who presented his offering on the first day was Nahshon son of Amminadab from the tribe of Judah.
Numbers7:13 His offering was one silver dish weighing three and a quarter pounds and one silver basin weighing one and three-quarter pounds
Numbers7:14 one gold bowl weighing four ounces
Numbers7:15 one young bull
Numbers7:16 one male goat for a sin offering;
Numbers7:17 and two bulls
Numbers7:18 On the second day Nethanel son of Zuar
Numbers7:19 As his offering
Numbers7:20 one gold bowl weighing four ounces
Numbers7:21 one young bull
Numbers7:22 one male goat for a sin offering;
Numbers7:23 and two bulls
Numbers7:24 On the third day Eliab son of Helon
Numbers7:25 His offering was one silver dish weighing three and a quarter pounds and one silver basin weighing one and three-quarter pounds
Numbers7:26 one gold bowl weighing four ounces
Numbers7:27 one young bull
Numbers7:28 one male goat for a sin offering;

Numbers7:89 When Moses entered the tent of meeting to speak with the LORD
Numbers8:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers8:2 Speak to Aaron and tell him: When you set up the lamps
Numbers8:3 So Aaron did this; he set up its lamps [to give light] in front of the lampstand just as the LORD had commanded Moses.
Numbers8:4 This is the way the lampstand was made: it was a hammered work of gold
Numbers8:5 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers8:6 Take the Levites from among the Israelites and ceremonially cleanse them.
Numbers8:7 This is what you must do to them for their purification: Sprinkle them with the purification water. Have them shave their entire bodies and wash their clothes
Numbers8:8 They are to take a young bull and its grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers8:9 Bring the Levites before the tent of meeting and assemble the entire Israelite community.
Numbers8:10 Then present the Levites before the LORD
Numbers8:11 Aaron is to present the Levites before the LORD as a presentation offering from the Israelites
Numbers8:12 Next the Levites are to lay their hands on the heads of the bulls. Sacrifice one as a sin offering and the other as a burnt offering to the Lord
Numbers8:13 You are to have the Levites stand before Aaron and his sons
Numbers8:14 In this way you are to separate the Levites from the rest of the Israelites so that the Levites will belong to Me.
Numbers8:15 After that the Levites may come to serve [at] the tent of meeting
Numbers8:16 For they have been exclusively assigned to Me from the Israelites. I have taken them for Myself in place of all who come first from the womb
Numbers8:17 For every firstborn among the Israelites is Mine
Numbers8:18 But I have taken the Levites in place of every firstborn among the Israelites.
Numbers8:19 From the Israelites
Numbers8:20 Moses
Numbers8:21 The Levites purified themselves and washed their clothes; then Aaron presented them before the LORD as a presentation offering. Aaron also made atonement for them to ceremonially cleanse them.
Numbers8:22 After that
Numbers8:23 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers8:24 In regard to the Levites: From 25 years old or more
Numbers8:25 But at 50 years old he is to retire from his service in the work and no longer serve.
Numbers8:26 He may assist his brothers to fulfill responsibilities at the tent of meeting
Numbers9:1 In the first month of the second year after their departure from the land of Egypt

Numbers9:5 and they observed it in the first month on the fourteenth day at twilight in the Wilderness of Sinai. The Israelites did everything as the LORD had commanded Moses.

Numbers11:1 Now the people began complaining openly before the LORD about hardship. When the LORD heard
Numbers11:2 Then the people cried out to Moses
Numbers11:3 So that place was named Taberah
Numbers11:4 Contemptible people among them had a strong craving [for other food]. The Israelites cried again and said

Numbers11:6 But now our appetite is gone; there's nothing to look at but this manna!
Numbers11:7 The manna resembled coriander seed
Numbers11:8 The people walked around and gathered [it]. They ground [it] on a pair of grinding stones or crushed [it] in a mortar
Numbers11:9 When the dew fell on the camp at night
Numbers11:10 Moses heard the people
Numbers11:11 So Moses asked the LORD
Numbers11:12 Did I conceive all these people? Did I give them birth so You should tell me
Numbers11:13 Where can I get meat to give all these people? For they are crying to me: 'Give us meat to eat!'
Numbers11:14 I can't carry all these people by myself. They are too much for me.
Numbers11:15 If You are going to treat me like this

Numbers11:19 You will eat

Numbers11:22 If flocks and herds were slaughtered for them
