Numbers11:32 The people were up all that day and night and all the next day gathering the quail-- the one who took the least gathered 33 bushels-- and they spread them out all around the camp.
Numbers11:33 While the meat was still between their teeth
Numbers11:34 So they named that place Kibroth-hattaavah

Numbers13:1 The LORD spoke to Moses:
Numbers13:2 Send men to scout out the land of Canaan I am giving to the Israelites. Send one man who is a leader among them from each of their ancestral tribes.
Numbers13:3 Moses sent them from the Wilderness of Paran at the LORD's command. All the men were leaders in Israel.
Numbers13:4 These were their names: Shammua son of Zaccur from the tribe of Reuben;
Numbers13:5 Shaphat son of Hori from the tribe of Simeon;
Numbers13:6 Caleb son of Jephunneh from the tribe of Judah;
Numbers13:7 Igal son of Joseph from the tribe of Issachar;
Numbers13:8 Hoshea son of Nun from the tribe of Ephraim;
Numbers13:9 Palti son of Raphu from the tribe of Benjamin;
Numbers13:10 Gaddiel son of Sodi from the tribe of Zebulun;
Numbers13:11 Gaddi son of Susi from the tribe of Manasseh (from the tribe of Joseph);
Numbers13:12 Ammiel son of Gemalli from the tribe of Dan;
Numbers13:13 Sethur son of Michael from the tribe of Asher;
Numbers13:14 Nahbi son of Vophsi from the tribe of Naphtali;
Numbers13:15 Geuel son of Machi from the tribe of Gad.
Numbers13:16 These were the names of the men Moses sent to scout out the land
Numbers13:17 When Moses sent them to scout out the land of Canaan
Numbers13:18 See what the land is like
Numbers13:19 Is the land they live in good or bad? Are the cities they live in encampments or fortifications?
Numbers13:20 Is the land fertile or unproductive? Are there trees in it or not? Be courageous. Bring back some fruit from the land. It was the season for the first ripe grapes.
Numbers13:21 So they went up and scouted out the land from the Wilderness of Zin as far as Rehob near the entrance to Hamath.
Numbers13:22 They went up through the Negev and came to Hebron
Numbers13:23 When they came to the Valley of Eshcol
Numbers13:24 That place was called the Valley of Eshcol because of the cluster [of grapes] the Israelites cut there.
Numbers13:25 At the end of 40 days they returned from scouting out the land.
Numbers13:26 The men went back to Moses
Numbers13:27 They reported to Moses: We went into the land where you sent us. Indeed it is flowing with milk and honey
Numbers13:28 However
Numbers13:29 The Amalekites are living in the land of the Negev; the Hittites

Numbers13:31 But the men who had gone up with him responded
Numbers13:32 So they gave a negative report to the Israelites about the land they had scouted: The land we passed through to explore is one that devours its inhabitants
Numbers13:33 We even saw the Nephilim there. (The offspring of Anak were descended from the Nephilim.) To ourselves we seemed like grasshoppers
Numbers14:1 Then the whole community broke into loud cries
Numbers14:2 All the Israelites complained about Moses and Aaron
Numbers14:3 Why is the LORD bringing us into this land to die by the sword? Our wives and little children will become plunder. Wouldn't it be better for us to go back to Egypt?
Numbers14:4 So they said to one another
Numbers14:5 Then Moses and Aaron fell down with their faces [to the ground] in front of the whole assembly of the Israelite community.
Numbers14:6 Joshua son of Nun and Caleb son of Jephunneh
Numbers14:7 and said to the entire Israelite community: The land we passed through and explored is an extremely good land.
Numbers14:8 If the LORD is pleased with us
Numbers14:9 Only don't rebel against the LORD

Numbers14:11 The LORD said to Moses

Numbers14:17 So now
Numbers14:18 The LORD is slow to anger and rich in faithful love
Numbers14:19 Please pardon the wrongdoing of this people in keeping with the greatness of Your faithful love
Numbers14:20 The LORD responded

Numbers14:22 none of the men who have seen My glory and the signs I performed in Egypt and in the wilderness
Numbers14:23 will ever see the land I swore to [give] their fathers. None of those who have despised Me will see it.
Numbers14:24 But since My servant Caleb has a different spirit and has followed Me completely
Numbers14:25 Since the Amalekites and Canaanites are living in the lowlands
Numbers14:26 Then the LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron:
Numbers14:27 How long [must I endure] this evil community that keeps complaining about Me? I have heard the Israelites' complaints that they make against Me.
Numbers14:28 Tell them: As surely as I live
Numbers14:29 Your corpses will fall in this wilderness-- all of you who were registered [in the census]
Numbers14:30 I swear that none of you will enter the land I promised to settle you in
Numbers14:31 I will bring your children whom you said would become plunder into the land you rejected
Numbers14:32 But as for you
Numbers14:33 Your children will be shepherds in the wilderness for 40 years and bear the penalty for your acts of unfaithfulness until all your corpses lie [scattered] in the wilderness.
Numbers14:34 You will bear the consequences of your sins 40 years based on the number of the 40 days that you scouted the land

Numbers15:1 The LORD instructed Moses:
Numbers15:2 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: When you enter the land I am giving you to settle in
Numbers15:3 and you make a fire offering to the LORD from the herd or flock-- either a burnt offering or a sacrifice
Numbers15:4 the one presenting his offering to the LORD must also present a grain offering of two quarts of fine flour mixed with a quart of oil.
Numbers15:5 Prepare a quart of wine as a drink offering with the burnt offering or sacrifice of each lamb.
Numbers15:6 If you prepare a grain offering with a ram
Numbers15:7 Also present a third of a gallon of wine for a drink offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers15:8 If you prepare a young bull as a burnt offering or as a sacrifice
Numbers15:9 a grain offering of six quarts of fine flour mixed with two quarts of oil must be presented with the bull.
Numbers15:10 Also present two quarts of wine as a drink offering. It is a fire offering of pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers15:11 This is to be done for each ox
Numbers15:12 This is how you must prepare each of them
Numbers15:13 Every Israelite is to prepare these things in this way when he presents a fire offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD.
Numbers15:14 When a foreigner resides with you or someone else is among you and wants to prepare a fire offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD
Numbers15:15 The assembly is to have the same statute for both you and the foreign resident as a permanent statute throughout your generations. You and the foreigner will be alike before the LORD.
Numbers15:16 The same law and the same ordinance will apply to both you and the foreigner who resides with you.
Numbers15:17 The LORD instructed Moses:
Numbers15:18 Speak to the Israelites and tell them: After you enter the land where I am bringing you
Numbers15:19 you are to offer a contribution to the LORD when you eat from the food of the land.
Numbers15:20 You are to offer a loaf from your first batch of dough as a contribution; offer it just like a contribution from the threshing floor.
Numbers15:21 Throughout your generations
Numbers15:22 When you sin unintentionally and do not obey all these commands that the LORD spoke to Moses--
Numbers15:23 all that the LORD has commanded you through Moses
Numbers15:24 and if it was done unintentionally without the community's awareness
Numbers15:25 The priest must then make atonement for the entire Israelite community so that they may be forgiven
Numbers15:26 The entire Israelite community and the foreigner who resides among them will be forgiven
Numbers15:27 If one person sins unintentionally
Numbers15:28 The priest must then make atonement before the LORD on behalf of the person who acts in error sinning unintentionally
Numbers15:29 You are to have the same law for the person who acts in error
Numbers15:30 But the person who acts defiantly
Numbers15:31 He will certainly be cut off
Numbers15:32 While the Israelites were in the wilderness
Numbers15:33 Those who found him gathering wood brought him to Moses
Numbers15:34 They placed him in custody
Numbers15:35 Then the LORD told Moses
Numbers15:36 So the entire community brought him outside the camp and stoned him to death

Numbers15:38 Speak to the Israelites and tell them that throughout their generations they are to make tassels for the corners of their garments
Numbers15:39 These will serve as tassels for you to look at
Numbers15:40 This way you will remember and obey all My commands and be holy to your God.
Numbers15:41 I am the LORD your God who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God; I am the LORD your God.

Numbers18:1 The LORD said to Aaron

Numbers18:5 You are to guard the sanctuary and the altar so that wrath may not fall on the Israelites again.

Numbers18:7 But you and your sons will carry out your priestly responsibilities for everything concerning the altar and for what is inside the veil
Numbers18:8 Then the LORD spoke to Aaron
Numbers18:9 A portion of the holiest offerings [kept] from the fire will be yours; every one of their offerings that they give Me
Numbers18:10 You are to eat it as a most holy offering. Every male may eat it; it is to be holy to you.
Numbers18:11 The contribution of their gifts also belongs to you. I have given all the Israelites' presentation offerings to you and to your sons and daughters as a perpetual statute. Every ceremonially clean person in your house may eat it.
Numbers18:12 I am giving you all the best of the fresh olive oil
Numbers18:13 The firstfruits of all that is in their land
Numbers18:14 Everything in Israel that is permanently dedicated [to the LORD] belongs to you.
Numbers18:15 The firstborn of every living thing
Numbers18:16 You will pay the redemption price for a month-old male according to your valuation: five shekels of silver by the standard sanctuary shekel
Numbers18:17 However
Numbers18:18 But their meat belongs to you. It belongs to you like the breast of the presentation offering and the right thigh.

Numbers18:20 The LORD told Aaron
Numbers18:21 Look

Numbers18:23 The Levites will do the work of the tent of meeting
Numbers18:24 For I have given them the tenth that the Israelites present to the LORD as a contribution for [their] inheritance. That is why I told them that they would not receive an inheritance among the Israelites.
Numbers18:25 The LORD instructed Moses
Numbers18:26 Speak to the Levites and tell them: When you receive from the Israelites the tenth that I have given you as your inheritance
Numbers18:27 Your offering will be credited to you as if [it were your] grain from the threshing floor or the full harvest from the winepress.
Numbers18:28 You are to present an offering to the LORD from every tenth you receive from the Israelites. Give some of it to Aaron the priest as an offering to the LORD.
Numbers18:29 You must present the entire offering due the LORD from all your gifts. The best part of the tenth is to be consecrated.
Numbers18:30 Tell them further: Once you have presented the best part of the tenth
Numbers18:31 then you and your household may eat it anywhere. It is your wage in return for your work at the tent of meeting.
Numbers18:32 You will not incur guilt because of it once you have presented the best part of it
Numbers19:1 The LORD spoke to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 This is the legal statute that the LORD has commanded: Instruct the Israelites to bring you an unblemished red cow that has no defect and has never been yoked.
Numbers19:3 Give it to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 Eleazar the priest is to take some of its blood with his finger and sprinkle it seven times toward the front of the tent of meeting.
Numbers19:5 The cow must be burned in his sight. Its hide
Numbers19:6 The priest is to take cedar wood
Numbers19:7 Then the priest must wash his clothes and bathe his body in water; after that he may enter the camp
Numbers19:8 The one who burned the cow must also wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:9 A man who is clean is to gather up the cow's ashes and deposit them outside the camp in a ceremonially clean place. The ashes must be kept by the Israelite community for [preparing] the water [to remove] impurity; it is a sin offering.
Numbers19:10 Then the one who gathers up the cow's ashes must wash his clothes
Numbers19:11 The person who touches any human corpse will be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 He is to purify himself with the water on the third day and the seventh day; then he will be clean. But if he does not purify himself on the third and seventh days
Numbers19:13 Anyone who touches a body of a person who has died
Numbers19:14 This is the law when a person dies in a tent: everyone who enters the tent and everyone who is [already] in the tent will be unclean for seven days
Numbers19:15 and any open container without a lid tied on it is unclean.
Numbers19:16 Anyone in the open field who touches a person who has been killed by the sword or has died
Numbers19:17 For [the purification of] the unclean person
Numbers19:18 A person who is clean is to take hyssop
Numbers19:19 The one who is clean is to sprinkle the unclean person on the third day and the seventh day. After he purifies the unclean person on the seventh day
Numbers19:20 But a person who is unclean and does not purify himself
Numbers19:21 This is a perpetual statute for them. The person who sprinkles the water for impurity is to wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 Anything the unclean person touches will become unclean

Numbers28:1 The LORD spoke to Moses
Numbers28:2 Command the Israelites and say to them: Be sure to present to Me at its appointed time My offering and My food as My fire offering
Numbers28:3 And say to them: This is the fire offering you are to present to the LORD: Each day [present] two unblemished year-old male lambs as a regular burnt offering.
Numbers28:4 Offer one lamb in the morning and the other lamb at twilight
Numbers28:5 along with two quarts of fine flour for a grain offering mixed with a quart of beaten olive oil.
Numbers28:6 It is a regular burnt offering established at Mount Sinai for a pleasing aroma
Numbers28:7 The drink offering is to be a quart with each lamb. Pour out the offering of beer to the LORD in the sanctuary area.
Numbers28:8 Offer the second lamb at twilight
Numbers28:9 On the Sabbath day [present] two unblemished year-old male lambs
Numbers28:10 It is the burnt offering for every Sabbath
Numbers28:11 At the beginning of each of your months present a burnt offering to the LORD: two young bulls
Numbers28:12 with six quarts of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for each bull
Numbers28:13 and two quarts of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering for each lamb. It is a burnt offering
Numbers28:14 Their drink offerings are to be two quarts of wine with each bull
Numbers28:15 And one male goat is to be offered as a sin offering to the Lord
Numbers28:16 The Passover to the LORD comes in the first month
Numbers28:17 On the fifteenth day of this month there will be a festival; unleavened bread is to be eaten for seven days.
Numbers28:18 On the first day there is to be a sacred assembly; you are not to do any daily work.
Numbers28:19 Present a fire offering
Numbers28:20 The grain offering with them is to be of fine flour mixed with oil; offer six quarts with each bull and four quarts with the ram.
Numbers28:21 Offer two quarts with each of the seven lambs
Numbers28:22 and one male goat for a sin offering to make atonement for yourselves.
Numbers28:23 Offer these with the morning burnt offering that is part of the regular burnt offering.
Numbers28:24 You are to offer the same food each day for seven days as a fire offering
Numbers28:25 On the seventh day you are to hold a sacred assembly; you are not to do any daily work.
Numbers28:26 On the day of firstfruits
Numbers28:27 Present a burnt offering for a pleasing aroma to the LORD: two young bulls
Numbers28:28 with their grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers28:29 and two quarts with each of the seven lambs
Numbers28:30 and one male goat to make atonement for yourselves.
Numbers28:31 Offer [them] with their drink offerings in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering. Your animals are to be unblemished.

Numbers29:2 Offer a burnt offering as a pleasing aroma to the LORD: one young bull
Numbers29:3 with their grain offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers29:4 and two quarts with each of the seven male lambs.
Numbers29:5 Also [offer] one male goat as a sin offering to make atonement for yourselves.

Numbers30:1 Moses told the leaders of the Israelite tribes
Numbers30:2 When a man makes a vow to the LORD or swears an oath to put himself under an obligation
Numbers30:3 When a woman in her father's house during her youth makes a vow to the LORD or puts [herself] under an obligation
Numbers30:4 and her father hears about her vow or the obligation she put herself under
Numbers30:5 But if her father prohibits her on the day he hears [about it]
Numbers30:6 If a woman marries while her vows or the rash commitment she herself made are binding
Numbers30:7 and her husband hears [about it] and says nothing to her when he finds out
Numbers30:8 But if her husband prohibits her when he hears [about it]
Numbers30:9 Any vow a widow or divorc�e put herself under is binding on her.
Numbers30:10 If a woman in her husband's house has made a vow or put herself under an obligation with an oath
Numbers30:11 and her husband hears [about it]
Numbers30:12 But if her husband cancels them on the day he hears [about it]
Numbers30:13 Her husband may confirm or cancel any vow or any sworn obligation to deny herself.

Numbers35:23 or drops a stone without looking that could kill a person and he dies

Numbers35:25 The assembly is to protect the one who kills someone from the hand of the avenger of blood. Then the assembly will return him to the city of refuge he fled to
Numbers35:26 If the one who kills someone ever goes outside the border of the city of refuge he fled to
Numbers35:27 and the avenger of blood finds him outside the border of his city of refuge and kills him
Numbers35:28 for the one who killed a person was supposed to live in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest. Only after the death of the high priest may the one who has killed a person return to the land he possesses.

Numbers35:30 If anyone kills a person
Numbers35:31 You are not to accept a ransom for the life of a murderer who is guilty of killing someone; he must be put to death.

Deuteronomy4:22 I won't be crossing the Jordan because I am going to die in this land. But you are about to cross over and take possession of this good land.

Deuteronomy4:24 For the LORD your God is a consuming fire

Deuteronomy4:28 There you will worship man-made gods of wood and stone
Deuteronomy4:29 But from there

Deuteronomy4:31 He will not leave you
Deuteronomy4:32 Indeed
Deuteronomy4:33 Has a people ever heard God's voice speaking from the fire as you have

Deuteronomy4:36 He let you hear His voice from heaven to instruct you. He showed you His great fire on earth
Deuteronomy4:37 Because He loved your fathers
Deuteronomy4:38 to drive out before you nations greater and stronger than you and to bring you in and give you their land as an inheritance
Deuteronomy4:39 Today
Deuteronomy4:40 Keep His statutes and commands

Deuteronomy28:32 Your sons and daughters will be given to another people

Deuteronomy28:34 You will be driven mad by what you see.
Deuteronomy28:35 The LORD will afflict you on your knees and thighs with painful and incurable boils from the sole of your foot to the top of your head.
Deuteronomy28:36 The LORD will bring you and your king that you have appointed to a nation neither you nor your fathers have known

Deuteronomy28:38 You will sow much seed in the field but harvest little

Deuteronomy28:40 You will have olives trees throughout your territory but not anoint yourself with oil
Deuteronomy28:41 You will father sons and daughters
Deuteronomy28:42 Whirring insects will take possession of all your trees and your land's produce.

Deuteronomy28:44 He will lend to you
Deuteronomy28:45 All these curses will come
Deuteronomy28:46 These curses will be a sign and a wonder against you and your descendants forever.

Deuteronomy32:1 Pay attention
Deuteronomy32:2 Let my teaching fall like rain and my word settle like dew
Deuteronomy32:3 For I will proclaim the LORD's name. Declare the greatness of our God!
Deuteronomy32:4 The Rock-- His work is perfect; all His ways are entirely just. A faithful God
Deuteronomy32:5 His people have acted corruptly toward Him; this is their defect-- they are not His children but a devious and crooked generation.
Deuteronomy32:6 Is this how you repay the LORD
Deuteronomy32:7 Remember the days of old; consider the years long past. Ask your father
Deuteronomy32:8 When the Most High gave the nations their inheritance and divided the human race
Deuteronomy32:9 But the LORD's portion is His people
Deuteronomy32:10 He found him in a desolate land
Deuteronomy32:11 He watches over His nest like an eagle and hovers over His young; He spreads His wings
Deuteronomy32:12 The LORD alone led him
Deuteronomy32:13 He made him ride on the heights of the land and eat the produce of the field. He nourished him with honey from the rock and oil from flintlike rock
Deuteronomy32:14 cream from the herd and milk from the flock
Deuteronomy32:15 Then Jeshurun became fat and rebelled-- you became fat
Deuteronomy32:16 They provoked His jealousy with foreign gods; they enraged Him with detestable practices.
Deuteronomy32:17 They sacrificed to demons
Deuteronomy32:18 You ignored the Rock who gave you birth; you forgot the God who brought you forth.
Deuteronomy32:19 When the LORD saw [this]
Deuteronomy32:20 He said: I will hide My face from them; I will see what will become of them
Deuteronomy32:21 They have provoked My jealousy with [their] so-called gods; they have enraged Me with their worthless idols. So I will provoke their jealousy with an inferior people; I will enrage them with a foolish nation.
Deuteronomy32:22 For fire has been kindled because of My anger and burns to the depths of Sheol; it devours the land and its produce
Deuteronomy32:23 I will pile disasters on them; I will use up My arrows against them.
Deuteronomy32:24 They will be weak from hunger
Deuteronomy32:25 Outside
Deuteronomy32:26 I would have said: I will cut them to pieces and blot out the memory of them from mankind
Deuteronomy32:27 if I had not feared insult from the enemy
Deuteronomy32:28 Israel is a nation lacking sense with no understanding at all.
Deuteronomy32:29 If only they were wise
Deuteronomy32:30 How could one man pursue a thousand
Deuteronomy32:31 But their rock is not like our Rock; even our enemies concede.
Deuteronomy32:32 For their vine is from the vine of Sodom and from the fields of Gomorrah. Their grapes are poisonous; their clusters are bitter.
Deuteronomy32:33 Their wine is serpents' venom
Deuteronomy32:34 Is it not stored up with Me
Deuteronomy32:35 Vengeance belongs to Me; I will repay. In time their foot will slip
Deuteronomy32:36 The LORD will indeed vindicate His people and have compassion on His servants when He sees that [their] strength is gone and no one is left-- slave or free.
Deuteronomy32:37 He will say: Where are their gods
Deuteronomy32:38 Who ate the fat of their sacrifices and drank the wine of their drink offerings? Let them rise up and help you; let it be a shelter for you.
Deuteronomy32:39 See now that I alone am He; there is no God but Me. I bring death and I give life; I wound and I heal. No one can rescue [anyone] from My hand.
Deuteronomy32:40 I raise My hand to heaven and declare: As surely as I live forever
Deuteronomy32:41 when I sharpen My flashing sword
Deuteronomy32:42 I will make My arrows drunk with blood while My sword devours flesh-- the blood of the slain and the captives
Deuteronomy32:43 Rejoice
Deuteronomy32:44 Moses came with Joshua son of Nun and recited all the words of this song in the presence of the people.
Deuteronomy32:45 After Moses finished reciting all these words to all Israel
Deuteronomy32:46 he said to them
Deuteronomy32:47 For they are not meaningless words to you but they are your life
Deuteronomy32:48 On that same day the LORD spoke to Moses

Deuteronomy32:50 Then you will die on the mountain that you go up
Deuteronomy32:51 For [both of] you broke faith with Me among the Israelites at the waters of Meribath-kadesh in the Wilderness of Zin by failing to treat Me as holy in their presence.
Deuteronomy32:52 Although you will view the land from a distance

Deuteronomy33:21 He chose the best [part] for himself
Deuteronomy33:22 He said about Dan: Dan is a young lion
Deuteronomy33:23 He said about Naphtali: Naphtali
Deuteronomy33:24 He said about Asher: May Asher be the most blessed of the sons; may he be the most favored among his brothers and dip his foot in [olive] oil.

Joshua1:4 Your territory will be from the wilderness and Lebanon to the great Euphrates River-- all the land of the Hittites-- and west to the Mediterranean Sea.

Joshua1:6 Be strong and courageous
Joshua1:7 Above all

2Samuel22:19 They confronted me in the day of my distress
2Samuel22:20 He brought me out to a wide-open place; He rescued me because He delighted in me.

2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the LORD and have not turned from my God to wickedness.

2Samuel22:24 I was blameless before Him and kept myself from sinning.

2Samuel22:33 God is my strong refuge; He makes my way perfect.
2Samuel22:34 He makes my feet like [the feet of] a deer and sets me securely on the heights.
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for war; my arms can bend a bow of bronze.

2Samuel22:37 You widen [a place] beneath me for my steps

2Samuel22:50 Therefore I will praise You

1Kings6:3 The portico in front of the temple sanctuary was 30 feet long extending across the temple's width
1Kings6:4 He also made windows with beveled frames for the temple.
1Kings6:5 He then built a chambered structure along the temple wall
1Kings6:6 The lowest chamber was seven and a half feet wide
1Kings6:7 The temple's construction used finished stones cut at the quarry so that no hammer

1Kings7:1 Solomon completed his entire palace-complex after 13 years of construction.
1Kings7:2 He built the House of the Forest of Lebanon. It was 150 feet long
1Kings7:3 It was paneled above with cedar at the top of the chambers that [rested] on 45 pillars
1Kings7:4 There were three rows of window frames
1Kings7:5 All the doors and doorposts had rectangular frames
1Kings7:6 He made the hall of pillars 75 feet long and 45 feet wide. A portico was in front of the pillars
1Kings7:7 He made the Hall of the Throne where he would judge-- the Hall of Judgment. It was paneled with cedar from the floor to the rafters.
1Kings7:8 Solomon's own palace where he would live
1Kings7:9 All of these [buildings] were of costly stones
1Kings7:10 The foundation was made of large
1Kings7:11 Above were also costly stones
1Kings7:12 Around the great courtyard
1Kings7:13 King Solomon had Hiram brought from Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was a widow's son from the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 He cast two [hollow] bronze pillars: each 27 feet high and 18 feet in circumference.
1Kings7:16 He also made two capitals of cast bronze to set on top of the pillars; seven and a half feet was the height of the first capital
1Kings7:17 The capitals on top of the pillars had gratings of latticework
1Kings7:18 He made the pillars with two encircling rows of pomegranates on the one grating to cover the capital on top; he did the same for the second capital.
1Kings7:19 And the capitals on top of the pillars in the portico were shaped like lilies
1Kings7:20 The capitals on the two pillars were also immediately above the rounded surface next to the grating
1Kings7:21 He set up the pillars at the portico of the sanctuary: he set up the right pillar and named it Jachin; then he set up the left pillar and named it Boaz.
1Kings7:22 The tops of the pillars were shaped like lilies. Then the work of the pillars was completed.
1Kings7:23 He made the cast [metal] reservoir
1Kings7:24 [Ornamental] gourds encircled it below the brim
1Kings7:25 It stood on 12 oxen
1Kings7:26 The reservoir was three inches thick
1Kings7:27 Then he made 10 bronze water carts. Each water cart was six feet long
1Kings7:28 This was the design of the carts: They had frames; the frames were between the cross-pieces
1Kings7:29 and on the frames between the cross-pieces were lions
1Kings7:30 Each cart had four bronze wheels with bronze axles. Underneath the four corners of the basin were cast supports
1Kings7:31 And the water cart's opening inside the crown on top was 18 inches wide. The opening was round
1Kings7:32 There were four wheels under the frames
1Kings7:33 The wheels' design was similar to that of chariot wheels: their axles
1Kings7:34 Four supports were at the four corners of each water cart; each support was one piece with the water cart.
1Kings7:35 At the top of the cart was a band nine inches high encircling it; also
1Kings7:36 He engraved cherubim
1Kings7:37 In this way he made the 10 water carts using the same casting
1Kings7:38 Then he made 10 bronze basins-- each basin holding 220 gallons and each was six feet wide-- one basin for each of the 10 water carts.
1Kings7:39 He set five water carts on the right side of the temple and five on the left side. He put the reservoir near the right side of the temple toward the southeast.
1Kings7:40 Then Hiram made the basins
1Kings7:41 two pillars; bowls for the capitals that were on top of the two pillars; the two gratings for covering both bowls of the capitals that were on top of the pillars;
1Kings7:42 the 400 pomegranates for the two gratings (two rows of pomegranates for each grating covering both capitals' bowls on top of the pillars);
1Kings7:43 the 10 water carts; the 10 basins on the water carts;
1Kings7:44 the reservoir; the 12 oxen underneath the reservoir;
1Kings7:45 and the pots
1Kings7:46 The king had them cast in clay molds in the Jordan Valley between Succoth and Zarethan.
1Kings7:47 Solomon left all the utensils unweighed because there were so many; the weight of the bronze was not determined.
1Kings7:48 Solomon also made all the equipment in the LORD's temple: the gold altar; the gold table that the bread of the Presence was placed on;
1Kings7:49 the pure gold lampstands in front of the inner sanctuary
1Kings7:50 the pure gold ceremonial bowls
1Kings7:51 So all the work King Solomon did in the LORD's temple was completed. Then Solomon brought in the consecrated things of his father David-- the silver
1Kings8:1 At that time Solomon assembled the elders of Israel

1Kings8:11 and because of the cloud

1Kings8:17 It was in the desire of my father David to build a temple for the name of the LORD God of Israel.

1Kings8:19 Yet you are not the one to build it; instead

1Kings8:21 I have provided a place there for the ark
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood before the altar of the LORD in front of the entire congregation of Israel and spread out his hands toward heaven.
1Kings8:23 He said: LORD God of Israel
1Kings8:24 You have kept what You promised to Your servant
1Kings8:25 Therefore
1Kings8:26 Now LORD God of Israel
1Kings8:27 But will God indeed live on earth? Even heaven
1Kings8:28 Listen to Your servant's prayer and his petition
1Kings8:29 so that Your eyes may watch over this temple night and day
1Kings8:30 Hear the petition of Your servant and Your people Israel
1Kings8:31 When a man sins against his neighbor and is forced to take an oath
1Kings8:32 may You hear in heaven and act. May You judge Your servants
1Kings8:33 When Your people Israel are defeated before an enemy
1Kings8:34 may You hear in heaven and forgive the sin of Your people Israel. May You restore them to the land You gave their ancestors.
1Kings8:35 When the skies are shut and there is no rain
1Kings8:36 may You hear in heaven and forgive the sin of Your servants and Your people Israel
1Kings8:37 When there is famine on the earth
1Kings8:38 whatever prayer or petition anyone from Your people Israel might have-- each man knowing his own afflictions and spreading out his hands toward this temple--
1Kings8:39 may You hear in heaven
1Kings8:40 so that they may fear You all the days they live on the land You gave our ancestors.
1Kings8:41 Even for the foreigner who is not of Your people Israel but has come from a distant land because of Your name--
1Kings8:42 for they will hear of Your great name
1Kings8:43 may You hear in heaven
1Kings8:44 When Your people go out to fight against their enemies
1Kings8:45 may You hear their prayer and petition in heaven and uphold their cause.
1Kings8:46 When they sin against You-- for there is no one who does not sin-- and You are angry with them and hand them over to the enemy
1Kings8:47 and when they come to their senses in the land where they were deported and repent and petition You in their captors' land: We have sinned and done wrong; we have been wicked
1Kings8:48 and when they return to You with their whole mind and heart in the land of their enemies who took them captive
1Kings8:49 may You hear in heaven
1Kings8:50 May You forgive Your people who sinned against You and all their rebellions against You
1Kings8:51 For they are Your people and Your inheritance; You brought them out of Egypt
1Kings8:52 May Your eyes be open to Your servant's petition and to the petition of Your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For You
1Kings8:54 When Solomon finished praying this entire prayer and petition to the LORD
1Kings8:55 and he stood and blessed the whole congregation of Israel with a loud voice:
1Kings8:56 May the LORD be praised! He has given rest to His people Israel according to all He has said. Not one of all the good promises He made through His servant Moses has failed.
1Kings8:57 May the LORD our God be with us as He was with our ancestors. May He not abandon us or leave us.
1Kings8:58 May He incline our hearts toward Him to walk in all His ways and to keep His commands
1Kings8:59 May my words I have made my petition with before the LORD be near the LORD our God day and night
1Kings8:60 and so that all the peoples of the earth may know that the LORD is God. There is no other!
1Kings8:61 Let your heart be completely devoted to the LORD our God to walk in His ordinances and to keep His commands
1Kings8:62 The king and all Israel with him were offering sacrifices in the LORD's presence.

1Kings8:64 On the same day
1Kings8:65 Solomon and all Israel with him-- a great assembly
1Kings8:66 On the fifteenth day he sent the people away. So they blessed the king and went home to their tents rejoicing and with joyful hearts for all the goodness that the LORD had done for His servant David and for His people Israel.

1Kings15:9 In the twentieth year of Israel's King Jeroboam
1Kings15:10 he reigned 41 years in Jerusalem. His grandmother's name was Maacah daughter of Abishalom.
1Kings15:11 Asa did what was right in the LORD's eyes
1Kings15:12 He banished the male shrine prostitutes from the land and removed all of the idols that his fathers had made.
1Kings15:13 He also removed his grandmother Maacah from being queen mother because she had made an obscene image of Asherah. Asa chopped down her obscene image and burned it in the Kidron Valley.
1Kings15:14 The high places were not taken away; but Asa's heart was completely with the LORD his entire life.
1Kings15:15 He brought his father's consecrated gifts and his own consecrated gifts into the LORD's temple: silver
1Kings15:16 There was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel throughout their reigns.
1Kings15:17 Israel's King Baasha went to war against Judah. He built Ramah in order to deny anyone access to Judah's King Asa.
1Kings15:18 So Asa withdrew all the silver and gold that remained in the treasuries of the LORD's temple and the treasuries of the royal palace and put it into the hands of his servants. Then King Asa sent them to Ben-hadad son of Tabrimmon son of Hezion king of Aram who lived in Damascus
1Kings15:19 There is a treaty between me and you

1Kings15:21 When Baasha heard [about it]
1Kings15:22 Then King Asa gave a command to everyone without exception in Judah
1Kings15:23 The rest of all the events of Asa's [reign]
1Kings15:24 Then Asa rested with his fathers and was buried in the city of his forefather David. His son Jehoshaphat became king in his place.
1Kings15:25 Nadab son of Jeroboam became king over Israel in the second year of Judah's King Asa; he reigned over Israel two years.
1Kings15:26 Nadab did what was evil in the LORD's sight and followed the example of his father and the sin he had caused Israel to commit.

2Kings17:6 In the ninth year of Hoshea
2Kings17:7 [This disaster] happened because the people of Israel had sinned against the LORD their God who had brought them out of the land of Egypt from the power of Pharaoh king of Egypt and because they had worshiped other gods.
2Kings17:8 They had lived according to the customs of the nations that the LORD had dispossessed before the Israelites and the customs the kings of Israel had introduced.
2Kings17:9 The Israelites secretly did what was not right against the LORD their God. They built high places in all their towns from watchtower to fortified city.
2Kings17:10 They set up for themselves sacred pillars and Asherah poles on every high hill and under every green tree.
2Kings17:11 They burned incense on all the high places just like those nations that the LORD had driven out before them. They did evil things
2Kings17:12 They served idols
2Kings17:13 Still
2Kings17:14 But they would not listen. Instead
2Kings17:15 They rejected His statutes and His covenant He had made with their ancestors and the warnings He had given them. They pursued worthless idols and became worthless themselves
2Kings17:16 They abandoned all the commandments of the LORD their God. They made for themselves molded images-- even two calves-- and an Asherah pole. They worshiped the whole heavenly host and served Baal.
2Kings17:17 They made their sons and daughters pass through the fire and practiced divination and interpreted omens. They devoted themselves to do what was evil in the LORD's sight and provoked Him.
2Kings17:18 Therefore
2Kings17:19 Even Judah did not keep the commandments of the LORD their God but lived according to the customs Israel had introduced.
2Kings17:20 So the LORD rejected all the descendants of Israel

2Kings17:22 The Israelites persisted in all the sins that Jeroboam committed and did not turn away from them.

2Kings21:1 Manasseh was 12 years old when he became king; he reigned 55 years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hephzibah.
2Kings21:2 He did what was evil in the LORD's sight
2Kings21:3 He rebuilt the high places that his father Hezekiah had destroyed and reestablished the altars for Baal. He made an Asherah
2Kings21:4 He would build altars in the LORD's temple
2Kings21:5 He built altars to the whole heavenly host in both courtyards of the LORD's temple.
2Kings21:6 He made his son pass through the fire
2Kings21:7 Manasseh set up the carved image of Asherah he made in the temple that the LORD had spoken about to David and his son Solomon
2Kings21:8 I will never again cause the feet of the Israelites to wander from the land I gave to their ancestors if only they will be careful to do all I have commanded them-- the whole law that My servant Moses commanded them.
2Kings21:9 But they did not listen; Manasseh caused them to stray so that they did greater evil than the nations the LORD had destroyed before the Israelites.
2Kings21:10 The LORD spoke through His servants the prophets
2Kings21:11 Since Manasseh king of Judah has committed all these abominations-- greater evil than the Amorites who preceded him had done-- and by means of his idols has also caused Judah to sin
2Kings21:12 this is what the LORD God of Israel says: 'I am about to bring such disaster on Jerusalem and Judah that everyone who hears about it will shudder.
2Kings21:13 I will stretch over Jerusalem the measuring line [used on] Samaria and the mason's level [used on] the house of Ahab
2Kings21:14 I will abandon the remnant of My inheritance and hand them over to their enemies. They will become plunder and spoil to all their enemies
2Kings21:15 because they have done what is evil in My sight and have provoked Me from the day their ancestors came out of Egypt until today.'
2Kings21:16 Manasseh also shed so much innocent blood that he filled Jerusalem with it from one end to another. This was in addition to his sin he caused Judah to commit so that they did what was evil in the LORD's sight.
2Kings21:17 The rest of the events of Manasseh's [reign]
2Kings21:18 Manasseh rested with his fathers and was buried in the garden of his own house

2Kings21:21 He walked in all the ways his father had walked; he served the idols his father had served
2Kings21:22 He abandoned the LORD God of his ancestors and did not walk in the way of the LORD.
2Kings21:23 Amon's servants conspired against the king and killed him in his own house.

1Chronicles10:14 but he did not inquire of the LORD. So the LORD put him to death and turned the kingdom over to David son of Jesse.

2Chronicles14:2  Asa did what was good and right in the sight of the LORD his God.
2Chronicles14:3 He removed the pagan altars and the high places. He shattered their sacred pillars and chopped down their Asherah poles.
2Chronicles14:4 He told [the people of] Judah to seek the LORD God of their ancestors and to carry out the instruction and the command.
2Chronicles14:5 He also removed the high places and the incense altars from all the cities of Judah

2Chronicles14:14 Then they attacked all the cities around Gerar because the terror of the LORD was on them. They also plundered all the cities
2Chronicles14:15 They also attacked the tents of the herdsmen and captured many sheep and camels. Then they returned to Jerusalem.

2Chronicles36:1 Then the common people took Jehoahaz son of Josiah and made him king in Jerusalem in place of his father.
2Chronicles36:2 Jehoahaz was 23 years old when he became king; he reigned three months in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles36:3 The king of Egypt deposed him in Jerusalem and fined the land 7
2Chronicles36:4 Then [Neco] king of Egypt made Jehoahaz's brother Eliakim king over Judah and Jerusalem and changed Eliakim's name to Jehoiakim. But Neco took his brother Jehoahaz and brought him to Egypt.
2Chronicles36:5 Jehoiakim was 25 years old when he became king; he reigned 11 years in Jerusalem. He did what was evil in the sight of the LORD his God.
2Chronicles36:6 Now Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon attacked him and bound him in bronze [shackles] to take him to Babylon.
2Chronicles36:7 Also Nebuchadnezzar took some of the utensils of the LORD's temple to Babylon and put them in his temple in Babylon.
2Chronicles36:8 The rest of the deeds of Jehoiakim
2Chronicles36:9 Jehoiachin was 18 years old when he became king; he reigned three months and 10 days in Jerusalem. He did what was evil in the LORD's sight.
2Chronicles36:10 In the spring Nebuchadnezzar sent [for him] and brought him to Babylon along with the valuable utensils of the LORD's temple. Then he made Jehoiachin's brother Zedekiah king over Judah and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles36:11 Zedekiah was 21 years old when he became king; he reigned 11 years in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles36:12 He did what was evil in the sight of the LORD his God and did not humble himself before Jeremiah the prophet at the LORD's command.
2Chronicles36:13 He also rebelled against King Nebuchadnezzar who had made him swear allegiance by God. He became obstinate and hardened his heart against returning to the LORD God of Israel.
2Chronicles36:14 All the leaders of the priests and the people multiplied their unfaithful deeds

2Chronicles36:18 He took everything to Babylon-- all the articles of God's temple

Nehemiah9:1 On the twenty-fourth day of this month the Israelites assembled; they were fasting
Nehemiah9:2 Those of Israelite descent separated themselves from all foreigners
Nehemiah9:3 While they stood in their places
Nehemiah9:4 Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 Then the Levites-- Jeshua
Nehemiah9:6 You alone are the LORD. You created the heavens
Nehemiah9:7 You are the LORD God who chose Abram and brought him out of Ur of the Chaldeans
Nehemiah9:8 You found his heart faithful in Your sight
Nehemiah9:9 You saw the oppression of our ancestors in Egypt and heard their cry at the Red Sea.
Nehemiah9:10 You performed signs and wonders against Pharaoh
Nehemiah9:11 You divided the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 You led them with a pillar of cloud by day
Nehemiah9:13 You came down on Mount Sinai
Nehemiah9:14 You revealed Your holy Sabbath to them
Nehemiah9:15 You provided bread from heaven for their hunger; You brought them water from the rock for their thirst. You told them to go in and possess the land You had sworn to give them.
Nehemiah9:16 But our ancestors acted arrogantly; they became stiff-necked and did not listen to Your commands.
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to listen and did not remember Your wonders You performed among them. They became stiff-necked and appointed a leader to return to their slavery in Egypt. But You are a forgiving God
Nehemiah9:18 Even after they had cast an image of a calf for themselves and said
Nehemiah9:19 You did not abandon them in the wilderness because of Your great compassion. During the day the pillar of cloud never turned away from them
Nehemiah9:20 You sent Your good Spirit to instruct them. You did not withhold Your manna from their mouths
Nehemiah9:21 You provided for them in the wilderness 40 years and they lacked nothing. Their clothes did not wear out
Nehemiah9:22 You gave them kingdoms and peoples and assigned them to be a boundary. They took possession of the land of Sihon king of Heshbon and of the land of Og king of Bashan.
Nehemiah9:23 You multiplied their descendants like the stars of heaven and brought them to the land You told their ancestors to go in and take possession [of it].
Nehemiah9:24 So their descendants went in and possessed the land: You subdued the Canaanites who inhabited the land before them and handed their kings and the surrounding peoples over to them
Nehemiah9:25 They captured fortified cities and fertile land and took possession of well-supplied houses
Nehemiah9:26 But they were disobedient and rebelled against You. They flung Your law behind their backs and killed Your prophets who warned them to turn them back to You. They committed terrible blasphemies.
Nehemiah9:27 So You handed them over to their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 But as soon as they had relief
Nehemiah9:29 You warned them to turn back to Your law
Nehemiah9:30 You were patient with them for many years
Nehemiah9:31 However
Nehemiah9:32 So now
Nehemiah9:33 You are righteous concerning all that has come on us
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 When they were in their kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 Here we are today
Nehemiah9:37 Its abundant harvest goes to the kings You have set over us
Nehemiah9:38  In view of all this

Esther1:1 These events took place during the days of Ahasuerus
Esther1:2 In those days King Ahasuerus reigned from his royal throne in the fortress at Susa.
Esther1:3 He held a feast in the third year of his reign for all his officials and staff
Esther1:4 He displayed the glorious wealth of his kingdom and the magnificent splendor of his greatness for a total of 180 days.
Esther1:5 At the end of this time
Esther1:6 White and violet linen hangings were fastened with fine white and purple linen cords to silver rods on marble columns. Gold and silver couches [were arranged] on a mosaic pavement of red feldspar
Esther1:7 Beverages were served in an array of gold goblets
Esther1:8 and no restraint was placed on the drinking. The king had ordered every wine steward in his household to serve as much as each person wanted.
Esther1:9 Queen Vashti also gave a feast for the women of King Ahasuerus' palace.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 to bring Queen Vashti before him with her royal crown. [He wanted] to show off her beauty to the people and the officials
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king's command that was delivered by his eunuchs. The king became furious and his anger burned within him.
Esther1:13 The king consulted the wise men who understood the times
Esther1:14 The most trusted ones were Carshena

Esther1:20 The decree the king issues will be heard throughout his vast kingdom

Esther2:6 He had been taken into exile from Jerusalem with the other captives when King Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon took King Jeconiah of Judah into exile.

Esther2:10 Esther did not reveal her ethnic background or her birthplace
Esther2:11 Every day Mordecai took a walk in front of the harem's courtyard to learn how Esther was doing and to see what was happening to her.

Esther2:14 She would go in the evening

Esther2:16 Esther was taken to King Ahasuerus in the royal palace in the tenth month
Esther2:17 The king loved Esther more than all the other women. She won more favor and approval from him than did any of the other virgins. He placed the royal crown on her head and made her queen in place of Vashti.
Esther2:18 The king held a great banquet for all his officials and staff. It was Esther's banquet. He freed his provinces from tax payments and gave gifts worthy of the king's bounty.

Esther9:1 The king's command and law went into effect on the thirteenth day of the twelfth month
Esther9:2 In each of King Ahasuerus' provinces the Jews assembled in their cities to attack those who intended to harm them. Not a single person could withstand them; terror of them fell on every nationality.
Esther9:3 All the officials of the provinces
Esther9:4 For Mordecai [exercised] great power in the palace

Esther9:7 including Parshandatha
Esther9:8 Poratha
Esther9:9 Parmashta

Esther9:14 The king gave the orders for this to be done
Esther9:15 The Jews in Susa assembled again on the fourteenth day of the month of Adar and killed 300 men in Susa
Esther9:16 The rest of the Jews in the royal provinces assembled
Esther9:17 [They fought] on the thirteenth day of the month of Adar and rested on the fourteenth
Esther9:18 But the Jews in Susa had assembled on the thirteenth and the fourteenth days of the month. They rested on the fifteenth day of the month
Esther9:19 This explains why the rural Jews who live in villages observe the fourteenth day of the month of Adar as [a time of] rejoicing and feasting. It is a holiday when they send gifts to one another.
Esther9:20 Mordecai recorded these events and sent letters to all the Jews in all of King Ahasuerus' provinces
Esther9:21 [He ordered] them to celebrate the fourteenth and fifteenth days of the month Adar every year
Esther9:22 because during those days the Jews got rid of their enemies. That was the month when their sorrow was turned into rejoicing and their mourning into a holiday. They were to be days of feasting
Esther9:23 So the Jews agreed to continue the practice they had begun

Esther9:31 in order to confirm these days of Purim at their proper time just as Mordecai the Jew and Queen Esther had established them and just as they had committed themselves and their descendants to the practices of fasting and lamentation.
Esther9:32 So Esther's command confirmed these customs of Purim

Job1:1 There was a man in the country of Uz named Job. He was a man of perfect integrity
Job1:2 He had seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 His estate included 7
Job1:4 His sons used to have banquets
Job1:5 Whenever a round of banqueting was over
Job1:6 One day the sons of God came to present themselves before the LORD
Job1:7 The LORD asked Satan
Job1:8 Then the LORD said to Satan
Job1:9 Satan answered the LORD
Job1:10 Haven't You placed a hedge around him
Job1:11 But stretch out Your hand and strike everything he owns
Job1:12 Very well
Job1:13 One day when Job's sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother's house
Job1:14 a messenger came to Job and reported: While the oxen were plowing and the donkeys grazing nearby
Job1:15 the Sabeans swooped down and took them away. They struck down the servants with the sword
Job1:16 He was still speaking when another [messenger] came and reported: A lightning storm struck from heaven. It burned up the sheep and the servants
Job1:17 That messenger was still speaking when [yet] another came and reported: The Chaldeans formed three bands
Job1:18 He was still speaking when another [messenger] came and reported: Your sons and daughters were eating and drinking wine in their oldest brother's house.
Job1:19 Suddenly a powerful wind swept in from the desert and struck the four corners of the house. It collapsed on the young people so that they died
Job1:20 Then Job stood up
Job1:21 saying: Naked I came from my mother's womb
Job1:22 Throughout all this Job did not sin or blame God for anything.
Job2:1 One day the sons of God came again to present themselves before the LORD
Job2:2 The LORD asked Satan
Job2:3 Then the LORD said to Satan
Job2:4 Skin for skin! Satan answered the LORD. A man will give up everything he owns in exchange for his life.
Job2:5 But stretch out Your hand and strike his flesh and bones
Job2:6 Very well
Job2:7 So Satan left the LORD's presence and infected Job with incurable boils from the sole of his foot to the top of his head.
Job2:8 Then Job took a piece of broken pottery to scrape himself while he sat among the ashes.
Job2:9 His wife said to him
Job2:10 You speak as a foolish woman speaks
Job2:11 Now when Job's three friends-- Eliphaz the Temanite
Job2:12 When they looked from a distance
Job2:13 Then they sat on the ground with him seven days and nights
Job3:1 After this Job began to speak and cursed the day he was born.
Job3:2 He said:
Job3:3 May the day I was born perish
Job3:4 If only that day had turned to darkness! May God above not care about it
Job3:5 May darkness and gloom reclaim it
Job3:6 If only darkness had taken that night away! May it not appear among the days of the year or be listed in the calendar.
Job3:7 Yes
Job3:8 Let those who curse [certain] days cast a spell on it
Job3:9 May its morning stars grow dark. May it wait for daylight but have none; may it not see the breaking of dawn.
Job3:10 For that night did not shut the doors of my [mother's] womb
Job3:11 Why was I not stillborn; [why] didn't I die as I came from the womb?
Job3:12 Why did the knees receive me
Job3:13 For then I would have laid down in peace; I would be asleep. Then I would be at rest
Job3:14 with the kings and counselors of the earth
Job3:15 or with princes who had gold
Job3:16 Or [why] was I not hidden like a miscarried child
Job3:17 There the wicked cease to make trouble
Job3:18 The captives are completely at ease; they do not hear the voice of [their] oppressor.
Job3:19 Both the small and the great are there
Job3:20 Why is light given to one burdened with grief
Job3:21 who wait for death
Job3:22 who are filled with much joy and are glad when they reach the grave?
Job3:23 [Why is life given] to a man whose path is hidden
Job3:24 I sigh when food is [put] before me
Job3:25 For the thing I feared has overtaken me
Job3:26 I cannot relax or be still; I have no rest
Job4:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
Job4:2 Should anyone try to speak with you when you are exhausted? Yet who can keep from speaking?
Job4:3 Look! You have instructed many and have strengthened weak hands.
Job4:4 Your words have steadied the one who was stumbling
Job4:5 But now that this has happened to you
Job4:6 Isn't your piety your confidence
Job4:7 Consider: who has perished when he was innocent? Where have the honest been destroyed?
Job4:8 In my experience
Job4:9 They perish at a [single] blast from God and come to an end by the breath of His nostrils.
Job4:10 The lion may roar and the fierce lion growl
Job4:11 The strong lion dies if [it catches] no prey
Job4:12 A word was brought to me in secret; my ears caught a whisper of it.
Job4:13 Among unsettling thoughts from visions in the night
Job4:14 fear and trembling came over me and made all my bones shake.
Job4:15 A wind passed by me
Job4:16 [A figure] stood there
Job4:17 Can a person be more righteous than God
Job4:18 If God puts no trust in His servants and He charges His angels with foolishness
Job4:19 how much more those who dwell in clay houses
Job4:20 They are smashed to pieces from dawn to dusk; they perish forever while no one notices.
Job4:21 Are their tent cords not pulled up? They die without wisdom.
Job5:1 Call out if you please. Will anyone answer you? Which of the holy ones will you turn to?
Job5:2 For anger kills a fool
Job5:3 I have seen a fool taking root
Job5:4 His children are far from safety. They are crushed at the [city] gate
Job5:5 The hungry consume his harvest
Job5:6 For distress does not grow out of the soil
Job5:7 But mankind is born for trouble as surely as sparks fly upward.
Job5:8 However
Job5:9 He does great and unsearchable things
Job5:10 He gives rain to the earth and sends water to the fields.
Job5:11 He sets the lowly on high
Job5:12 He frustrates the schemes of the crafty so that they achieve no success.
Job5:13 He traps the wise in their craftiness so that the plans of the deceptive are quickly brought to an end.
Job5:14 They encounter darkness by day
Job5:15 He saves the needy from their sharp words and from the clutches of the powerful.
Job5:16 So the poor have hope
Job5:17 See how happy the man is God corrects; so do not reject the discipline of the Almighty.
Job5:18 For He crushes but also binds up; He strikes
Job5:19 He will rescue you from six calamities; no harm will touch you in seven.
Job5:20 In famine He will redeem you from death
Job5:21 You will be safe from slander and not fear destruction when it comes.
Job5:22 You will laugh at destruction and hunger and not fear the animals of the earth.
Job5:23 For you will have a covenant with the stones of the field
Job5:24 You will know that your tent is secure
Job5:25 You will also know that your offspring will be many and your descendants like the grass of the earth.
Job5:26 You will approach the grave in full vigor
Job5:27 We have investigated this
Job6:1 Then Job answered:
Job6:2 If only my grief could be weighed and my devastation placed with it on a scale.
Job6:3 For then it would outweigh the sand of the seas! That is why my words are rash.
Job6:4 Surely the arrows of the Almighty have pierced me; my spirit drinks their poison. God's terrors are arrayed against me.
Job6:5 Does a wild donkey bray over fresh grass or an ox low over its fodder?
Job6:6 Is bland food eaten without salt? Is there flavor in an egg white?
Job6:7 I refuse to touch [them]; they are like contaminated food.
Job6:8 If only my request would be granted and God would provide what I hope for:
Job6:9 that He would decide to crush me
Job6:10 It would still bring me comfort
Job6:11 What strength do I have that I should continue to hope? What is my future
Job6:12 Is my strength that of stone
Job6:13 Since I cannot help myself
Job6:14 A despairing man should receive loyalty from his friends
Job6:15 My brothers are as treacherous as a wadi
Job6:16 and become darkened because of ice
Job6:17 The wadis evaporate in warm weather; they disappear from their channels in hot weather.
Job6:18 Caravans turn away from their routes
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema look [for these streams]. The traveling merchants of Sheba hope for them.
Job6:20 They are ashamed because they had been confident [of finding water]. When they arrive there
Job6:21 So [this] is what you have now become [to me]. When you see something dreadful
Job6:22 Have I ever said: Give me [something] or Pay a bribe for me from your wealth
Job6:23 or Deliver me from the enemy's power or Redeem me from the grasp of the ruthless?
Job6:24 Teach me
Job6:25 How painful honest words can be! But what does your rebuke prove?
Job6:26 Do you think that you can disprove [my] words or that a despairing man's words are [mere] wind?
Job6:27 No doubt you would cast [lots] for a fatherless child and negotiate a price to [sell] your friend.
Job6:28 But now
Job6:29 Reconsider; don't be unjust. Reconsider; my righteousness is still the issue.
Job6:30 Am I lying
Job7:1 Isn't mankind consigned to forced labor on earth? Are not his days like those of a hired hand?
Job7:2 Like a slave he longs for shade; like a hired man he waits for his pay.
Job7:3 So I have been made to inherit months of futility
Job7:4 When I lie down I think: When will I get up? But the evening drags on endlessly
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with maggots and encrusted with dirt. My skin forms scabs and then oozes.
Job7:6 My days pass more swiftly than a weaver's shuttle; they come to an end without hope.
Job7:7 Remember that my life is [but] a breath. My eye will never again see anything good.
Job7:8 The eye of anyone who looks on me will no longer see me. Your eyes will look for me
Job7:9 As a cloud fades away and vanishes
Job7:10 He will never return to his house; his hometown will no longer remember him.
Job7:11 Therefore I will not restrain my mouth. I will speak in the anguish of my spirit; I will complain in the bitterness of my soul.
Job7:12 Am I the sea or a sea monster
Job7:13 When I say: My bed will comfort me
Job7:14 then You frighten me with dreams
Job7:15 so that I prefer strangling
Job7:16 I give up! I will not live forever. Leave me alone
Job7:17 What is man
Job7:18 You inspect him every morning
Job7:19 Will You ever look away from me
Job7:20 [If] I have sinned
Job7:21 Why not forgive my sin and pardon my transgression? For soon I will lie down in the grave. You will eagerly seek me
Job8:1 Then Bildad the Shuhite replied:
Job8:2 How long will you go on saying these things? Your words are a blast of wind.
Job8:3 Does God pervert justice? Does the Almighty pervert what is right?
Job8:4 Since your children sinned against Him
Job8:5 But if you earnestly seek God and ask the Almighty for mercy
Job8:6 if you are pure and upright
Job8:7 Then
Job8:8 For ask the previous generation
Job8:9 since we were [born only] yesterday and know nothing. Our days on earth are but a shadow.
Job8:10 Will they not teach you and tell you and speak from their understanding?
Job8:11 Does papyrus grow where there is no marsh? Do reeds flourish without water?
Job8:12 While still uncut shoots
Job8:13 Such is the destiny of all who forget God; the hope of the godless will perish.
Job8:14 His source of confidence is fragile; what he trusts in is a spider's web.
Job8:15 He leans on his web
Job8:16 He is an amply watered plant in the sunshine; his shoots spread out over his garden.
Job8:17 His roots are intertwined around a pile of rocks. He looks for a home among the stones.
Job8:18 If he is uprooted from his place
Job8:19 Surely this is the joy of his way of life; yet others will sprout from the dust.
Job8:20 Look
Job8:21 He will yet fill your mouth with laughter and your lips with a shout of joy.
Job8:22 Your enemies will be clothed with shame; the tent of the wicked will exist no longer.
Job9:1 Then Job answered:
Job9:2 Yes
Job9:3 If one wanted to take Him to court
Job9:4 God is wise and all-powerful. Who has opposed Him and come out unharmed?
Job9:5 He removes mountains without their knowledge
Job9:6 He shakes the earth from its place so that its pillars tremble.
Job9:7 He commands the sun not to shine and seals off the stars.
Job9:8 He alone stretches out the heavens and treads on the waves of the sea.
Job9:9 He makes [the stars]: the Bear
Job9:10 He performs great and unsearchable things
Job9:11 If He passes by me
Job9:12 If He snatches [something]
Job9:13 God does not hold back His anger; Rahab's assistants cringe in fear beneath Him!
Job9:14 How then can I answer Him or choose my arguments against Him?
Job9:15 Even if I were in the right
Job9:16 If I summoned [Him] and He answered me
Job9:17 He batters me with a whirlwind and multiplies my wounds without cause.
Job9:18 He doesn't let me catch my breath but soaks me with bitter experiences.
Job9:19 If it is a matter of strength
Job9:20 Even if I were in the right
Job9:21 Though I am blameless
Job9:22 It is all the same. Therefore I say
Job9:23 When disaster brings sudden death
Job9:24 The earth is handed over to the wicked; He blindfolds its judges. If it isn't He
Job9:25 My days fly by faster than a runner; they flee without seeing any good.
Job9:26 They sweep by like boats made of papyrus
Job9:27 If I said
Job9:28 I would still live in terror of all my pains. I know You will not acquit me.
Job9:29 Since I will be found guilty
Job9:30 If I wash myself with snow
Job9:31 then You dip me in a pit [of mud]
Job9:32 For He is not a man like me
Job9:33 There is no one to judge between us
Job9:34 Let Him take His rod away from me so His terror will no longer frighten me.
Job9:35 Then I would speak and not fear Him. But that is not the case; I am on my own.
Job10:1 I am disgusted with my life. I will express my complaint and speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say to God: Do not declare me guilty! Let me know why You prosecute me.
Job10:3 Is it good for You to oppress
Job10:4 Do You have eyes of flesh
Job10:5 Are Your days like those of a human
Job10:6 that You look for my wrongdoing and search for my sin
Job10:7 even though You know that I am not wicked and that there is no one who can deliver from Your hand?
Job10:8 Your hands shaped me and formed me. Will You now turn around and destroy me?
Job10:9 Please remember that You formed me like clay. Will You now return me to dust?
Job10:10 Did You not pour me out like milk and curdle me like cheese?
Job10:11 You clothed me with skin and flesh
Job10:12 You gave me life and faithful love
Job10:13 Yet You concealed these [thoughts] in Your heart; I know that this was Your hidden plan:
Job10:14 if I sin
Job10:15 If I am wicked
Job10:16 If I am proud
Job10:17 You produce new witnesses against me and multiply Your anger toward me. Hardships assault me
Job10:18 Why did You bring me out of the womb? I should have died and never been seen.
Job10:19 I wish I had never existed but had been carried from the womb to the grave.
Job10:20 Are my days not few? Stop [it]! Leave me alone
Job10:21 before I go to a land of darkness and gloom
Job10:22 [It is] a land of blackness like the deepest darkness
Job11:1 Then Zophar the Naamathite replied:
Job11:2 Should this stream of words go unanswered and such a talker be acquitted?
Job11:3 Should your babbling put others to silence
Job11:4 You have said
Job11:5 But if only God would speak and declare His case against you
Job11:6 He would show you the secrets of wisdom
Job11:7 Can you fathom the depths of God or discover the limits of the Almighty?
Job11:8 [They are] higher than the heavens-- what can you do? [They are] deeper than Sheol-- what can you know?
Job11:9 Their measure is longer than the earth and wider than the sea.
Job11:10 If He passes by and throws [someone] in prison or convenes a court
Job11:11 Surely He knows which people are worthless. If He sees iniquity
Job11:12 But a stupid man will gain understanding as soon as a wild donkey is born a man!
Job11:13 As for you
Job11:14 if there is iniquity in your hand
Job11:15 then you will hold your head high
Job11:16 For you will forget your suffering
Job11:17 [Your] life will be brighter than noonday; [its] darkness will be like the morning.
Job11:18 You will be confident
Job11:19 You will lie down without fear
Job11:20 But the sight of the wicked will fail. Their way of escape will be cut off
Job12:1 Then Job answered:
Job12:2 No doubt you are the people
Job12:3 But I also have a mind; I am not inferior to you. Who doesn't know the things you are talking about?
Job12:4 I am a laughingstock to my friends
Job12:5 The one who is at ease holds calamity in contempt [and thinks] it is prepared for those whose feet are slipping.
Job12:6 The tents of robbers are safe
Job12:7 But ask the animals
Job12:8 Or speak to the earth
Job12:9 Which of all these does not know that the hand of the LORD has done this?
Job12:10 The life of every living thing is in His hand
Job12:11 Doesn't the ear test words as the palate tastes food?
Job12:12 Wisdom is found with the elderly
Job12:13 Wisdom and strength belong to God; counsel and understanding are His.
Job12:14 Whatever He tears down cannot be rebuilt; whoever He imprisons cannot be released.
Job12:15 When He withholds the waters
Job12:16 True wisdom and power belong to Him. The deceived and the deceiver are His.
Job12:17 He leads counselors away barefoot and makes judges go mad.
Job12:18 He releases the bonds put on by kings and ties a cloth around their waists.
Job12:19 He leads priests away barefoot and overthrows established leaders.
Job12:20 He deprives trusted advisers of speech and takes away the elders' good judgment.
Job12:21 He pours out contempt on nobles and disarms the strong.
Job12:22 He reveals mysteries from the darkness and brings the deepest darkness into the light.
Job12:23 He makes nations great
Job12:24 He deprives the world's leaders of reason
Job12:25 They grope around in darkness without light; He makes them stagger like drunken men.
Job13:1 Look
Job13:2 Everything you know
Job13:3 Yet I prefer to speak to the Almighty and argue my case before God.
Job13:4 But you coat [the truth] with lies; you are all worthless doctors.
Job13:5 If only you would shut up and let that be your wisdom!
Job13:6 Hear now my argument
Job13:7 Would you testify unjustly on God's behalf or speak deceitfully for Him?
Job13:8 Would you show partiality to Him or argue the case in His defense?
Job13:9 Would it go well if He examined you? Could you deceive Him as you would deceive a man?
Job13:10 Surely He would rebuke you if you secretly showed partiality.
Job13:11 Would God's majesty not terrify you? Would His dread not fall on you?
Job13:12 Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ash; your defenses are made of clay.
Job13:13 Be quiet
Job13:14 Why do I put myself at risk and take my life in my own hands?
Job13:15 Even if He kills me
Job13:16 Yes
Job13:17 Pay close attention to my words; let my declaration [ring] in your ears.

Job13:25 Will You frighten a wind-driven leaf? Will You chase after dry straw?
Job13:26 For You record bitter accusations against me and make me inherit the iniquities of my youth.
Job13:27 You put my feet in the stocks and stand watch over all my paths

Job14:1 Man born of woman is short of days and full of trouble.
Job14:2 He blossoms like a flower
Job14:3 Do You really take notice of one like this? Will You bring me into judgment against You?
Job14:4 Who can produce something pure from what is impure? No one!
Job14:5 Since man's days are determined and the number of his months depends on You
Job14:6 look away from him and let him rest so that he can enjoy his day like a hired hand.
Job14:7 There is hope for a tree: If it is cut down
Job14:8 If its roots grow old in the ground and its stump starts to die in the soil
Job14:9 the smell of water makes it thrive and produce twigs like a sapling.
Job14:10 But a man dies and fades away; he breathes his last-- where is he?
Job14:11 As water disappears from the sea and a wadi becomes parched and dry
Job14:12 so man lies down never to rise again. They will not wake up until the heavens are no more; they will not stir from their sleep.
Job14:13 If only You would hide me in Sheol and conceal me until Your anger passes
Job14:14 When a man dies
Job14:15 You would call
Job14:16 For then You would count my steps but would not take note of my sin.
Job14:17 My rebellion would be sealed up in a bag
Job14:18 But as a mountain collapses and crumbles and a rock is dislodged from its place
Job14:19 as water wears away stones and torrents wash away the soil from the land
Job14:20 You completely overpower him
Job14:21 If his sons receive honor
Job14:22 He feels only the pain of his own body and mourns only for himself.
Job15:1 Then Eliphaz the Temanite replied:
Job15:2 Does a wise man answer with empty counsel or fill himself with the hot east wind?
Job15:3 Should he argue with useless talk or with words that serve no good purpose?
Job15:4 But you even undermine the fear [of God] and hinder meditation before Him.
Job15:5 Your iniquity teaches you what to say
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 Were you the first person ever born
Job15:8 Do you listen in on the council of God
Job15:9 What do you know that we don't? [What] do you understand that is not [clear] to us?
Job15:10 Both the gray-haired and the elderly are with us
Job15:11 Are God's consolations not enough for you
Job15:12 Why has your heart misled you
Job15:13 as you turn your anger against God and allow such words to leave your mouth?
Job15:14 What is man
Job15:15 If God puts no trust in His holy ones and the heavens are not pure in His sight
Job15:16 how much less one who is revolting and corrupt
Job15:17 Listen to me and I will inform you. I will describe what I have seen
Job15:18 what was declared by wise men and was not suppressed by their ancestors
Job15:19 the land was given to them alone when no foreigner passed among them.
Job15:20 A wicked man writhes in pain all his days; few years are stored up for the ruthless.
Job15:21 Dreadful sounds fill his ears; when he is at peace
Job15:22 He doesn't believe he will return from darkness; he is destined for the sword.
Job15:23 He wanders about for food
Job15:24 Trouble and distress terrify him
Job15:25 For he has stretched out his hand against God and has arrogantly opposed the Almighty.
Job15:26 He rushes headlong at Him with his thick
Job15:27 Though his face is covered with fat and his waistline bulges with it
Job15:28 he will dwell in ruined cities
Job15:29 He will no longer be rich; his wealth will not endure. His possessions will not spread over the land.
Job15:30 He will not escape from the darkness; flames will wither his shoots
Job15:31 Let him not put trust in worthless things
Job15:32 It will be accomplished before his time
Job15:33 He will be like a vine that drops its unripe grapes and like an olive tree that sheds its blossoms.
Job15:34 For the company of the godless will be barren
Job15:35 They conceive trouble and give birth to evil; their womb prepares deception.
Job16:1 Then Job answered:
Job16:2 I have heard many things like these. You are all miserable comforters.
Job16:3 Is there [no] end to your empty words? What provokes you that you continue testifying?
Job16:4 If you were in my place I could also talk like you. I could string words together against you and shake my head at you
Job16:5 I would encourage you with my mouth
Job16:6 Even if I speak
Job16:7 Surely He has now exhausted me. You have devastated my entire family.
Job16:8 You have shriveled me up-- it has become a witness; My frailty rises up against me and testifies to my face.
Job16:9 His anger tears [at me]
Job16:10 They open their mouths against me and strike my cheeks with contempt; they join themselves together against me.
Job16:11 God hands me over to unjust men; He throws me into the hands of the wicked.
Job16:12 I was at ease
Job16:13 His archers surround me. He pierces my kidneys without mercy and pours my bile on the ground.
Job16:14 He breaks through my defenses again and again; He charges at me like a warrior.
Job16:15 I have sewn sackcloth over my skin; I have buried my strength in the dust.
Job16:16 My face has grown red with weeping
Job16:17 although my hands are free from violence and my prayer is pure.
Job16:18 Earth
Job16:19 Even now my witness is in heaven
Job16:20 My friends scoff at me as I weep before God.
Job16:21 I wish that someone might arbitrate between a man and God just as a man [pleads] for his friend.
Job16:22 For [only] a few years will pass before I go the way of no return.

Job17:2 Surely mockers surround me and my eyes must gaze at their rebellion.

Job17:4 You have closed their minds to understanding
Job17:5 If a man informs on his friends for a price

Job18:6 The light in his tent grows dark
Job18:7 His powerful stride is shortened
Job18:8 For his own feet lead him into a net

Job19:1 Then Job answered:
Job19:2 How long will you torment me and crush me with words?
Job19:3 You have humiliated me ten times now

Job19:6 then understand that it is God who has wronged me and caught me in His net.

Job19:8 He has blocked my way so that I cannot pass through; He has veiled my paths with darkness.
Job19:9 He has stripped me of my honor and removed the crown from my head.
Job19:10 He tears me down on every side so that I am ruined. He uproots my hope like a tree.
