Ecclesiastes5:7 For many dreams bring futility
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see oppression of the poor and perversion of justice and righteousness in the province
Ecclesiastes5:9 The profit from the land is taken by all; the king is served by the field.
Ecclesiastes5:10 The one who loves money is never satisfied with money
Ecclesiastes5:11 When good things increase
Ecclesiastes5:12 The sleep of the worker is sweet
Ecclesiastes5:13 There is a sickening tragedy I have seen under the sun: wealth kept by its owner to his harm.
Ecclesiastes5:14 That wealth was lost in a bad venture
Ecclesiastes5:15 As he came from his mother's womb
Ecclesiastes5:16 This too is a sickening tragedy: exactly as he comes
Ecclesiastes5:17 What is more
Ecclesiastes5:18 Here is what I have seen to be good: it is appropriate to eat
Ecclesiastes5:19 God has also given riches and wealth to every man
Ecclesiastes5:20 for he does not often consider the days of his life because God keeps him occupied with the joy of his heart.

Ecclesiastes6:2 God gives a man riches
Ecclesiastes6:3 A man may father a hundred children and live many years. No matter how long he lives
Ecclesiastes6:4 For he comes in futility and he goes in darkness
Ecclesiastes6:5 Though a stillborn child does not see the sun and is not conscious
Ecclesiastes6:6 And if he lives a thousand years twice
Ecclesiastes6:7 All man's labor is for his stomach

Ecclesiastes6:11 For when there are many words
Ecclesiastes6:12 For who knows what is good for man in life

Ecclesiastes7:12 For wisdom is protection as money is protection

Ecclesiastes7:14 In the day of prosperity be joyful

Ecclesiastes7:17 Don't be excessively wicked

SongOfSongs2:10 My love calls to me: Arise
SongOfSongs2:11 For now the winter is past; the rain has ended and gone away.
SongOfSongs2:12 The blossoms appear in the countryside. The time of singing has come
SongOfSongs2:13 The fig tree ripens its figs; the blossoming vines give off their fragrance. Arise
SongOfSongs2:14 My dove
SongOfSongs2:15 Catch the foxes for us-- the little foxes that ruin the vineyards-- for our vineyards are in bloom.
SongOfSongs2:16 My love is mine and I am his; he feeds among the lilies.
SongOfSongs2:17 Before the day breaks and the shadows flee
SongOfSongs3:1 In my bed at night I sought the one I love; I sought him
SongOfSongs3:2 I will arise now and go about the city
SongOfSongs3:3 The guards who go about the city found me. Have you seen the one I love? [I asked them].
SongOfSongs3:4 I had just passed them when I found the one I love. I held on to him and would not let him go until I brought him to my mother's house-- to the chamber of the one who conceived me.
SongOfSongs3:5 Young women of Jerusalem
SongOfSongs3:6 What is this coming up from the wilderness like columns of smoke
SongOfSongs3:7 It is Solomon's royal litter surrounded by 60 warriors from the mighty of Israel.
SongOfSongs3:8 All of them are skilled with swords and trained in warfare. Each has his sword at his side [to guard] against the terror of the night.
SongOfSongs3:9 King Solomon made a sedan chair for himself with wood from Lebanon.
SongOfSongs3:10 He made its posts of silver
SongOfSongs3:11 Come out

SongOfSongs4:5 Your breasts are like two fawns

SongOfSongs4:9 You have captured my heart

SongOfSongs4:11 Your lips drip [sweetness like] the honeycomb
SongOfSongs4:12 My sister
SongOfSongs4:13 Your branches are a paradise of pomegranates with choicest fruits
SongOfSongs4:14 nard and saffron
SongOfSongs4:15 [You are] a garden spring

SongOfSongs5:1 I have come to my garden-- my sister
SongOfSongs5:2 I sleep
SongOfSongs5:3 I have taken off my clothing. How can I put it back on? I have washed my feet. How can I get them dirty?
SongOfSongs5:4 My love thrust his hand through the opening
SongOfSongs5:5 I rose to open for my love. My hands dripped with myrrh
SongOfSongs5:6 I opened to my love
SongOfSongs5:7 The guards who go about the city found me. They beat and wounded me; they took my cloak from me-- the guardians of the walls.
SongOfSongs5:8 Young women of Jerusalem
SongOfSongs5:9 What makes the one you love better than another
SongOfSongs5:10 My love is fit and strong
SongOfSongs5:11 His head is purest gold. His hair is wavy and black as a raven.
SongOfSongs5:12 His eyes are like doves beside streams of water
SongOfSongs5:13 His cheeks are like beds of spice
SongOfSongs5:14 His arms are rods of gold set with topaz. His body is an ivory panel covered with sapphires.
SongOfSongs5:15 His legs are alabaster pillars set on pedestals of pure gold. His presence is like Lebanon
SongOfSongs5:16 His mouth is sweetness. He is absolutely desirable. This is my love

Isaiah1:1 The vision concerning Judah and Jerusalem that Isaiah son of Amoz saw during the reignsof Uzziah
Isaiah1:2 Listen
Isaiah1:3 The ox knows its owner
Isaiah1:4 Oh-- sinful nation
Isaiah1:5 Why do you want more beatings? Why do you keep on rebelling? The whole head is hurt
Isaiah1:6 From the sole of the foot even to the head
Isaiah1:7 Your land is desolate
Isaiah1:8 Daughter Zion is abandoned like a shelter in a vineyard
Isaiah1:9 If the LORD of Hosts had not left us a few survivors
Isaiah1:10 Hear the word of the LORD
Isaiah1:11 What are all your sacrifices to Me? asks the LORD. I have had enough of burnt offerings and rams and the fat of well-fed cattle; I have no desire for the blood of bulls
Isaiah1:12 When you come to appear before Me
Isaiah1:13 Stop bringing useless offerings. I despise [your] incense. New Moons and Sabbaths
Isaiah1:14 I hate your New Moons and prescribed festivals. They have become a burden to Me; I am tired of putting up with [them].
Isaiah1:15 When you lift up your hands [in prayer]
Isaiah1:16 Wash yourselves. Cleanse yourselves. Remove your evil deeds from My sight. Stop doing evil.
Isaiah1:17 Learn to do what is good. Seek justice. Correct the oppressor. Defend the rights of the fatherless. Plead the widow's cause.
Isaiah1:18 Come
Isaiah1:19 If you are willing and obedient
Isaiah1:20 But if you refuse and rebel
Isaiah1:21 The faithful city-- what an adulteress she has become! She was once full of justice. Righteousness once dwelt in her-- but now
Isaiah1:22 Your silver has become dross
Isaiah1:23 Your rulers are rebels
Isaiah1:24 Therefore the Lord God of Hosts
Isaiah1:25 I will turn My hand against you and will burn away your dross completely; I will remove all your impurities.
Isaiah1:26 I will restore your judges to what they once were
Isaiah1:27 Zion will be redeemed by justice
Isaiah1:28 But both rebels and sinners will be destroyed
Isaiah1:29 Indeed
Isaiah1:30 For you will become like an oak whose leaves are withered
Isaiah1:31 The strong one will become tinder
Isaiah2:1 The vision that Isaiah son of Amoz saw concerning Judah and Jerusalem:
Isaiah2:2 In the last days the mountain of the LORD's house will be established at the top of the mountains and will be raised above the hills. All nations will stream to it
Isaiah2:3 and many peoples will come and say
Isaiah2:4 He will settle disputes among the nations and provide arbitration for many peoples. They will turn their swords into plows and their spears into pruning knives. Nations will not take up the sword against [other] nations
Isaiah2:5 House of Jacob
Isaiah2:6 For You have abandoned Your people
Isaiah2:7 Their land is full of silver and gold
Isaiah2:8 Their land is full of idols; they bow down to the work of their hands
Isaiah2:9 So humanity is brought low
Isaiah2:10 Go into the rocks and hide in the dust from the terror of the LORD and from His majestic splendor.
Isaiah2:11 Human pride will be humbled
Isaiah2:12 For a day belonging to the LORD of Hosts is [coming] against all that is proud and lofty
Isaiah2:13 against all the cedars of Lebanon
Isaiah2:14 against all the high mountains
Isaiah2:15 against every high tower
Isaiah2:16 against every ship of Tarshish
Isaiah2:17 So human pride will be brought low
Isaiah2:18 The idols will vanish completely.
Isaiah2:19 People will go into caves in the rocks and holes in the ground
Isaiah2:20 On that day people will throw their silver and gold idols
Isaiah2:21 They will go into the caves of the rocks and the crevices in the cliffs
Isaiah2:22 Put no more trust in man
Isaiah3:1 Observe this: The Lord God of Hosts is about to remove from Jerusalem and from Judah every kind of security: the entire supply of bread and water
Isaiah3:2 the hero and warrior
Isaiah3:3 the commander of 50 and the dignitary
Isaiah3:4 I will make youths their leaders
Isaiah3:5 The people will oppress one another
Isaiah3:6 A man will even seize his brother in his father's house
Isaiah3:7 On that day he will cry out
Isaiah3:8 For Jerusalem has stumbled and Judah has fallen because they have spoken and acted against the LORD
Isaiah3:9 The look on their faces testifies against them
Isaiah3:10 Tell the righteous that it will go well [for them]
Isaiah3:11 Woe to the wicked-- [it will go] badly [for them]
Isaiah3:12 Youths oppress My people
Isaiah3:13 The LORD rises to argue the case and stands to judge the people.
Isaiah3:14 The LORD brings [this] charge against the elders and leaders of His people: You have devastated the vineyard. The plunder from the poor is in your houses.
Isaiah3:15 Why do you crush My people and grind the faces of the poor? says the Lord God of Hosts.
Isaiah3:16 The LORD also says: Because the daughters of Zion are haughty
Isaiah3:17 the Lord will put scabs on the heads of the daughters of Zion
Isaiah3:18 On that day the Lord will strip their finery: ankle bracelets
Isaiah3:19 pendants
Isaiah3:20 headdresses
Isaiah3:21 signet rings
Isaiah3:22 festive robes
Isaiah3:23 garments
Isaiah3:24 Instead of perfume there will be a stench; instead of a belt
Isaiah3:25 Your men will fall by the sword
Isaiah3:26 Then her gates will lament and mourn; deserted
Isaiah4:1 On that day seven women will seize one man
Isaiah4:2 On that day the branch of the LORD will be beautiful and glorious
Isaiah4:3 Whoever remains in Zion and whoever is left in Jerusalem will be called holy-- all in Jerusalem who are destined to live--
Isaiah4:4 when the Lord has washed away the filth of the daughters of Zion and cleansed the bloodguilt from the heart of Jerusalem by a spirit of judgment and a spirit of burning.
Isaiah4:5 Then the LORD will create a cloud of smoke by day and a glowing flame of fire by night over the entire site of Mount Zion and over its assemblies. For there will be a canopy over all the glory
Isaiah4:6 and there will be a booth for shade from heat by day
Isaiah5:1 I will sing about the one I love
Isaiah5:2 He broke up the soil
Isaiah5:3 So now
Isaiah5:4 What more could I have done for My vineyard than I did? Why
Isaiah5:5 Now I will tell you what I am about to do to My vineyard: I will remove its hedge
Isaiah5:6 I will make it a wasteland. It will not be pruned or weeded; thorns and briers will grow up. I will also give orders to the clouds that rain should not fall on it.
Isaiah5:7 For the vineyard of the LORD of Hosts is the house of Israel
Isaiah5:8 Woe to those who add house to house and join field to field until there is no more room and you alone are left in the land.
Isaiah5:9 In my hearing the LORD of Hosts [has taken an oath]: Indeed
Isaiah5:10 For a ten-acre vineyard will yield only six gallons
Isaiah5:11 Woe to those who rise early in the morning in pursuit of beer
Isaiah5:12 At their feasts they have lyre
Isaiah5:13 Therefore My people go into exile because they lack knowledge; the dignitaries are starving
Isaiah5:14 Therefore Sheol enlarges its throat and opens wide its enormous jaws
Isaiah5:15 Humanity is brought low
Isaiah5:16 But the LORD of Hosts is exalted by His justice
Isaiah5:17 Lambs will graze as [if in] their own pastures
Isaiah5:18 Woe to those who drag wickedness with cords of deceit and [pull] sin along with cart ropes
Isaiah5:19 to those who say: Let Him hurry up and do His work quickly so that we can see it! Let the plan of the Holy One of Israel take place so that we can know it!
Isaiah5:20 Woe to those who call evil good and good evil
Isaiah5:21 Woe to those who are wise in their own opinion and clever in their own sight.
Isaiah5:22 Woe to those who are heroes at drinking wine
Isaiah5:23 who acquit the guilty for a bribe and deprive the innocent of justice.
Isaiah5:24 Therefore
Isaiah5:25 Therefore the LORD's anger burns against His people. He raised His hand against them and struck them; the mountains quaked
Isaiah5:26 He raises a signal flag for the distant nations and whistles for them from the ends of the earth. Look-- how quickly and swiftly they come!
Isaiah5:27 None of them grows weary or stumbles; no one slumbers or sleeps. No belt is loose
Isaiah5:28 Their arrows are sharpened
Isaiah5:29 Their roaring is like a lion's; they roar like young lions; they growl and seize their prey and carry [it] off
Isaiah5:30 On that day they will roar over it
Isaiah6:1 In the year that King Uzziah died
Isaiah6:2 Seraphim were standing above Him; each one had six wings: with two he covered his face
Isaiah6:3 And one called to another: Holy
Isaiah6:4 The foundations of the doorways shook at the sound of their voices
Isaiah6:5 Then I said: Woe is me
Isaiah6:6 Then one of the seraphim flew to me
Isaiah6:7 He touched my mouth [with it] and said: Now that this has touched your lips
Isaiah6:8 Then I heard the voice of the Lord saying: Who should I send? Who will go for Us? I said: Here I am. Send me.
Isaiah6:9 And He replied: Go! Say to these people: Keep listening
Isaiah6:10 Dull the minds of these people; deafen their ears and blind their eyes; otherwise they might see with their eyes and hear with their ears
Isaiah6:11 Then I said
Isaiah6:12 and the LORD drives the people far away
Isaiah6:13 Though a tenth will remain in the land

Isaiah7:2 When it became known to the house of David that Aram had occupied Ephraim

Isaiah7:5 For Aram

Isaiah7:9 the head of Ephraim is Samaria

Isaiah7:11 Ask for a sign from the LORD your God-- from the depths of Sheol to the heights of heaven.
Isaiah7:12 But Ahaz replied
Isaiah7:13 Isaiah said

Isaiah7:19 All of them will come and settle in the steep ravines
Isaiah7:20 On that day the Lord will use a razor hired from beyond the Euphrates River-- the king of Assyria-- to shave the head
Isaiah7:21 On that day a man will raise a young cow and two sheep
Isaiah7:22 and from the abundant milk they give he will eat butter
Isaiah7:23 And on that day every place where there were 1
Isaiah7:24 A man will go there with bow and arrows because the whole land will be thorns and briers.

Isaiah8:2 I have appointed trustworthy witnesses-- Uriah the priest and Zechariah son of Jeberechiah.
Isaiah8:3 I was then intimate with the prophetess
Isaiah8:4 for before the boy knows how to call out father or mother
Isaiah8:5 The LORD spoke to me again:
Isaiah8:6 Because these people rejected the slowly flowing waters of Shiloah and rejoiced with Rezin and the son of Remaliah
Isaiah8:7 the Lord will certainly bring against them the mighty rushing waters of the Euphrates River-- the king of Assyria and all his glory. It will overflow its channels and spill over all its banks.
Isaiah8:8 It will pour into Judah
Isaiah8:9 Band together
Isaiah8:10 Devise a plan; it will fail. Make a prediction; it will not happen. For God is with us.
Isaiah8:11 For this is what the LORD said to me with great power
Isaiah8:12 Do not call everything an alliance these people say is an alliance. Do not fear what they fear; do not be terrified.
Isaiah8:13 You are to regard only the LORD of Hosts as holy. Only He should be feared; only He should be held in awe.
Isaiah8:14 He will be a sanctuary; but for the two houses of Israel
Isaiah8:15 Many will stumble over these; they will fall and be broken; they will be snared and captured.
Isaiah8:16 Bind up the testimony. Seal up the instruction among my disciples.
Isaiah8:17 I will wait for the Lord
Isaiah8:18 Here I am with the children the LORD has given me to be signs and wonders in Israel from the LORD of Hosts who dwells on Mount Zion.

Isaiah8:20 To the law and to the testimony! If they do not speak according to this word
Isaiah8:21 They will wander through the land
Isaiah8:22 They will look toward the earth and see only distress
Isaiah9:1  Nevertheless
Isaiah9:2  The people walking in darkness have seen a great light; on those living in the land of darkness
Isaiah9:3 You have enlarged the nation and increased its joy. [The people] have rejoiced before You as they rejoice at harvest time and as they rejoice when dividing spoils.
Isaiah9:4 For You have shattered their burdensome yoke and the rod on their shoulders
Isaiah9:5 For the trampling boot of battle and the bloodied garments of war will be burned as fuel for the fire.
Isaiah9:6 For a child will be born for us
Isaiah9:7 The dominion will be vast
Isaiah9:8 The Lord sent a message against Jacob; it came against Israel.
Isaiah9:9 All the people-- Ephraim and the inhabitants of Samaria-- will know it. They will say with pride and arrogance:
Isaiah9:10 The bricks have fallen
Isaiah9:11 The LORD has raised up Rezin's adversaries against him and stirred up his enemies.
Isaiah9:12 Aram from the east and Philistia from the west have consumed Israel with open mouths. In all this
Isaiah9:13 The people did not turn to Him who struck them; they did not seek the LORD of Hosts.
Isaiah9:14 So the LORD cut off Israel's head and tail
Isaiah9:15 The head is the elder
Isaiah9:16 The leaders of the people mislead [them]
Isaiah9:17 Therefore the Lord does not rejoice over Israel's young men and has no compassion on its fatherless and widows
Isaiah9:18 For wickedness burns like a fire that consumes thorns and briers and kindles the forest thickets so that they go up in a column of smoke.
Isaiah9:19 The land is scorched by the wrath of the LORD of Hosts
Isaiah9:20 They carve [meat] on the right
Isaiah9:21 Manasseh is with Ephraim
Isaiah10:1 Woe to those enacting crooked statutes and writing oppressive laws
Isaiah10:2 to keep the poor from getting a fair trial and to deprive the afflicted among my people of justice
Isaiah10:3 What will you do on the day of punishment when devastation comes from far away? Who will you run to for help? Where will you leave your wealth?
Isaiah10:4 [There will be nothing to do] except crouch among the prisoners or fall among the slain. In all this
Isaiah10:5 Woe to Assyria
Isaiah10:6 I will send him against a godless nation; I will command him [to go] against a people destined for My rage
Isaiah10:7 But this is not what he intends; this is not what he plans. It is his intent to destroy and to cut off many nations.
Isaiah10:8 For he says: Aren't all my commanders kings?
Isaiah10:9 Isn't Calno like Carchemish? Isn't Hamath like Arpad? Isn't Samaria like Damascus?
Isaiah10:10 As my hand seized the idolatrous kingdoms
Isaiah10:11 and as I did to Samaria and its idols will I not also do to Jerusalem and its idols?
Isaiah10:12 But when the Lord finishes all His work against Mount Zion and Jerusalem
Isaiah10:13 For he said: I have done [this] by my own strength and wisdom
Isaiah10:14 My hand has reached out
Isaiah10:15 Does an ax exalt itself above the one who chops with it? Does a saw magnify itself above the one who saws with it? As if a staff could wave those who lift it! As if a rod could lift what isn't wood!
Isaiah10:16 Therefore the Lord God of Hosts will inflict an emaciating disease on the well-fed of Assyria
Isaiah10:17 Israel's Light will become a fire
Isaiah10:18 He will completely destroy the glory of its forests and orchards as a sickness consumes a person.
Isaiah10:19 The remaining trees of its forest will be so few in number that a child could count them.
Isaiah10:20 On that day the remnant of Israel and the survivors of the house of Jacob will no longer depend on the one who struck them
Isaiah10:21 The remnant will return
Isaiah10:22 Israel
Isaiah10:23 For throughout the land the Lord God of Hosts is carrying out a destruction that was decreed.
Isaiah10:24 Therefore
Isaiah10:25 In just a little while My wrath will be spent and My anger will turn to their destruction.
Isaiah10:26 And the LORD of Hosts will brandish a whip against him as [He did when He] struck Midian at the rock of Oreb; and He will raise His staff over the sea as [He did] in Egypt.
Isaiah10:27 On that day his burden will fall from your shoulders
Isaiah10:28 Assyria has come to Aiath and has gone through Migron
Isaiah10:29 They crossed over at the ford
Isaiah10:30 Cry aloud
Isaiah10:31 Madmenah has fled. The inhabitants of Gebim have sought refuge.
Isaiah10:32 Today he will stand at Nob
Isaiah10:33 Look
Isaiah10:34 He is clearing the thickets of the forest with an ax
Isaiah11:1 Then a shoot will grow from the stump of Jesse
Isaiah11:2 The Spirit of the LORD will rest on Him-- a Spirit of wisdom and understanding
Isaiah11:3 His delight will be in the fear of the LORD. He will not judge by what He sees with His eyes
Isaiah11:4 but He will judge the poor righteously and execute justice for the oppressed of the land. He will strike the land with discipline from His mouth
Isaiah11:5 Righteousness and faithfulness will be a belt around His waist.
Isaiah11:6 The wolf will live with the lamb
Isaiah11:7 The cow and the bear will graze
Isaiah11:8 An infant will play beside the cobra's pit
Isaiah11:9 No one will harm or destroy on My entire holy mountain
Isaiah11:10 On that day the root of Jesse will stand as a banner for the peoples. The nations will seek Him
Isaiah11:11 On that day the Lord will [extend] His hand a second time to recover-- from Assyria
Isaiah11:12 He will lift up a banner for the nations and gather the dispersed of Israel; He will collect the scattered of Judah from the four corners of the earth.
Isaiah11:13 Ephraim's envy will cease; Judah's harassment will end. Ephraim will no longer be envious of Judah
Isaiah11:14 But they will swoop down on the Philistine flank to the west. Together they will plunder the people of the east. They will extend their power over Edom and Moab
Isaiah11:15 The LORD will divide the Gulf of Suez. He will wave His hand over the Euphrates with His mighty wind and will split it into seven streams
Isaiah11:16 There will be a highway for the remnant of His people who will survive from Assyria

Isaiah12:2 Indeed

Isaiah13:1 An oracle against Babylon that Isaiah son of Amoz saw:
Isaiah13:2 Lift up a banner on a barren mountain. Call out to them. Wave your hand
Isaiah13:3 I have commanded My chosen ones; I have also called My warriors
Isaiah13:4 Listen
Isaiah13:5 They are coming from a far land
Isaiah13:6 Wail! For the day of the LORD is near. It will come like destruction from the Almighty.
Isaiah13:7 Therefore everyone's hands will become weak
Isaiah13:8 They will be horrified; pain and agony will seize [them]; they will be in anguish like a woman in labor. They will look at each other
Isaiah13:9 Look
Isaiah13:10 Indeed
Isaiah13:11 I will bring disaster on the world
Isaiah13:12 I will make man scarcer than gold
Isaiah13:13 Therefore I will make the heavens tremble
Isaiah13:14 Like wandering gazelles and like sheep without a shepherd
Isaiah13:15 Whoever is found will be stabbed
Isaiah13:16 Their children will be smashed [to death] before their eyes; their houses will be looted
Isaiah13:17 Look! I am stirring up the Medes against them
Isaiah13:18 [Their] bows will cut young men to pieces. They will have no compassion on little ones; they will not look with pity on children.
Isaiah13:19 And Babylon
Isaiah13:20 It will never be inhabited or lived in from generation to generation; a nomad will not pitch his tent there
Isaiah13:21 But wild animals will lie down there
Isaiah13:22 Hyenas will howl in the fortresses
Isaiah14:1 For the LORD will have compassion on Jacob and will choose Israel again. He will settle them on their own land. The foreigner will join them and be united with the house of Jacob.
Isaiah14:2 The nations will escort Israel and bring it to its homeland. Then the house of Israel will possess them as male and female slaves in the LORD's land. They will make captives of their captors and will rule over their oppressors.
Isaiah14:3 When the LORD gives you rest from your pain
Isaiah14:4 you will sing this song [of contempt] about the king of Babylon and say: How the oppressor has quieted down
Isaiah14:5 The LORD has broken the staff of the wicked
Isaiah14:6 It struck the peoples in anger with unceasing blows. It subdued the nations in rage with relentless persecution.
Isaiah14:7 All the earth is calm and at rest; people shout with a ringing cry.
Isaiah14:8 Even the cypresses and the cedars of Lebanon rejoice over you: Since you have been laid low
Isaiah14:9 Sheol below is eager to greet your coming. He stirs up the spirits of the departed for you-- all the rulers of the earth. He makes all the kings of the nations rise from their thrones.
Isaiah14:10 They all respond to you
Isaiah14:11 Your splendor has been brought down to Sheol
Isaiah14:12 Shining morning star
Isaiah14:13 You said to yourself: I will ascend to the heavens; I will set up my throne above the stars of God. I will sit on the mount of the [gods'] assembly
Isaiah14:14 I will ascend above the highest clouds; I will make myself like the Most High.
Isaiah14:15 But you will be brought down to Sheol into the deepest regions of the Pit.
Isaiah14:16 Those who see you will stare at you; they will look closely at you: Is this the man who caused the earth to tremble
Isaiah14:17 who turned the world into a wilderness
Isaiah14:18 All the kings of the nations lie in splendor
Isaiah14:19 But you are thrown out without a grave

Isaiah38:9 A poem by Hezekiah king of Judah after he had been sick and had recovered from his illness:

Isaiah38:13 I thought until the morning: He will break all my bones like a lion; You make an end of me day and night.

Jeremiah1:1 The words of Jeremiah
Jeremiah1:2 The word of the LORD came to him in the thirteenth year of the reign of Josiah son of Amon
Jeremiah1:3 It also came throughout the days of Jehoiakim son of Josiah
Jeremiah1:4 The word of the LORD came to me:
Jeremiah1:5 I chose you before I formed you in the womb; I set you apart before you were born. I appointed you a prophet to the nations.
Jeremiah1:6 But I protested
Jeremiah1:7 Then the LORD said to me: Do not say: I am [only] a youth
Jeremiah1:8 Do not be afraid of anyone
Jeremiah1:9 Then the LORD reached out His hand
Jeremiah1:10 See
Jeremiah1:11 Then the word of the LORD came to me
Jeremiah1:12 The LORD said to me
Jeremiah1:13 Again the word of the LORD came to me inquiring
Jeremiah1:14 Then the LORD said to me
Jeremiah1:15 Indeed
Jeremiah1:16 I will pronounce My judgments against them for all the evil they did when they abandoned Me to burn incense to other gods and to worship the works of their own hands.
Jeremiah1:17 Now
Jeremiah1:18 Today
Jeremiah1:19 They will fight against you but never prevail over you
Jeremiah2:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Jeremiah2:2 Go and announce directly to Jerusalem that this is what the LORD says: I remember the loyalty of your youth
Jeremiah2:3 Israel was holy to the LORD
Jeremiah2:4 Hear the word of the LORD
Jeremiah2:5 Here is what the LORD says: What fault did your fathers find in Me that they went so far from Me
Jeremiah2:6 They stopped asking: Where is the LORD who brought us from the land of Egypt
Jeremiah2:7 I brought you to a fertile land to eat its fruit and bounty
Jeremiah2:8 The priests quit asking: Where is the LORD? The experts in the law no longer knew Me
Jeremiah2:9 Therefore
Jeremiah2:10 Cross over to Cyprusand take a look. Send [someone] to Kedar and consider carefully; see if there has ever been anything like this:
Jeremiah2:11 Has a nation [ever] exchanged its gods? (but they were not gods!) Yet My people have exchanged their Glory for useless idols.
Jeremiah2:12 Be horrified at this
Jeremiah2:13 For My people have committed a double evil: They have abandoned Me
Jeremiah2:14 Is Israel a slave? Was he born into slavery? Why else has he become a prey?
Jeremiah2:15 The young lions have roared at him; they have roared loudly. They have laid waste his land. His cities are in ruins
Jeremiah2:16 The men of Memphis and Tahpanhes have also broken your skull.
Jeremiah2:17 Have you not brought this on yourself by abandoning the LORD your God while He was leading you along the way?
Jeremiah2:18 Now what will you gain by traveling along the way to Egypt to drink the waters of the Nile? What will you gain by traveling along the way to Assyria to drink the waters of the Euphrates?
Jeremiah2:19 Your own evil will discipline you; your own apostasies will reprimand you. Think it over and see how evil and bitter it is for you to abandon the LORD your God and to have no fear of Me. [This is] the declaration of the Lord God of Hosts.
Jeremiah2:20 For long ago I broke your yoke; I tore off your fetters. You insisted: I will not serve! On every high hill and under every leafy tree you lie down [like] a prostitute.
Jeremiah2:21 I planted you
Jeremiah2:22 Even if you wash with lye and use a great amount of soap
Jeremiah2:23 How can you protest: I am not defiled; I have not followed the Baals? Look at your behavior in the valley; acknowledge what you have done. [You are] a swift young camel twisting and turning on her way
Jeremiah2:24 a wild donkey at home in the wilderness. She sniffs the wind in the heat of her desire. Who can control her passion? All who look for her will not become tired; they will find her in her mating season.
Jeremiah2:25 Keep your feet from going bare and your throat from thirst. But you say: It's hopeless; I love strangers
Jeremiah2:26 Like the shame of a thief when he is caught
Jeremiah2:27 say to a tree: You are my father
Jeremiah2:28 But where are your gods you made for yourself? Let them rise up and save you in your time of disaster if they can
Jeremiah2:29 Why do you bring a case against Me? All of you have rebelled against Me. [This is] the LORD's declaration.
Jeremiah2:30 I have struck down your children in vain; they would not accept discipline. Your own sword has devoured your prophets like a ravaging lion.
Jeremiah2:31 [Evil] generation
Jeremiah2:32 Can a young woman forget her jewelry or a bride her wedding sash? Yet My people have forgotten Me for countless days.
Jeremiah2:33 How skillfully you pursue love; you also teach evil women your ways.
Jeremiah2:34 Moreover
Jeremiah2:35 you claim: I am innocent. His anger is sure to turn away from me. But I will certainly judge you because you have said: I have not sinned.
Jeremiah2:36 How unstable you are
Jeremiah2:37 Moreover

Jeremiah6:19 Listen
Jeremiah6:20 What use to Me is frankincense from Sheba or sweet cane from a distant land? Your burnt offerings are not acceptable; your sacrifices do not please Me.
Jeremiah6:21 Therefore
Jeremiah6:22 This is what the LORD says: Look
Jeremiah6:23 They grasp bow and javelin. They are cruel and show no mercy. Their voice roars like the sea

Jeremiah23:7 The days are coming-- the LORD's declaration-- when it will no longer be said: As the LORD lives who brought the Israelites from the land of Egypt

Jeremiah23:13 Among the prophets of Samaria I saw something disgusting: They prophesied by Baal and led My people Israel astray.

Jeremiah23:15 Therefore
Jeremiah23:16 This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Do not listen to the words of the prophets who prophesy to you. They are making you worthless. They speak visions from their own minds
Jeremiah23:17 They keep on saying to those who despise Me: The LORD has said: You will have peace. To everyone who walks in the stubbornness of his heart they have said
Jeremiah23:18 For who has stood in the council of the LORD to see and hear His word? Who has paid attention to His word and obeyed?
Jeremiah23:19 Look
Jeremiah23:20 The LORD's anger will not turn back until He has completely fulfilled the purposes of His heart. In time to come you will understand it clearly.
Jeremiah23:21 I did not send these prophets
Jeremiah23:22 If they had really stood in My council
Jeremiah23:23 Am I a God who is only near-- [this is] the LORD's declaration-- and not a God who is far away?
Jeremiah23:24 Can a man hide himself in secret places where I cannot see him?-- the LORD's declaration. Do I not fill the heavens and the earth?-- the LORD's declaration.
Jeremiah23:25 I have heard what the prophets who prophesy a lie in My name have said: I had a dream! I had a dream!
Jeremiah23:26 How long will this continue in the minds of the prophets prophesying lies
Jeremiah23:27 Through their dreams that they tell one another
Jeremiah23:28 The prophet who has [only] a dream should recount the dream
Jeremiah23:29 Is not My word like fire-- the LORD's declaration-- and like a sledgehammer that pulverizes rock?
Jeremiah23:30 Therefore
Jeremiah23:31 I am against the prophets-- the LORD's declaration-- who use their own tongues to deliver an oracle.
Jeremiah23:32 I am against those who prophesy false dreams-- the LORD's declaration-- telling them and leading My people astray with their falsehoods and their boasting. It was not I who sent or commanded them
Jeremiah23:33 Now when these people or a prophet or a priest asks you: What is the burden of the LORD? you will respond to them: What is the burden? I will throw you away-- [this is] the LORD's declaration.
Jeremiah23:34 As for the prophet
Jeremiah23:35 This is what each man is to say to his friend and to his brother: What has the LORD answered? or What has the LORD spoken?
Jeremiah23:36 But no longer refer to the burden of the LORD
Jeremiah23:37 You must say to the prophet: What has the LORD answered you? and What has the LORD spoken?
Jeremiah23:38 But if you say: The burden of the LORD
Jeremiah23:39 I will surely forget you and throw away from My presence both you and the city that I gave you and your fathers.
Jeremiah23:40 I will bring on you everlasting shame and humiliation that will never be forgotten.

Jeremiah49:23 About Damascus: Hamath and Arpad are put to shame
Jeremiah49:24 Damascus has become weak; she has turned to run; panic has gripped her. Distress and labor pains have seized her like a woman in labor.
Jeremiah49:25 How can the city of praise not be abandoned
Jeremiah49:26 Therefore
Jeremiah49:27 I will set fire to the wall of Damascus; it will devour Ben-hadad's citadels.
Jeremiah49:28 About Kedar and the kingdoms of Hazor
Jeremiah49:29 They will take their tents and their flocks along with their tent curtains and all their equipment. They will take their camels for themselves. They will call out to them: Terror is on every side!
Jeremiah49:30 Run! Escape quickly! Lie low
Jeremiah49:31 Rise up
Jeremiah49:32 Their camels will become plunder
Jeremiah49:33 Hazor will become a jackals' den
Jeremiah49:34 [This is] the word of the LORD that came to Jeremiah the prophet about Elam at the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah.
Jeremiah49:35 This is what the LORD of Hosts says: I am about to shatter Elam's bow
Jeremiah49:36 I will bring the four winds against Elam from the four corners of the heavens
Jeremiah49:37 I will devastate Elam before their enemies
Jeremiah49:38 I will set My throne in Elam
Jeremiah49:39 In the last days
Jeremiah50:1 The word the LORD spoke about Babylon
Jeremiah50:2 Announce to the nations; proclaim and raise up a signal flag; proclaim
Jeremiah50:3 For a nation from the north will come against her; it will make her land desolate. No one will be living in it-- both man and beast will escape.
Jeremiah50:4 In those days and at that time-- [this is] the LORD's declaration-- the Israelites and Judeans will come together
Jeremiah50:5 They will ask about Zion
Jeremiah50:6 My people are lost sheep; their shepherds have led them astray
Jeremiah50:7 All who found them devoured them. Their adversaries said: We're not guilty; instead
Jeremiah50:8 Escape from Babylon; depart from the Chaldeans' land. Be like the rams that lead the flock.
Jeremiah50:9 For I will soon stir up and bring against Babylon an assembly of great nations from the north country. They will line up in battle formation against her; from there she will be captured. Their arrows will be like those of a skilled warrior who does not return empty-handed.
Jeremiah50:10 The Chaldeans will become plunder; all her plunderers will be fully satisfied. [This is] the LORD's declaration.
Jeremiah50:11 Because you rejoice
Jeremiah50:12 your mother will be utterly humiliated; she who bore you will be put to shame. Look! She will lag behind all the nations-- a dry land
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the LORD's wrath
Jeremiah50:14 Line up in battle formation around Babylon
Jeremiah50:15 Raise a war cry against her on every side! She has thrown up her hands [in surrender]; her defense towers have fallen; her walls are demolished. Since this is the LORD's vengeance
Jeremiah50:16 Cut off the sower from Babylon as well as him who wields the sickle at harvest time. Because of the oppressor's sword
Jeremiah50:17 Israel is a stray lamb
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore
Jeremiah50:19 I will return Israel to his grazing land
Jeremiah50:20 In those days and at that time-- [this is] the LORD's declaration-- one will search for Israel's guilt
Jeremiah50:21 Go against the land of Merathaim
Jeremiah50:22 The sound of war is in the land-- a great destruction.
Jeremiah50:23 How the hammer of the whole earth is cut down and smashed! What a horror Babylon has become among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 Babylon
Jeremiah50:25 The LORD opened His armory and brought out His weapons of wrath
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from the most distant places. Open her granaries; pile her up like mounds of grain and completely destroy her. Leave her no survivors.
Jeremiah50:27 Put all her young bulls to the sword; let them go down to the slaughter. Woe to them
Jeremiah50:28 [There is] a voice of fugitives and escapees from the land of Babylon announcing in Zion the vengeance of the LORD our God
Jeremiah50:29 Summon the archers to Babylon
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore
Jeremiah50:31 Look
Jeremiah50:32 The arrogant will stumble and fall with no one to pick him up. I will set fire to his cities
Jeremiah50:33 This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Israelites and Judeans alike have been oppressed. All their captors hold them fast; they refuse to release them.
Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong; the LORD of Hosts is His name. He will fervently plead their case so that He might bring rest to the earth but turmoil to those who live in Babylon.
Jeremiah50:35 A sword is over the Chaldeans-- [this is] the LORD's declaration-- against those who live in Babylon
Jeremiah50:36 A sword is against the diviners
Jeremiah50:37 A sword is against his horses and chariots and against all the foreigners among them
Jeremiah50:38 A drought will come on her waters
Jeremiah50:39 Therefore
Jeremiah50:40 Just as when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring towns-- [this is] the LORD's declaration-- so no one will live there; no human being will even stay in it as a resident alien.
Jeremiah50:41 Look! A people comes from the north. A great nation and many kings will be stirred up from the remote regions of the earth.
Jeremiah50:42 They grasp bow and javelin. They are cruel and show no mercy. Their voice roars like the sea
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon has heard reports about them
Jeremiah50:44 Look
Jeremiah50:45 Therefore
Jeremiah50:46 At the sound of Babylon's conquest the earth will quake; a cry will be heard among the nations.
Jeremiah51:1 This is what the LORD says: I am about to stir up a destructive wind against Babylon and against the population of Leb-qamai.
Jeremiah51:2 I will send strangers to Babylon who will scatter her and strip her land bare
Jeremiah51:3 Don't let the archer string his bow; don't let him put on his armor. Don't spare her young men; completely destroy her entire army!
Jeremiah51:4 Those who were slain will fall in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For Israel and Judah are not left widowed by their God
Jeremiah51:6 Leave Babylon; save your lives
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon was a golden cup in the LORD's hand making the whole earth drunk. The nations drank her wine; therefore
Jeremiah51:8 Suddenly Babylon fell and was shattered. Wail for her; get balm for her wound-- perhaps she can be healed.
Jeremiah51:9 We tried to heal Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 The LORD has brought about our vindication; come
Jeremiah51:11 Sharpen the arrows! Fill the quivers! The LORD has put it into the mind of the kings of the Medes because His plan is aimed at Babylon to destroy her
Jeremiah51:12 Raise up a signal flag against the walls of Babylon; fortify the watch post; set the watchmen in place; prepare the ambush. For the LORD has both planned and accomplished what He has threatened against those who live in Babylon.
Jeremiah51:13 You who reside by many waters
Jeremiah51:14 The LORD of Hosts has sworn by Himself: I will fill you up with men as with locusts
Jeremiah51:15 He made the earth by His power
Jeremiah51:16 When He thunders
Jeremiah51:17 Everyone is stupid and ignorant. Every goldsmith is put to shame by [his] carved image
Jeremiah51:18 They are worthless
Jeremiah51:19 Jacob's Portion is not like these because He is the One who formed all things. [Israel is] the tribe of His inheritance; the LORD of Hosts is His name.
Jeremiah51:20 You are My battle club
Jeremiah51:21 With you I will smash the horse and its rider; with you I will smash the chariot and its rider.
Jeremiah51:22 With you I will smash man and woman; with you I will smash the old man and the youth; with you I will smash the young man and the virgin.
Jeremiah51:23 With you I will smash the shepherd and his flock; with you I will smash the farmer and his ox-team. With you I will smash governors and officials.
Jeremiah51:24 I will repay Babylon and all the residents of Chaldea for all their evil they have done in Zion before your very eyes. [This is] the LORD's declaration.
Jeremiah51:25 Look
Jeremiah51:26 No one will be able to retrieve a cornerstone or a foundation stone from you
Jeremiah51:27 Raise a signal flag in the land; blow a ram's horn among the nations; set apart the nations against her. Summon kingdoms against her-- Ararat
Jeremiah51:28 Set apart the nations for battle against her-- the kings of Media
Jeremiah51:29 The earth quakes and trembles
Jeremiah51:30 Babylon's warriors have stopped fighting; they sit in their strongholds. Their might is exhausted; they have become like women. Babylon's homes have been set ablaze
Jeremiah51:31 Messenger races to meet messenger
Jeremiah51:32 The fords have been seized
Jeremiah51:33 For this is what the LORD of Hosts
Jeremiah51:34 Nebuchadnezzar of Babylon has devoured me; he has crushed me. He has set me aside like an empty dish; he has swallowed me like a sea monster; he filled his belly with my delicacies; he has vomited me out
Jeremiah51:35 says the inhabitant of Zion; Let the violence [done] to me and my family [be done] to Babylon. Let my blood be on the inhabitants of Chaldea
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore
Jeremiah51:37 Babylon will become a heap of rubble
Jeremiah51:38 They will roar together like young lions; they will growl like lion cubs.
Jeremiah51:39 While they are flushed with heat
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How Sheshach has been captured
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has risen over Babylon; she is covered with its turbulent waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities have become a desolation
Jeremiah51:44 I will punish Bel in Babylon. I will make him vomit what he swallowed. The nations will no longer stream to him; even Babylon's wall will fall.
Jeremiah51:45 Come out from among her
Jeremiah51:46 May you not become faint-hearted and fearful when the report is proclaimed in the land
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore
Jeremiah51:48 Heaven and earth and everything in them will shout for joy over Babylon because the destroyers from the north will come against her. [This is] the LORD's declaration.
Jeremiah51:49 Babylon must fall [because of] the slain of Israel
Jeremiah51:50 You who have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are ashamed because we have heard insults. Humiliation covers our faces because foreigners have entered the holy places of the LORD's temple.
Jeremiah51:52 Therefore
Jeremiah51:53 Even if Babylon should ascend to the heavens and fortify her tall fortresses
Jeremiah51:54 The sound of a cry from Babylon! The sound of great destruction from the land of the Chaldeans!
Jeremiah51:55 For the LORD is going to devastate Babylon; He will silence her mighty voice. Their waves roar like abundant waters; the tumult of their voice resounds
Jeremiah51:56 for a destroyer is coming against her
Jeremiah51:57 I will make her princes and sages drunk
Jeremiah51:58 This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Babylon's thick walls will be totally demolished
Jeremiah51:59 [This is] what Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah son of Neriah son of Mahseiah
Jeremiah51:60 Jeremiah wrote on one scroll about all the disaster that would come to Babylon; all these words were written against Babylon.
Jeremiah51:61 Jeremiah told Seraiah
Jeremiah51:62 You must say
Jeremiah51:63 When you have finished reading this scroll
Jeremiah51:64 Then say
Jeremiah52:1 Zedekiah was 21 years old when he became king; he reigned 11 years in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hamutal daughter of Jeremiah; [she was] from Libnah.
Jeremiah52:2 Zedekiah did what was evil in the LORD's sight just as Jehoiakim had done.
Jeremiah52:3 Because of the LORD's anger
Jeremiah52:4 In the ninth year of Zedekiah's reign
Jeremiah52:5 The city was under siege until King Zedekiah's eleventh year.
Jeremiah52:6 By the ninth day of the fourth month the famine was so severe in the city that the people of the land had no food.
Jeremiah52:7 Then the city was broken into
Jeremiah52:8 The Chaldean army pursued the king and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho. Zedekiah's entire army was scattered from him.
Jeremiah52:9 The Chaldeans seized the king and brought him to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the land of Hamath
Jeremiah52:10 At Riblah the king of Babylon slaughtered Zedekiah's sons before his eyes and also slaughtered the Judean commanders.
Jeremiah52:11 Then he blinded Zedekiah and bound him with bronze chains. The king of Babylon brought Zedekiah to Babylon
Jeremiah52:12 On the tenth day of the fifth month-- which was the nineteenth year of King Nebuchadnezzar
Jeremiah52:13 He burned the LORD's temple
Jeremiah52:14 The whole Chaldean army with the commander of the guards tore down all the walls surrounding Jerusalem.
Jeremiah52:15 Nebuzaradan
Jeremiah52:16 But some of the poor people of the land Nebuzaradan
Jeremiah52:17 Now the Chaldeans broke into pieces the bronze pillars for the LORD's temple and the water carts and the bronze reservoir that were in the LORD's temple
Jeremiah52:18 They took the pots
Jeremiah52:19 The commander of the guards took away the bowls
Jeremiah52:20 As for the two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 One pillar was 27 feet tall
Jeremiah52:22 and had a bronze capital on top of it. One capital
Jeremiah52:23 [Each capital had] 96 pomegranates all around it. All the pomegranates around the latticework numbered 100.
Jeremiah52:24 The commander of the guards also took away Seraiah the chief priest
Jeremiah52:25 From the city he took a court official who had been appointed over the warriors; seven trusted royal aides found in the city; the secretary of the commander of the army
Jeremiah52:26 Nebuzaradan
Jeremiah52:27 The king of Babylon put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah went into exile from its land.
Jeremiah52:28 These are the people Nebuchadnezzar deported: in the seventh year
Jeremiah52:29 in his eighteenth year
Jeremiah52:30 in Nebuchadnezzar's twenty-third year
Jeremiah52:31 On the twenty-fifth day of the twelfth month of the thirty-seventh year of the exile of Judah's King Jehoiachin
Jeremiah52:32 He spoke kindly to him and set his throne above the thrones of the kings who were with him in Babylon.
Jeremiah52:33 So Jehoiachin changed his prison clothes
Jeremiah52:34 As for his allowance
Lamentations1:1 How she sits alone
Lamentations1:2 She weeps aloud during the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into exile following affliction and harsh slavery; she lives among the nations but finds no place to rest. All her pursuers have overtaken her in narrow places.
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries have become [her] masters; her enemies are at ease
Lamentations1:6 All her splendor has vanished from Daughter Zion. Her leaders are like stags that find no pasture; they walk away exhausted before the hunter.
Lamentations1:7 During the days of her affliction and homelessness Jerusalem remembers all her precious belongings that were [hers] in days of old. When her people fell into the adversary's hand
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem has sinned grievously; therefore
Lamentations1:9 Her uncleanness [stains] her skirts. She never considered her end. Her downfall was astonishing; there was no one to comfort her. LORD
Lamentations1:10 The adversary has seized all her precious belongings. She has even seen the nations enter her sanctuary-- those You had forbidden to enter Your assembly.
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan while they search for bread. They have traded their precious belongings for food in order to stay alive. LORD
Lamentations1:12 Is this nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 He sent fire from on high into my bones; He made it descend. He spread a net for my feet and turned me back. He made me desolate
Lamentations1:14 My transgressions have been formed into a yoke
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has rejected all the mighty men within me. He has summoned an army against me to crush my young warriors. The Lord has trampled Virgin Daughter Judah [like grapes] in a winepress.
Lamentations1:16 I weep because of these things; my eyes flow with tears. For there is no one nearby to comfort [me]
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches out her hands; there is no one to comfort her. The LORD has issued a decree against Jacob that his neighbors should be his adversaries. Jerusalem has become something impure among them.
Lamentations1:18 The LORD is in the right
Lamentations1:19 I called to my lovers
Lamentations1:20 LORD
Lamentations1:21 People have heard me groaning
Lamentations1:22 Let all their wickedness come before You
Lamentations2:1 How the Lord has overshadowed Daughter Zion with His anger! He has thrown down Israel's glory from heaven to earth. He has abandoned His footstool in the day of His anger.
Lamentations2:2 Without compassion the Lord has swallowed up all the dwellings of Jacob. In His wrath He has demolished the fortified cities of Daughter Judah. He brought [them] to the ground and defiled the kingdom and its leaders.
Lamentations2:3 He has cut off every horn of Israel in His burning anger and withdrawn His right hand in the presence of the enemy. He has blazed against Jacob like a flaming fire that consumes everything [in its path].
Lamentations2:4 Like an enemy He has bent His bow; His right hand is positioned like an adversary. He has killed everyone who was loved
Lamentations2:5 The Lord is like an enemy; He has swallowed up Israel. He swallowed up all its palaces and destroyed its fortified cities. He has multiplied mourning and lamentation within Daughter Judah.
Lamentations2:6 He has done violence to His temple as if [it were] a garden [booth]
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has rejected His altar
Lamentations2:8 The LORD determined to destroy the wall of Daughter Zion. He stretched out a measuring line and did not restrain Himself from destroying. He made the ramparts and walls grieve; together they waste away.
Lamentations2:9 Zion's gates have fallen to the ground; He has destroyed and shattered the bars on her [gates]. Her king and her leaders [live] among the nations
Lamentations2:10 The elders of Daughter Zion sit on the ground in silence. They have thrown dust on their heads and put on sackcloth. The young women of Jerusalem have bowed their heads to the ground.
Lamentations2:11 My eyes are worn out from weeping; I am churning within. My heart is poured out in grief because of the destruction of my dear people
Lamentations2:12 They cry out to their mothers: Where is the grain and wine? as they faint like the wounded in the streets of the city
Lamentations2:13 What can I say on your behalf? To what can I compare you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets saw visions for you that were empty and deceptive; they did not reveal your guilt and so restore your fortunes. They saw oracles for you that were empty and misleading.
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by [scornfully] clap their hands at you. They hiss and shake their heads at Daughter Jerusalem: Is this the city that was called the perfection of beauty
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies open their mouths against you. They hiss and gnash [their] teeth
Lamentations2:17 The LORD has done what He planned; He has accomplished His decree
Lamentations2:18 The hearts of the people cry out to the Lord. Wall of Daughter Zion
Lamentations2:19 Arise
Lamentations2:20 LORD
Lamentations2:21 [Both] young and old are lying on the ground in the streets. My young men and women have fallen by the sword. You have killed [them] in the day of Your anger
Lamentations2:22 You summoned my attackers on every side
Lamentations3:1 I am the man who has seen affliction under the rod of God's wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He has driven me away and forced [me] to walk in darkness instead of light.
Lamentations3:3 Yes
Lamentations3:4 He has worn away my flesh and skin; He has shattered my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He has laid siege against me
Lamentations3:6 He has made me dwell in darkness like those who have been dead for ages.
Lamentations3:7 He has walled me in so I cannot escape; He has weighed me down with chains.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry out and plead for help
Lamentations3:9 He has walled in my ways with cut stones; He has made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He is a bear waiting in ambush
Lamentations3:11 He forced me off my way and tore me to pieces; He left me desolate.
Lamentations3:12 He bent His bow and set me as the target for His arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He pierced my kidneys with His arrows.
Lamentations3:14 I am a laughingstock to all my people
Lamentations3:15 He filled me with bitterness
Lamentations3:16 He ground my teeth on gravel and made me cower in the dust.
Lamentations3:17 My soul has been deprived of peace; I have forgotten what happiness is.
Lamentations3:18 Then I thought: My future is lost
Lamentations3:19 Remember my affliction and my homelessness
Lamentations3:20 I continually remember [them] and have become depressed.
Lamentations3:21 Yet I call this to mind
Lamentations3:22 [Because of] the LORD's faithful love we do not perish
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning; great is Your faithfulness!
Lamentations3:24 I say: The LORD is my portion
Lamentations3:25 The LORD is good to those who wait for Him
Lamentations3:26 It is good to wait quietly for deliverance from the LORD.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man to bear the yoke while he is [still] young.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone and be silent
Lamentations3:29 Let him put his mouth in the dust-- perhaps there is [still] hope.
Lamentations3:30 Let him offer [his] cheek to the one who would strike him; let him be filled with shame.
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not reject [us] forever.
Lamentations3:32 Even if He causes suffering
Lamentations3:33 For He does not enjoy bringing affliction or suffering on mankind.
Lamentations3:34 Crushing all the prisoners of the land beneath one's feet
Lamentations3:35 denying justice to a man in the presence of the Most High
Lamentations3:36 or suppressing a person's lawsuit-- the Lord does not approve [of these things].
Lamentations3:37 Who is there who speaks and it happens
Lamentations3:38 Do not both adversity and good come from the mouth of the Most High?
Lamentations3:39 Why should [any] living person complain
Lamentations3:40 Let us search out and examine our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our hearts and [our] hands to God in heaven:
Lamentations3:42 We have sinned and rebelled; You have not forgiven.
Lamentations3:43 You have covered Yourself in anger and pursued us; You have killed without compassion.
Lamentations3:44 You have covered Yourself with a cloud so that no prayer can pass through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us disgusting filth among the peoples.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies open their mouths against us.
Lamentations3:47 We have experienced panic and pitfall
Lamentations3:48 My eyes flow with streams of tears because of the destruction of my dear people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes overflow unceasingly
Lamentations3:50 until the LORD looks down from heaven and sees.
Lamentations3:51 My eyes bring me grief because of [the fate of] all the women in my city.
Lamentations3:52 For no [apparent] reason
Lamentations3:53 They dropped me alive into a pit and threw stones at me.
Lamentations3:54 Water flooded over my head
Lamentations3:55 I called on Your name
Lamentations3:56 You hear my plea: Do not ignore my cry for relief.
Lamentations3:57 You come near when I call on You; You say: Do not be afraid.
Lamentations3:58 You defend my cause
Lamentations3:59 LORD
Lamentations3:60 You see all their malice
Lamentations3:61 LORD
Lamentations3:62 The slander and murmuring of my opponents attack me all day long.
Lamentations3:63 When they sit and when they rise
Lamentations3:64 You will pay them back what they deserve
Lamentations3:65 You will give them a heart filled with anguish. May Your curse be on them!
Lamentations3:66 You will pursue [them] in anger and destroy them under Your heavens.
Lamentations4:1 How the gold has become tarnished
Lamentations4:2 Zion's precious people-- [once] worth their weight in pure gold-- how they are regarded as clay jars
Lamentations4:3 Even jackals offer [their] breasts to nurse their young
Lamentations4:4 The nursing infant's tongue clings to the roof of his mouth from thirst. Little children beg for bread
Lamentations4:5 Those who used to eat delicacies are destitute in the streets; those who were reared in purple [garments] huddle in garbage heaps.
Lamentations4:6 The punishment of my dear people is greater than that of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 Her dignitaries were brighter than snow
Lamentations4:8 [Now] they appear darker than soot; they are not recognized in the streets. Their skin has shriveled on their bones; it has become dry like wood.
Lamentations4:9 Those slain by the sword are better off than those slain by hunger
Lamentations4:10 The hands of compassionate women have cooked their own children; they became their food during the destruction of my dear people.
Lamentations4:11 The LORD has exhausted His wrath
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth and all the world's inhabitants did not believe that an enemy or adversary could enter Jerusalem's gates.
Lamentations4:13 [Yet it happened] because of the sins of her prophets and the guilt of her priests
Lamentations4:14 Blind
Lamentations4:15 Stay away! Unclean! people shouted at them. Away
Lamentations4:16 The LORD Himself has scattered them; He regards them no more. The priests are not respected; the elders find no favor.
Lamentations4:17 All the while our eyes were failing [as we looked] in vain for assistance; we watched from our towers for a nation that refused to help.
Lamentations4:18 Our steps were closely followed
Lamentations4:19 Those who chased us were swifter than eagles in the sky; they relentlessly pursued us over the mountains and ambushed us in the wilderness.
Lamentations4:20 The LORD's anointed
Lamentations4:21 So rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 Daughter Zion
Lamentations5:1 Yahweh
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has been turned over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans
Lamentations5:4 We must pay for the water we drink; our wood comes at a price.
Lamentations5:5 We are closely pursued; we are tired
Lamentations5:6 We made a treaty with Egypt and with Assyria
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned; they no longer exist
Lamentations5:8 Slaves rule over us; no one rescues [us] from their hands.
Lamentations5:9 We secure our food at the risk of our lives because of the sword in the wilderness.
Lamentations5:10 Our skin is as hot as an oven from the ravages of hunger.
Lamentations5:11 Women are raped in Zion
Lamentations5:12 Princes are hung up by their hands; elders are shown no respect.
Lamentations5:13 Young men labor at millstones; boys stumble under [loads of] wood.
Lamentations5:14 The elders have left the city gate
Lamentations5:15 Joy has left our hearts; our dancing has turned to mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head. Woe to us
Lamentations5:17 Because of this
Lamentations5:18 because of Mount Zion
Lamentations5:19 You
Lamentations5:20 Why have You forgotten us forever
Lamentations5:21 LORD
Lamentations5:22 unless You have completely rejected us and are intensely angry with us.
Ezekiel1:1 In the thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth [day] of the month-- it was the fifth year of King Jehoiachin's exile--
Ezekiel1:3 the word of the LORD came directly to Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 I looked and there was a whirlwind coming from the north
Ezekiel1:5 The form of four living creatures came from it. And this was their appearance: They had human form
Ezekiel1:6 but each of them had four faces and four wings.
Ezekiel1:7 Their legs were straight
Ezekiel1:8 [They had] human hands under their wings on their four sides. All four of them had faces and wings.
Ezekiel1:9 Their wings were touching. The creatures did not turn as they moved; each one went straight ahead.
Ezekiel1:10 The form of [each of] their faces was that of a man
Ezekiel1:11 [That is what] their faces [were like]. Their wings were spread upward; each had two [wings] touching that of another and two wings covering its body.
Ezekiel1:12 Each creature went straight ahead. Wherever the Spirit wanted to go
Ezekiel1:13 The form of the living creatures was like the appearance of burning coals of fire and torches. Fire was moving back and forth between the living creatures; it was bright
Ezekiel1:14 The creatures were darting back and forth like flashes of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 When I looked at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 The appearance of the wheels and their craftsmanship was like the gleam of beryl
Ezekiel1:17 When they moved
Ezekiel1:18 Their rims were large and frightening. Each of their four rims were full of eyes all around.
Ezekiel1:19 So when the living creatures moved
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the Spirit wanted to go
Ezekiel1:21 When the creatures moved
Ezekiel1:22 The shape of an expanse
Ezekiel1:23 And under the expanse their wings extended one toward another. Each of them also had two wings covering their bodies.
Ezekiel1:24 When they moved
Ezekiel1:25 A voice came from above the expanse over their heads; when they stood still
Ezekiel1:26 The shape of a throne with the appearance of sapphire stone was above the expanse. There was a form with the appearance of a human on the throne high above.
Ezekiel1:27 From what seemed to be His waist up
Ezekiel1:28 The appearance of the brilliant light all around was like that of a rainbow in a cloud on a rainy day. This was the appearance of the form of the LORD's glory. When I saw [it]
Ezekiel2:1 He said to me
Ezekiel2:2 As He spoke to me
Ezekiel2:3 He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel2:4 The children are obstinate and hardhearted. I am sending you to them
Ezekiel2:5 Whether they listen or refuse [to listen]-- for they are a rebellious house-- they will know that a prophet has been among them.
Ezekiel2:6 But you
Ezekiel2:7 But speak My words to them whether they listen or refuse [to listen]
Ezekiel2:8 And you
Ezekiel2:9 So I looked and saw a hand reaching out to me
Ezekiel2:10 When He unrolled it before me
Ezekiel3:1 He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel3:2 So I opened my mouth
Ezekiel3:3 Son of man
Ezekiel3:4 Then He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel3:5 For you are not being sent to a people of unintelligible speech or difficult language but to the house of Israel.
Ezekiel3:6 [You are] not [being sent] to many peoples of unintelligible speech or difficult language
Ezekiel3:7 But the house of Israel will not want to listen to you because they do not want to listen to Me. For the whole house of Israel is hardheaded and hardhearted.
Ezekiel3:8 Look
Ezekiel3:9 I have made your forehead like a diamond
Ezekiel3:10 Next He said to me: Son of man
Ezekiel3:11 Go to your people
Ezekiel3:12 The Spirit then lifted me up
Ezekiel3:13 with the sound of the living creatures' wings brushing against each other and the sound of the wheels beside them
Ezekiel3:14 So the Spirit lifted me up and took me away. I left in bitterness and in an angry spirit
Ezekiel3:15 I came to the exiles at Tel-abib
Ezekiel3:16 Now at the end of seven days the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel3:17 Son of man
Ezekiel3:18 If I say to the wicked person: You will surely die
Ezekiel3:19 But if you warn a wicked person and he does not turn from his wickedness or his wicked way
Ezekiel3:20 Now if a righteous person turns from his righteousness and practices iniquity
Ezekiel3:21 But if you warn the righteous person that he should not sin
Ezekiel3:22 Then the hand of the LORD was on me there
Ezekiel3:23 So I got up and went out to the plain. The LORD's glory was present there
Ezekiel3:24 The Spirit entered me and set me on my feet. He spoke with me and said: Go
Ezekiel3:25 And you
Ezekiel3:26 I will make your tongue stick to the roof of your mouth
Ezekiel3:27 But when I speak with you
Ezekiel4:1 Now you
Ezekiel4:2 Then lay siege against it: construct a siege wall
Ezekiel4:3 Take an iron plate and set it up as an iron wall between yourself and the city. Turn your face toward it so that it is under siege
Ezekiel4:4 Then lie down on your left side and place the iniquity of the house of Israel on it. You will bear their iniquity for the number of days you lie on your side.
Ezekiel4:5 For I have assigned you the years of their iniquity according to the number of days [you lie down]
Ezekiel4:6 When you have completed these days
Ezekiel4:7 You must turn your face toward the siege of Jerusalem with your arm bared
Ezekiel4:8 Be aware that I will put cords on you so you cannot turn from side to side until you have finished the days of your siege.
Ezekiel4:9 Also take wheat
Ezekiel4:10 The food you eat each day will be eight ounces by weight; you will eat it from time to time.
Ezekiel4:11 You are also to drink water by measure
Ezekiel4:12 You will eat it as [you would] a barley cake and bake it over dried human excrement in their sight.
Ezekiel4:13 The LORD said
Ezekiel4:14 But I said
Ezekiel4:15 He replied to me
Ezekiel4:16 Then He said to me
Ezekiel4:17 So they will lack bread and water; everyone will be devastated and waste away because of their iniquity.
Ezekiel5:1 Now you
Ezekiel5:2 You are to burn up one third [of it] in the city when the days of the siege have ended; you are to take one third and slash [it] with the sword all around the city; and you are to scatter one third to the wind
Ezekiel5:3 But you are to take a few strands from the hair and secure them in the folds of your [robe].
Ezekiel5:4 Take some more of them
Ezekiel5:5 This is what the Lord God says: I have set this Jerusalem in the center of the nations
Ezekiel5:6 But she has rebelled against My ordinances with more wickedness than the nations
Ezekiel5:7 Therefore
Ezekiel5:8 therefore
Ezekiel5:9 Because of all your abominations
Ezekiel5:10 As a result
Ezekiel5:11 Therefore
Ezekiel5:12 One third of your people will die by plague and be consumed by famine within you; one third will fall by the sword all around you; and I will scatter one third to every direction of the wind
Ezekiel5:13 When My anger is spent and I have vented My wrath on them
Ezekiel5:14 I will make you a ruin and a disgrace among the nations around you
Ezekiel5:15 So you will be a disgrace and a taunt
Ezekiel5:16 When I shoot deadly arrows of famine at them
Ezekiel5:17 I will send famine and dangerous animals against you. They will leave you childless
Ezekiel6:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel6:2 Son of man
Ezekiel6:3 You are to say: Mountains of Israel
Ezekiel6:4 Your altars will be desolated and your incense altars smashed. I will throw down your slain in front of your idols.
Ezekiel6:5 I will lay the corpses of the Israelites in front of their idols and scatter your bones around your altars.
Ezekiel6:6 Wherever you live the cities will be in ruins and the high places will be desolate
Ezekiel6:7 The slain will fall among you
Ezekiel6:8 Yet I will leave a remnant when you are scattered among the nations
Ezekiel6:9 Then your survivors will remember Me among the nations where they are taken captive
Ezekiel6:10 And they will know that I am the LORD; I did not threaten to bring this disaster on them without a reason.
Ezekiel6:11 This is what the Lord God says: Clap your hands
Ezekiel6:12 The one who is far off will die by plague; the one who is near will fall by the sword; and the one who remains and is spared will die of famine. In this way I will exhaust My wrath on them.
Ezekiel6:13 You will [all] know that I am the LORD when their slain lie among their idols around their altars
Ezekiel6:14 I will stretch out My hand against them
Ezekiel7:1 And the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel7:2 Son of man
Ezekiel7:3 The end is now on you; I will send My anger against you and judge you according to your ways. I will punish you for all your abominations.
Ezekiel7:4 I will not look on you with pity or spare [you]
Ezekiel7:5 This is what the Lord God says: Look
Ezekiel7:6 An end has come; the end has come! It has awakened against you. Look
Ezekiel7:7 Doom has come on you
Ezekiel7:8 I will pour out My wrath on you very soon; I will exhaust My anger against you and judge you according to your ways. I will punish you for all your abominations.
Ezekiel7:9 I will not look on [you] with pity or spare [you]. I will punish you for your ways and for your abominations within you. Then you will know that it is I
Ezekiel7:10 Look
Ezekiel7:11 Violence has grown into a rod of wickedness; none of them [will remain]: none of their multitude
Ezekiel7:12 The time has come; the day has arrived. Let the buyer not rejoice and the seller not mourn
Ezekiel7:13 The seller will certainly not return to what was sold as long as he and the buyer remain alive. For the vision concerning all its people will not be revoked
Ezekiel7:14 They have blown the trumpet and prepared everything
Ezekiel7:15 The sword is on the outside; plague and famine are on the inside. Whoever is in the field will die by the sword
Ezekiel7:16 The survivors among them will escape and live on the mountains like doves of the valley
Ezekiel7:17 All their hands will become weak
Ezekiel7:18 They will put on sackcloth
Ezekiel7:19 They will throw their silver into the streets
Ezekiel7:20 He appointed His beautiful ornaments for majesty
Ezekiel7:21 I will hand these things over to foreigners as plunder and to the wicked of the earth as spoil
Ezekiel7:22 I will turn My face from the wicked as they profane My treasured place. Violent men will enter it and profane it.
Ezekiel7:23 Forge the chain
Ezekiel7:24 So I will bring the most evil of nations to take possession of their houses. I will put an end to the pride of the strong
Ezekiel7:25 Anguish is coming! They will seek peace
Ezekiel7:26 Disaster after disaster will come
Ezekiel7:27 The king will mourn; the prince will be clothed in grief; and the hands of the people of the land will tremble. I will deal with them according to their own conduct

Ezekiel9:4 Pass throughout the city of Jerusalem

Ezekiel9:9 He answered me: The iniquity of the house of Israel and Judah is extremely great; the land is full of bloodshed

Ezekiel10:1 Then I looked
Ezekiel10:2 The LORD spoke to the man clothed in linen and said
Ezekiel10:3 Now the cherubim were standing to the south of the temple when the man went in
Ezekiel10:4 Then the glory of the LORD rose from above the cherubim to the threshold of the temple. The temple was filled with the cloud
Ezekiel10:5 The sound of the cherubim's wings could be heard as far as the outer court; it was like the voice of God Almighty when He speaks.
Ezekiel10:6 After the LORD commanded the man clothed in linen
Ezekiel10:7 Then one of the cherubim reached out his hand to the fire that was among them. He took [some]
Ezekiel10:8 The cherubim appeared to have the form of human hands under their wings.
Ezekiel10:9 I looked
Ezekiel10:10 In appearance
Ezekiel10:11 When they moved
Ezekiel10:12 Their entire bodies
Ezekiel10:13 As I listened the wheels were called the wheelwork.
Ezekiel10:14 Each one had four faces: the first face was that of a cherub
Ezekiel10:15 The cherubim ascended; these were the living creatures I had seen by the Chebar Canal.
Ezekiel10:16 When the cherubim moved
Ezekiel10:17 When the cherubim stood still
Ezekiel10:18 Then the glory of the LORD moved away from the threshold of the temple and stood above the cherubim.
Ezekiel10:19 The cherubim lifted their wings and ascended from the earth right before my eyes; the wheels were beside them as they went. The glory of the God of Israel was above them
Ezekiel10:20 These were the living creatures I had seen beneath the God of Israel by the Chebar Canal
Ezekiel10:21 Each had four faces and each had four wings
Ezekiel10:22 Their faces looked like the same faces I had seen by the Chebar Canal. Each creature went straight ahead.
Ezekiel11:1 The Spirit then lifted me up and brought me to the eastern gate of the LORD's house
Ezekiel11:2 The LORD said to me
Ezekiel11:3 They are saying: Isn't the time near to build houses? The city is the pot
Ezekiel11:4 Therefore
Ezekiel11:5 Then the Spirit of the LORD came on me
Ezekiel11:6 You have multiplied your slain in this city
Ezekiel11:7 Therefore
Ezekiel11:8 You fear the sword
Ezekiel11:9 I will bring you out of the city and hand you over to foreigners; I will execute judgments against you.
Ezekiel11:10 You will fall by the sword
Ezekiel11:11 The city will not be a pot for you
Ezekiel11:12 so you will know that I am the LORD
Ezekiel11:13 Now while I was prophesying
Ezekiel11:14 The word of the LORD came to me again:
Ezekiel11:15 Son of man
Ezekiel11:16 Therefore say: This is what the Lord God says: Though I sent them far away among the nations and scattered them among the countries
Ezekiel11:17 Therefore say: This is what the Lord God says: I will gather you from the peoples and assemble you from the countries where you have been scattered
Ezekiel11:18 When they arrive there
Ezekiel11:19 And I will give them one heart and put a new spirit within them; I will remove their heart of stone from their bodies and give them a heart of flesh
Ezekiel11:20 so they may follow My statutes
Ezekiel11:21 But as for those whose hearts pursue their desire for detestable things and abominations
Ezekiel11:22 Then the cherubim
Ezekiel11:23 The glory of the LORD rose up from within the city and stood on the mountain east of the city.
