Ezekiel11:25 I spoke to the exiles about all the things the LORD had shown me.
Ezekiel12:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel12:2 Son of man
Ezekiel12:3 Son of man
Ezekiel12:4 During the day
Ezekiel12:5 As they watch
Ezekiel12:6 And while they look on
Ezekiel12:7 So I did just as I was commanded. I brought out my bags like an exile's bags in the daytime. In the evening I dug through the wall by hand; I took [them] out in the dark
Ezekiel12:8 Then the word of the LORD came to me in the morning:
Ezekiel12:9 Son of man
Ezekiel12:10 Say to them: This is what the Lord God says: This oracle is about the prince in Jerusalem and all the house of Israel who are living there.
Ezekiel12:11 You are to say: I am a sign for you. Just as I have done
Ezekiel12:12 The prince who is among them will lift [his bags] to his shoulder in the dark and go out. They will dig through the wall to bring [him] out through it. He will cover his face so he cannot see the land with his eyes.
Ezekiel12:13 But I will spread My net over him
Ezekiel12:14 I will also scatter all the attendants who surround him and all his troops to every direction of the wind
Ezekiel12:15 They will know that I am the LORD when I disperse them among the nations and scatter them among the countries.
Ezekiel12:16 But I will spare a few of them from the sword
Ezekiel12:17 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel12:18 Son of man
Ezekiel12:19 Then say to the people of the land: This is what the Lord God says about the residents of Jerusalem in the land of Israel: They will eat their bread with anxiety and drink their water in dread
Ezekiel12:20 The inhabited cities will be destroyed
Ezekiel12:21 Again the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel12:22 Son of man
Ezekiel12:23 Therefore say to them: This is what the Lord God says: I will put a stop to this proverb
Ezekiel12:24 For there will no longer be any false vision or flattering divination within the house of Israel.
Ezekiel12:25 But I
Ezekiel12:26 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel12:27 Son of man
Ezekiel12:28 Therefore say to them: This is what the Lord God says: None of My words will be delayed any longer. The message I speak will be fulfilled. [This is] the declaration of the Lord God.
Ezekiel13:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel13:2 Son of man
Ezekiel13:3 This is what the Lord God says: Woe to the foolish prophets who follow their own spirit and have seen nothing.
Ezekiel13:4 Your prophets
Ezekiel13:5 You did not go up to the gaps or restore the wall around the house of Israel so that it might stand in battle on the day of the LORD.
Ezekiel13:6 They see false visions and speak lying divinations. They claim: [This is] the LORD's declaration
Ezekiel13:7 Didn't you see a false vision and speak a lying divination when you proclaimed: [This is] the LORD's declaration
Ezekiel13:8 Therefore
Ezekiel13:9 My hand will be against the prophets who see false visions and speak lying divinations. They will not be present in the fellowship of My people or be recorded in the register of the house of Israel
Ezekiel13:10 Since they have led My people astray saying: Peace
Ezekiel13:11 therefore
Ezekiel13:12 Now when the wall has fallen
Ezekiel13:13 So this is what the Lord God says: I will release a windstorm in My wrath. Torrential rain will come in My anger
Ezekiel13:14 I will tear down the wall you plastered with whitewash and knock it to the ground so that its foundation is exposed. The city will fall
Ezekiel13:15 After I exhaust My wrath against the wall and against those who plaster it with whitewash
Ezekiel13:16 those prophets of Israel who prophesied to Jerusalem and saw a vision of peace for her when there was no peace. [This is] the declaration of the Lord God.
Ezekiel13:17 Now
Ezekiel13:18 and say: This is what the Lord God says: Woe to the women who sew [magic] bands on the wrist of every hand and who make veils for the heads of people of every height in order to ensnare lives. Will you ensnare the lives of My people but preserve your own?
Ezekiel13:19 You profane Me in front of My people for handfuls of barley and scraps of bread; you kill those who should not die and spare those who should not live
Ezekiel13:20 Therefore
Ezekiel13:21 I will also tear off your veils and deliver My people from your hands
Ezekiel13:22 Because you have disheartened the righteous person with lies
Ezekiel13:23 therefore you will no longer see false visions or practice divination. I will deliver My people from your hands. Then you will know that I am the LORD.
Ezekiel14:1 Some of the elders of Israel came to me and sat down in front of me.
Ezekiel14:2 Then the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel14:3 Son of man
Ezekiel14:4 Therefore
Ezekiel14:5 so that I may take hold of the house of Israel by their hearts
Ezekiel14:6 Therefore
Ezekiel14:7 For when anyone from the house of Israel or from the foreigners who reside in Israel separates himself from Me
Ezekiel14:8 I will turn against that one and make him a sign and a proverb; I will cut him off from among My people. Then you will know that I am the LORD.
Ezekiel14:9 But if the prophet is deceived and speaks a message
Ezekiel14:10 They will bear their punishment-- the punishment of the one who inquires will be the same as that of the prophet--
Ezekiel14:11 in order that the house of Israel may no longer stray from following Me and no longer defile themselves with all their transgressions. Then they will be My people and I will be their God. [This is] the declaration of the Lord God.
Ezekiel14:12 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel14:13 Son of man
Ezekiel14:14 even [if] these three men-- Noah
Ezekiel14:15 If I allow dangerous animals to pass through the land and depopulate it so that it becomes desolate
Ezekiel14:16 even [if] these three men were in it
Ezekiel14:17 Or if I bring a sword against that land and say: Let a sword pass through it
Ezekiel14:18 even [if] these three men were in it
Ezekiel14:19 Or if I send a plague into that land and pour out My wrath on it with bloodshed to wipe out [both] man and animal from it
Ezekiel14:20 even [if] Noah
Ezekiel14:21 For this is what the Lord God says: How much worse will it be when I send My four devastating judgments against Jerusalem-- sword
Ezekiel14:22 Even so
Ezekiel14:23 They will bring you consolation when you see their conduct and actions
Ezekiel15:1 Then the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel15:2 Son of man
Ezekiel15:3 Can wood be taken from it to make something useful? Or can anyone make a peg from it to hang things on?
Ezekiel15:4 In fact
Ezekiel15:5 Even when it was whole it could not be made into a useful object. How much less can it ever be made into anything useful when the fire has devoured it and it is charred!
Ezekiel15:6 Therefore
Ezekiel15:7 I will turn against them. They may have escaped from the fire
Ezekiel15:8 I will make the land desolate because they have acted unfaithfully. [This is] the declaration of the Lord God.
Ezekiel16:1 The word of the LORD came to me again:
Ezekiel16:2 Son of man
Ezekiel16:3 You are to say: This is what the Lord God says to Jerusalem: Your origin and your birth were in the land of the Canaanites. Your father was an Amorite and your mother a Hittite.
Ezekiel16:4 As for your birth
Ezekiel16:5 No one cared [enough] about you to do even one of these things out of compassion for you. But you were thrown out into the open field because you were despised on the day you were born.
Ezekiel16:6 I passed by you and saw you lying in your blood
Ezekiel16:7 I made you thrive like plants of the field. You grew up and matured and became very beautiful. Your breasts were formed and your hair grew
Ezekiel16:8 Then I passed by you and saw you
Ezekiel16:9 I washed you with water
Ezekiel16:10 I clothed you in embroidered cloth and provided you with leather sandals. I also wrapped you in fine linen and covered you with silk.
Ezekiel16:11 I adorned you with jewelry
Ezekiel16:12 I put a ring in your nose
Ezekiel16:13 So you were adorned with gold and silver
Ezekiel16:14 Your fame spread among the nations because of your beauty
Ezekiel16:15 But you were confident in your beauty and acted like a prostitute because of your fame. You lavished your sexual favors on everyone who passed by. Your beauty became his.
Ezekiel16:16 You took some of your garments and made colorful high places for yourself
Ezekiel16:17 You also took your beautiful jewelry made from the gold and silver I had given you
Ezekiel16:18 Then you took your embroidered garments to cover them
Ezekiel16:19 You also set before them as a pleasing aroma the food I gave you-- the fine flour
Ezekiel16:20 You even took your sons and daughters you bore to Me and sacrificed them to these images as food. Wasn't your prostitution enough?
Ezekiel16:21 You slaughtered My children and gave them up when you passed them through [the fire] to the images.
Ezekiel16:22 In all your abominations and acts of prostitution
Ezekiel16:23 Then after all your evil-- Woe
Ezekiel16:24 you built yourself a mound and made yourself an elevated place in every square.
Ezekiel16:25 You built your elevated place at the head of every street and turned your beauty into an abomination. You spread your legs to everyone who passed by and increased your prostitution.
Ezekiel16:26 You engaged in promiscuous acts with Egyptian men
Ezekiel16:27 Therefore
Ezekiel16:28 Then you engaged in prostitution with the Assyrian men because you were not satisfied. Even though you did this with them
Ezekiel16:29 So you extended your prostitution to Chaldea
Ezekiel16:30 How your heart was inflamed [with lust]-- the declaration of the Lord God-- when you did all these things
Ezekiel16:31 building your mound at the head of every street and making your elevated place in every square. But you were unlike a prostitute because you scorned payment.
Ezekiel16:32 You adulterous wife
Ezekiel16:33 Men give gifts to all prostitutes
Ezekiel16:34 So you were the opposite of other women in your acts of prostitution; no one solicited you. When you paid a fee instead of one being paid to you
Ezekiel16:35 Therefore
Ezekiel16:36 This is what the Lord God says: Because your lust was poured out and your nakedness exposed by your acts of prostitution with your lovers
Ezekiel16:37 I am therefore going to gather all the lovers you pleased-- all those you loved as well as all those you hated. I will gather them against you from all around and expose your nakedness to them so they see you completely naked.
Ezekiel16:38 I will judge you the way adulteresses and those who shed blood are judged. Then I will bring about your bloodshed in wrath and jealousy.
Ezekiel16:39 I will hand you over to them
Ezekiel16:40 They will bring a mob against you to stone you and cut you to pieces with their swords.
Ezekiel16:41 Then they will burn down your houses and execute judgments against you in the sight of many women. I will stop you from being a prostitute
Ezekiel16:42 So I will satisfy My wrath against you
Ezekiel16:43 Because you did not remember the days of your youth but enraged Me with all these things
Ezekiel16:44 Look
Ezekiel16:45 You are the daughter of your mother
Ezekiel16:46 Your older sister was Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 Didn't you walk in their ways and practice their abominations? It was only a short time before you behaved more corruptly than they did.
Ezekiel16:48 As I live-- the declaration of the Lord God-- your sister Sodom and her daughters have not behaved as you and your daughters have.
Ezekiel16:49 Now this was the iniquity of your sister Sodom: she and her daughters had pride
Ezekiel16:50 They were haughty and did detestable things before Me
Ezekiel16:51 But Samaria did not commit [even] half your sins. You have multiplied your abominations beyond theirs and made your sisters appear righteous by all the abominations you have committed.
Ezekiel16:52 You must also bear your disgrace
Ezekiel16:53 I will restore their fortunes
Ezekiel16:54 so you will bear your disgrace and be ashamed of all you did when you comforted them.
Ezekiel16:55 As for your sisters
Ezekiel16:56 Didn't you treat your sister Sodom as an object of scorn when you were proud
Ezekiel16:57 before your wickedness was exposed? It was like the time you were scorned by the daughters of Aram and all those around her
Ezekiel16:58 You yourself must bear the consequences of your indecency and abominations-- the LORD's declaration.
Ezekiel16:59 For this is what the Lord God says: I will deal with you according to what you have done
Ezekiel16:60 But I will remember the covenant I made with you in the days of your youth
Ezekiel16:61 Then you will remember your ways and be ashamed when you receive your older and younger sisters. I will give them to you as daughters
Ezekiel16:62 I will establish My covenant with you
Ezekiel16:63 so that when I make atonement for all you have done
Ezekiel17:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel17:2 Son of man
Ezekiel17:3 You are to say: This is what the Lord God says: A great eagle with great wings
Ezekiel17:4 He plucked off its topmost shoot
Ezekiel17:5 Then he took some of the land's seed and put it in a fertile field; he set it [like] a willow
Ezekiel17:6 It sprouted and became a spreading vine
Ezekiel17:7 But there was another great eagle with great wings and thick plumage. And this vine bent its roots toward him! It stretched out its branches to him from its planting bed
Ezekiel17:8 It had been planted in a good field by abundant waters in order to produce branches
Ezekiel17:9 You are to say: This is what the Lord God says: Will it flourish? Will he not tear out its roots and strip off its fruit so that it shrivels? All its fresh leaves will wither! Great strength and many people will not be needed to pull it from its roots.
Ezekiel17:10 Even though it is planted
Ezekiel17:11 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel17:12 Now say to that rebellious house: Don't you know what these things mean? Tell [them]: The king of Babylon came to Jerusalem
Ezekiel17:13 He took one of the royal family and made a covenant with him
Ezekiel17:14 so the kingdom might be humble and not exalt itself but might keep his covenant in order to endure.
Ezekiel17:15 However
Ezekiel17:16 As I live-- [this is] the declaration of the Lord God-- he will die in Babylon
Ezekiel17:17 Pharaoh will not help him with [his] great army and vast horde in battle
Ezekiel17:18 He despised the oath by breaking the covenant. He did all these things even though he gave his hand [in pledge]. He will not escape!
Ezekiel17:19 Therefore
Ezekiel17:20 I will spread My net over him
Ezekiel17:21 All the fugitives among his troops will fall by the sword
Ezekiel17:22 This is what the Lord God says: I will take [a sprig] from the lofty top of the cedar and plant [it]. I will pluck a tender sprig from its topmost shoots
Ezekiel17:23 I will plant it on Israel's high mountain so that it may bear branches
Ezekiel17:24 Then all the trees of the field will know that I am the LORD. I bring down the tall tree
Ezekiel18:1 The word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel18:2 What do you mean by using this proverb concerning the land of Israel: The fathers eat sour grapes
Ezekiel18:3 As I live-- [this is] the declaration of the Lord God-- you will no longer use this proverb in Israel.
Ezekiel18:4 Look
Ezekiel18:5 Now suppose a man is righteous and does what is just and right:
Ezekiel18:6 He does not eat at the mountain [shrines] or raise his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel. He does not defile his neighbor's wife or come near a woman during her menstrual impurity.
Ezekiel18:7 He doesn't oppress anyone but returns his collateral to the debtor. He does not commit robbery
Ezekiel18:8 He doesn't lend at interest or for profit but keeps his hand from wrongdoing and carries out true justice between men.
Ezekiel18:9 He follows My statutes and keeps My ordinances
Ezekiel18:10 Now suppose the man has a violent son
Ezekiel18:11 though the father has done none of them. Indeed
Ezekiel18:12 and [when] he oppresses the poor and needy
Ezekiel18:13 and lends at interest or for profit
Ezekiel18:14 Now suppose he has a son who sees all the sins his father has committed
Ezekiel18:15 He does not eat at the mountain [shrines] or raise his eyes to the idols of the house of Israel. He does not defile his neighbor's wife.
Ezekiel18:16 He doesn't oppress anyone
Ezekiel18:17 He keeps his hand from [harming] the poor
Ezekiel18:18 As for his father
Ezekiel18:19 But you may ask: Why doesn't the son suffer punishment for the father's iniquity? Since the son has done what is just and right
Ezekiel18:20 The person who sins is the one who will die. A son won't suffer punishment for the father's iniquity
Ezekiel18:21 Now if the wicked person turns from all the sins he has committed
Ezekiel18:22 None of the transgressions he has committed will be held against him. He will live because of the righteousness he has practiced.
Ezekiel18:23 Do I take any pleasure in the death of the wicked? [This is] the declaration of the Lord God. Instead
Ezekiel18:24 But when a righteous person turns from his righteousness and practices iniquity
Ezekiel18:25 But you say: The Lord's way isn't fair. Now listen
Ezekiel18:26 When a righteous person turns from his righteousness and practices iniquity
Ezekiel18:27 But if a wicked person turns from the wickedness he has committed and does what is just and right
Ezekiel18:28 He will certainly live because he thought it over and turned from all the transgressions he had committed; he will not die.
Ezekiel18:29 But the house of Israel says: The Lord's way isn't fair. Is it My ways that are unfair
Ezekiel18:30 Therefore
Ezekiel18:31 Throw off all the transgressions you have committed
Ezekiel18:32 For I take no pleasure in anyone's death. [This is] the declaration of the Lord God. So repent and live!
Ezekiel19:1 Now
Ezekiel19:2 and say: What was your mother? A lioness! She lay down among the lions; she reared her cubs among the young lions.
Ezekiel19:3 She brought up one of her cubs
Ezekiel19:4 When the nations heard about him
Ezekiel19:5 When she saw that she waited [in vain]
Ezekiel19:6 He prowled among the lions
Ezekiel19:7 He devastated their strongholds and destroyed their cities. The land and everything in it shuddered at the sound of his roaring.
Ezekiel19:8 Then the nations from the surrounding provinces set out against him. They spread their net over him; he was caught in their pit.
Ezekiel19:9 They put a wooden yoke on him with hooks and led him away to the king of Babylon. They brought him into the fortresses so his roar could no longer be heard on the mountains of Israel.
Ezekiel19:10 Your mother was like a vine in your vineyard
Ezekiel19:11 It had strong branches
Ezekiel19:12 But it was uprooted in fury
Ezekiel19:13 Now it is planted in the wilderness
Ezekiel19:14 Fire has gone out from its main branch and has devoured its fruit
Ezekiel20:1 In the seventh year
Ezekiel20:2 Then the word of the LORD came to me:
Ezekiel20:3 Son of man
Ezekiel20:4 Will you pass judgment against them
Ezekiel20:5 Say to them: This is what the Lord God says: On the day I chose Israel
Ezekiel20:6 On that day I swore to them that I would bring them out of the land of Egypt into a land I had searched out for them
Ezekiel20:7 I also said to them: Each of you must throw away the detestable things that are before your eyes and not defile yourselves with the idols of Egypt. I am the LORD your God.
Ezekiel20:8 But they rebelled against Me and were unwilling to listen to Me. None of them threw away the detestable things that were before their eyes
Ezekiel20:9 But I acted for the sake of My name
Ezekiel20:10 So I brought them out of the land of Egypt and led them into the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:11 Then I gave them My statutes and explained My ordinances to them-- the person who does them will live by them.
Ezekiel20:12 I also gave them My Sabbaths to serve as a sign between Me and them
Ezekiel20:13 But the house of Israel rebelled against Me in the wilderness. They did not follow My statutes and they rejected My ordinances-- the person who does them will live by them. They also completely profaned My Sabbaths. So I considered pouring out My wrath on them in the wilderness to put an end to them.
Ezekiel20:14 But I acted because of My name
Ezekiel20:15 However
Ezekiel20:16 because they rejected My ordinances
Ezekiel20:17 But I spared them from destruction and did not bring them to an end in the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:18 Then I said to their children in the wilderness: Don't follow the statutes of your fathers
Ezekiel20:19 I am the LORD your God. Follow My statutes
Ezekiel20:20 Keep My Sabbaths holy
Ezekiel20:21 But the children rebelled against Me. They did not follow My statutes or carefully keep My ordinances-- the person who does them will live by them. They also profaned My Sabbaths. So I considered pouring out My wrath on them and exhausting My anger against them in the wilderness.
Ezekiel20:22 But I withheld My hand and acted because of My name
Ezekiel20:23 However
Ezekiel20:24 For they did not practice My ordinances but rejected My statutes and profaned My Sabbaths
Ezekiel20:25 I also gave them statutes that were not good and ordinances that did not bring them life.
Ezekiel20:26 When they made every firstborn pass through [the fire]
Ezekiel20:27 Therefore
Ezekiel20:28 When I brought them into the land that I swore to give them and they saw any high hill or leafy tree
Ezekiel20:29 So I asked them: What is this high place you are going to? And it is called High Place to this day.
Ezekiel20:30 Therefore say to the house of Israel: This is what the Lord God says: Are you defiling yourselves the way your fathers did
Ezekiel20:31 When you offer your gifts
Ezekiel20:32 When you say: Let us be like the nations
Ezekiel20:33 As I live-- the declaration of the Lord God-- I will rule over you with a strong hand
Ezekiel20:34 I will bring you from the peoples and gather you from the countries where you were scattered
Ezekiel20:35 I will lead you into the wilderness of the peoples and enter into judgment with you there face to face.
Ezekiel20:36 Just as I entered into judgment with your fathers in the wilderness of the land of Egypt
Ezekiel20:37 I will make you pass under the rod and will bring you into the bond of the covenant.
Ezekiel20:38 And I will also purge you of those who rebel and transgress against Me. I will bring them out of the land where they live as foreign residents
Ezekiel20:39 As for you
Ezekiel20:40 For on My holy mountain
Ezekiel20:41 When I bring you from the peoples and gather you from the countries where you have been scattered
Ezekiel20:42 When I lead you into the land of Israel
Ezekiel20:43 There you will remember your ways and all your deeds you have defiled yourselves with
Ezekiel20:44 You will know that I am the LORD

Ezekiel21:15 I have appointed a sword for slaughter at all their gates

Ezekiel21:19 Now you

Ezekiel22:7 Father and mother are treated with contempt

Ezekiel22:10 Men within you have sexual intercourse with [their] father's wife
Ezekiel22:11 One man within you commits an abomination with his neighbor's wife; another wickedly defiles his daughter-in-law; and [yet] another violates his sister
Ezekiel22:12 People who [live] in you accept bribes in order to shed blood. You take interest and profit [on a loan] and brutally extort your neighbors. You have forgotten Me. [This is] the declaration of the Lord God.

Ezekiel22:14 Will your courage endure or your hands be strong in the days when I deal with you? I

Ezekiel22:31 So I have poured out My indignation on them and consumed them with the fire of My fury. I have brought their actions down on their own heads. [This is] the declaration of the Lord God.

Ezekiel23:10 They exposed her nakedness

Ezekiel23:15 wearing belts on their waists and flowing turbans on their heads; all of them looked like officers

Ezekiel23:29 They will treat you with hatred
Ezekiel23:30 These things will be done to you because you acted like a prostitute with the nations

Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed adultery
Ezekiel23:38 They also did this to Me: they defiled My sanctuary on that same day and profaned My Sabbaths.
Ezekiel23:39 On the same day they slaughtered their children for their idols
Ezekiel23:40 In addition
Ezekiel23:41 You sat on a luxurious couch with a table spread before it
Ezekiel23:42 The sound of a carefree crowd was there. Drunkards from the desert were brought in
Ezekiel23:43 Then I said concerning this woman worn out by adultery: Will they now have illicit sex with her
Ezekiel23:44 Yet they had sex with her as one does with a prostitute. This is how they had sex with Oholah and Oholibah
Ezekiel23:45 But righteous men will judge them the way adulteresses and those who shed blood are judged
Ezekiel23:46 This is what the Lord God says: Summon an assembly against them and consign them to terror and plunder.
Ezekiel23:47 The assembly will stone them and cut them down with their swords. They will kill their sons and daughters and burn their houses with fire.
Ezekiel23:48 So I will put an end to indecency in the land
Ezekiel23:49 They will repay you for your indecency
Ezekiel24:1 The word of the LORD came to me in the ninth year

Ezekiel24:4 Place the pieces of meat in it
Ezekiel24:5 Take the choicest of the flock and also pile up the fuel under it. Bring it to a boil and cook the bones in it.
Ezekiel24:6 Therefore
Ezekiel24:7 For the blood she shed is in her midst. She put it out on the bare rock; she didn't pour it on the ground to cover it with dust.
Ezekiel24:8 In order to stir up wrath and take vengeance
Ezekiel24:9 Therefore
Ezekiel24:10 Pile on the logs and kindle the fire! Cook the meat well and mix in the spices! Let the bones be burned!
Ezekiel24:11 Set the empty pot on its coals so that it becomes hot and its copper glows. Then its impurity will melt inside it; its rust will be consumed.
Ezekiel24:12 It has frustrated every effort; its thick rust will not come off. Into the fire with its rust!

Ezekiel27:27 Your wealth

Ezekiel27:33 When your merchandise was unloaded from the seas
Ezekiel27:34 Now you are shattered by the sea in the depths of the waters; your goods and the people within you have gone down.

Ezekiel27:36 Those who trade among the peoples hiss at you; you have become an object of horror and will never exist again.

Ezekiel31:1 In the eleventh year
Ezekiel31:2 Son of man
Ezekiel31:3 Think of Assyria
Ezekiel31:4 The waters caused it to grow; the underground springs made it tall
Ezekiel31:5 Therefore the cedar became greater in height than all the trees of the field. Its branches multiplied
Ezekiel31:6 All the birds of the sky nested in its branches
Ezekiel31:7 It was beautiful in its greatness
Ezekiel31:8 The cedars in God's garden could not rival it; the pine trees couldn't compare with its branches
Ezekiel31:9 I made it beautiful with its many limbs
Ezekiel31:10 Therefore this is what the Lord God says: Since it became great in height and set its top among the clouds
Ezekiel31:11 I determined to hand it over to a ruler of nations; he would surely deal with it. I banished it because of its wickedness.
Ezekiel31:12 Foreigners
Ezekiel31:13 All the birds of the sky nested on its fallen trunk
Ezekiel31:14 [This happened] so that no trees [planted] beside water would become great in height and set their tops among the clouds
Ezekiel31:15 This is what the Lord God says: I caused grieving on the day the cedar went down to Sheol. I closed off the underground deep because of it: I held back the rivers of the deep
Ezekiel31:16 I made the nations quake at the sound of its downfall
Ezekiel31:17 They too descended with it to Sheol
Ezekiel31:18 Who then are you like in glory and greatness among Eden's trees? You also will be brought down to the underworld [to be] with the trees of Eden. You will lie among the uncircumcised with those slain by the sword. This is Pharaoh and all his hordes-- the declaration of the Lord God.

Ezekiel40:1 In the twenty-fifth year of our exile
Ezekiel40:2 In visions of God He took me to the land of Israel and set me down on a very high mountain. On its southern [slope] was a structure resembling a city.
Ezekiel40:3 He brought me there
Ezekiel40:4 He spoke to me: Son of man
Ezekiel40:5 Now there was a wall surrounding the outside of the temple. The measuring rod in the man's hand was six units of 21 inches; each unit was the standard length plus three inches. He measured the thickness of the [wall] structure; it was about 10 feet
Ezekiel40:6 Then he came to the gate that faced east and climbed its steps. He measured the threshold of the gate; it was 10 feet deep-- the first threshold was 10 feet deep.
Ezekiel40:7 Each recess was about 10 feet long and 10 feet deep
Ezekiel40:8 Next he measured the portico of the gate;
Ezekiel40:9 it was 14 feet
Ezekiel40:10 There were three recesses on each side of the east gate
Ezekiel40:11 Then he measured the width of the gate's entrance; it was 17 and a half feet
Ezekiel40:12 There was a barrier of 21 inches in front of the recesses on both sides
Ezekiel40:13 Then he measured the gateway from the roof of one recess to the roof of the [opposite] one; the distance was 43 and three-quarter feet. The openings of the recesses faced each other.
Ezekiel40:14 Next
Ezekiel40:15 [The distance] from the front of the gate at the entrance to the front of the gate's portico on the inside was 87 and a half feet.
Ezekiel40:16 The recesses and their pilasters had beveled windows all around the inside of the gateway. The porticos also had windows all around on the inside. Each pilaster was decorated with palm trees.
Ezekiel40:17 Then he brought me into the outer court
Ezekiel40:18 which flanked the gates and corresponded to the length of the gates; [this] was the lower pavement.
Ezekiel40:19 Then he measured the distance from the front of the lower gate to the exterior front of the inner court; it was 175 feet. [This] was the east; next the north [is described].
Ezekiel40:20 He measured the gate of the outer court facing north
Ezekiel40:21 Its three recesses on each side
Ezekiel40:22 Its windows
Ezekiel40:23 The inner court had a gate facing the north gate
Ezekiel40:24 He brought me to the south side
Ezekiel40:25 Both the gate and its portico had windows all around
Ezekiel40:26 Its stairway had seven steps
Ezekiel40:27 The inner court had a gate on the south. He measured from gate to gate on the south; it was 175 feet.
Ezekiel40:28 Then he brought me to the inner court through the south gate. When he measured the south gate
Ezekiel40:29 Its recesses
Ezekiel40:30 (There were porticoes all around
Ezekiel40:31 Its portico faced the outer court
Ezekiel40:32 Then he brought me to the inner court on the east side. When he measured the gate
Ezekiel40:33 Its recesses
Ezekiel40:34 Its portico faced the outer court
Ezekiel40:35 Then he brought me to the north gate. When he measured [it]
Ezekiel40:36 [as did] its recesses
Ezekiel40:37 Its portico faced the outer court
Ezekiel40:38 There was a chamber whose door [opened] into the portico of the gate. The burnt offering was to be washed there.
Ezekiel40:39 Inside the portico of the gate there were two tables on each side
Ezekiel40:40 Outside
Ezekiel40:41 So there were four tables inside the gate and four outside
Ezekiel40:42 There were also four tables of cut stone for the burnt offering
Ezekiel40:43 There were three-inch hooks fastened all around the inside of the room
Ezekiel40:44 Outside the inner gate
Ezekiel40:45 Then the man said to me: This chamber that faces south is for the priests who keep charge of the temple.
Ezekiel40:46 The chamber that faces north is for the priests who keep charge of the altar. These are the sons of Zadok
Ezekiel40:47 Next he measured the court. It was square
Ezekiel40:48 Then he brought me to the portico of the temple and measured the pilasters of the portico; they were eight and three-quarter feet [thick] on each side. The width of the gateway was 24 and a half feet
Ezekiel40:49 The portico was 35 feet across and 21 feet deep
Ezekiel41:1 Next he brought me into the great hall and measured the pilasters; on each side the width of the pilaster was 10 and a half feet.
Ezekiel41:2 The width of the entrance was 17 and a half feet
Ezekiel41:3 He went inside [the next room] and measured the pilasters at the entrance; they were three and a half feet [wide]. The entrance was 10 and a half feet [wide]
Ezekiel41:4 He then measured the length of the room adjacent to the great hall
Ezekiel41:5 Then he measured the wall of the temple; it was 10 and a half feet [thick]. The width of the side rooms all around the temple was seven feet.
Ezekiel41:6 The side rooms were arranged one above another in three stories of 30 rooms each. There were ledges on the wall of the temple all around to serve as supports for the side rooms
Ezekiel41:7 The side rooms surrounding [the temple] widened at each successive story
Ezekiel41:8 I saw that the temple had a raised platform surrounding [it]; this foundation for the side rooms was 10 and a half feet high.
Ezekiel41:9 The thickness of the outer wall of the side rooms was eight and three-quarter feet. The free space between the side rooms of the temple
Ezekiel41:10 and the [outer] chambers was 35 feet wide all around the temple.
Ezekiel41:11 The side rooms opened into the free space
Ezekiel41:12 Now the building that faced the temple yard toward the west was 122 and a half feet wide. The wall of the building was eight and three-quarter feet thick on all sides
Ezekiel41:13 Then the man measured the temple; it was 175 feet long. In addition

Daniel1:8 Daniel determined that he would not defile himself with the king's food or with the wine he drank. So he asked permission from the chief official not to defile himself.

Daniel1:10 yet he said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 So Daniel said to the guard whom the chief official had assigned to Daniel

Daniel1:14 He agreed with them in this matter and tested them for 10 days.
Daniel1:15 At the end of 10 days they looked better and healthier than all the young men who were eating the king's food.
Daniel1:16 So the guard continued to remove their food and the wine they were to drink and gave them vegetables.

Daniel1:19 The king interviewed them

Daniel6:14 As soon as the king heard this
Daniel6:15 Then these men went to the king and said to him
Daniel6:16 So the king gave the order
Daniel6:17 A stone was brought and placed over the mouth of the den. The king sealed it with his own signet ring and with the signet rings of his nobles
Daniel6:18 Then the king went to his palace and spent the night fasting. No diversions were brought to him
Daniel6:19 At the first light of dawn the king got up and hurried to the lions' den.
Daniel6:20 When he reached the den
Daniel6:21 Then Daniel spoke with the king: May the king live forever.
Daniel6:22 My God sent His angel and shut the lions' mouths. They haven't hurt me
Daniel6:23 The king was overjoyed and gave orders to take Daniel out of the den. So Daniel was taken out of the den
Daniel6:24 The king then gave the command
Daniel6:25 Then King Darius wrote to those of every people
Daniel6:26 I issue a decree that in all my royal dominion
Daniel6:27 He rescues and delivers; He performs signs and wonders in the heavens and on the earth
Daniel6:28 So Daniel prospered during the reign of Darius and the reign of Cyrus the Persian.

Daniel8:11 It made itself great
Daniel8:12 Because of rebellion

Daniel11:15 Then the king of the North will come

Daniel11:38 Instead

Hosea2:1  Call your brothers: My People and your sisters: Compassion.
Hosea2:2 Rebuke your mother; rebuke [her]. For she is not My wife and I am not her husband. Let her remove the promiscuous look from her face and her adultery from between her breasts.
Hosea2:3 Otherwise
Hosea2:4 I will have no compassion on her children because they are the children of promiscuity.
Hosea2:5 For their mother is promiscuous; she conceived them and acted shamefully. For she thought: I will go after my lovers
Hosea2:6 Therefore
Hosea2:7 She will pursue her lovers but not catch them; she will seek them but not find [them]. Then she will think: I will go back to my former husband
Hosea2:8 She does not recognize that it is I who gave her the grain
Hosea2:9 Therefore
Hosea2:10 Now I will expose her shame in the sight of her lovers
Hosea2:11 I will put an end to all her celebrations: her feasts
Hosea2:12 I will devastate her vines and fig trees. She thinks that these are her wages that her lovers have given her. I will turn them into a thicket
Hosea2:13 And I will punish her for the days of the Baals when she burned incense to them
Hosea2:14 Therefore
Hosea2:15 There I will give her vineyards back to her and make the Valley of Achor into a gateway of hope. There she will respond as [she did] in the days of her youth
Hosea2:16 In that day-- the LORD's declaration-- you will call [Me]: My husband
Hosea2:17 For I will remove the names of the Baals from her mouth; they will no longer be remembered by their names.
Hosea2:18 On that day I will make a covenant for them with the wild animals
Hosea2:19 I will take you to be My wife forever. I will take you to be My wife in righteousness
Hosea2:20 I will take you to be My wife in faithfulness
Hosea2:21 On that day I will respond-- the LORD's declaration. I will respond to the sky
Hosea2:22 The earth will respond to the grain
Hosea2:23 I will sow her in the land for Myself

Joel2:7 They attack as warriors [attack]; they scale walls as men of war [do]. Each goes on his own path
Joel2:8 They do not push each other; each man proceeds on his own path. They dodge the missiles
Joel2:9 They storm the city; they run on the wall; they climb into the houses; they enter through the windows like thieves.
Joel2:10 The earth quakes before them; the sky shakes. The sun and moon grow dark
Joel2:11 The LORD raises His voice in the presence of His army. His camp is very large; Those who carry out His command are powerful. Indeed
Joel2:12 Even now-- [this is] the LORD's declaration-- turn to Me with all your heart
Joel2:13 Tear your hearts
Joel2:14 Who knows? He may turn and relent and leave a blessing behind Him
Joel2:15 Blow the horn in Zion! Announce a sacred fast; proclaim an assembly.
Joel2:16 Gather the people; sanctify the congregation; assemble the aged; gather the children
Joel2:17 Let the priests
Joel2:18 Then the LORD became jealous for His land and spared His people.

Joel2:20 I will drive the northerner far from you and banish him to a dry and desolate land
Joel2:21 Don't be afraid
Joel2:22 Don't be afraid
Joel2:23 Children of Zion

Joel2:25 I will repay you for the years that the swarming locust ate
Joel2:26 You will have plenty to eat and be satisfied. You will praise the name of Yahweh your God
Joel2:27 You will know that I am present in Israel and that I am the LORD your God
Joel2:28  After this I will pour out My Spirit on all humanity; then your sons and your daughters will prophesy
Joel2:29 I will even pour out My Spirit on the male and female slaves in those days.

Amos2:1 The LORD says: I will not relent from punishing Moab for three crimes
Amos2:2 Therefore
Amos2:3 I will cut off the judge from the land and kill all its officials with him. The LORD has spoken.

Amos2:5 Therefore
Amos2:6 The LORD says: I will not relent from punishing Israel for three crimes
Amos2:7 They trample the heads of the poor on the dust of the ground and block the path of the needy. A man and his father have sexual relations with the same girl
Amos2:8 They stretch out beside every altar on garments taken as collateral
Amos2:9 Yet I destroyed the Amorite as Israel advanced; his height was like the cedars
Amos2:10 And I brought you from the land of Egypt and led you 40 years in the wilderness in order to possess the land of the Amorite.
Amos2:11 I raised up some of your sons as prophets and some of your young men as Nazirites. Is this not the case
Amos2:12 But you made the Nazirites drink wine and commanded the prophets: Do not prophesy.

Amos2:14 Escape will fail the swift
Amos2:15 The archer will not stand [his ground]

Amos3:1 Listen to this message that the LORD has spoken against you
Amos3:2 I have known only you out of all the clans of the earth; therefore
Amos3:3 Can two walk together without agreeing to meet?
Amos3:4 Does a lion roar in the forest when it has no prey? Does a young lion growl from its lair unless it has captured [something]?
Amos3:5 Does a bird land in a trap on the ground if there is no bait for it? Does a trap spring from the ground when it has caught nothing?
Amos3:6 If a ram's horn is blown in a city
Amos3:7 Indeed
Amos3:8 A lion has roared; who will not fear? The Lord God has spoken; who will not prophesy?

Jonah1:5 The sailors were afraid

Jonah1:9 He answered them

Jonah1:12 He answered them

Jonah3:3 So Jonah got up and went to Nineveh according to the LORD's command. Now Nineveh was an extremely large city
Jonah3:4 Jonah set out on the first day of his walk in the city and proclaimed
Jonah3:5 The men of Nineveh believed in God. They proclaimed a fast and dressed in sackcloth-- from the greatest of them to the least.

Micah7:2 Godly people have vanished from the land; there is no one upright among the people. All of them wait in ambush to shed blood; they hunt each other with a net.
Micah7:3 Both hands are good at accomplishing evil: the official and the judge demand a bribe; when the powerful man communicates his evil desire
Micah7:4 The best of them is like a brier; the most upright is worse than a hedge of thorns. The day of your watchmen
Micah7:5 Do not rely on a friend; don't trust in a close companion. Seal your mouth from the woman who lies in your arms.

Micah7:18 Who is a God like You
Micah7:19 He will again have compassion on us; He will vanquish our iniquities. You will cast all our sins into the depths of the sea.
Micah7:20 You will show loyalty to Jacob and faithful love to Abraham
Nahum1:1 The oracle concerning Nineveh. The book of the vision of Nahum the Elkoshite.
Nahum1:2 The LORD is a jealous and avenging God; the LORD takes vengeance and is fierce in wrath. The LORD takes vengeance against His foes; He is furious with His enemies.
Nahum1:3 The LORD is slow to anger but great in power; the LORD will never leave [the guilty] unpunished. His path is in the whirlwind and storm
Nahum1:4 He rebukes the sea so that it dries up
Nahum1:5 The mountains quake before Him
Nahum1:6 Who can withstand His indignation? Who can endure His burning anger? His wrath is poured out like fire
Nahum1:7 The LORD is good
Nahum1:8 But He will completely destroy Nineveh with an overwhelming flood
Nahum1:9 Whatever you plot against the Lord
Nahum1:10 For they will be consumed like entangled thorns
Nahum1:11 One has gone out from Nineveh
Nahum1:12 This is what the LORD says: Though they are strong and numerous
Nahum1:13 For I will now break off his yoke from you and tear off your shackles.
Nahum1:14 The LORD has issued an order concerning you: There will be no offspring to carry on your name. I will eliminate the carved idol and cast image from the house of your gods; I will prepare your grave
Nahum1:15  Look to the mountains-- the feet of one bringing good news and proclaiming peace! Celebrate your festivals
Nahum2:1 One who scatters is coming up against you. Man the fortifications! Watch the road! Brace yourself! Summon all your strength!
Nahum2:2 For the LORD will restore the majesty of Jacob
Nahum2:3 The shields of his warriors are dyed red; the valiant men are dressed in scarlet. The fittings of the chariot flash like fire on the day of its [battle] preparations
Nahum2:4 The chariots dash madly through the streets; they rush around in the plazas. They look like torches; they dart back and forth like lightning.
Nahum2:5 He gives orders to his officers; they stumble as they advance. They race to its wall; the protective shield is set in place.
Nahum2:6 The river gates are opened
Nahum2:7 Beauty is stripped
Nahum2:8 Nineveh has been like a pool of water from her [first] days
Nahum2:9 Plunder the silver! Plunder the gold! There is no end to the treasure
Nahum2:10 Desolation
Nahum2:11 Where is the lions' lair
Nahum2:12 The lion mauled whatever its cubs needed and strangled [prey] for its lionesses. It filled up its dens with the kill
Nahum2:13 Beware
Nahum3:1 Woe to the city of blood
Nahum3:2 The crack of the whip and rumble of the wheel
Nahum3:3 Charging horseman
Nahum3:4 Because of the continual prostitution of the prostitute
Nahum3:5 I am against you-- the declaration of the Lord of Hosts. I will lift your skirts over your face and display your nakedness to nations
Nahum3:6 I will throw filth on you and treat you with contempt; I will make a spectacle of you.
Nahum3:7 Then all who see you will recoil from you
Nahum3:8 Are you better than Thebes that sat along the Nile with water surrounding her
Nahum3:9 Cush and Egypt were her endless source of strength; Put and Libya were among her allies.
Nahum3:10 Yet she became an exile; she went into captivity. Her children were also dashed to pieces at the head of every street. They cast lots for her dignitaries
Nahum3:11 You also will become drunk; you will hide yourself. You also will seek refuge from the enemy.
Nahum3:12 All your fortresses are fig trees with figs that ripened first; when shaken
Nahum3:13 Look
Nahum3:14 Draw water for the siege; strengthen your fortresses. Step into the clay and tread the mortar; take hold of the brick-mold!
Nahum3:15 The fire will devour you there; the sword will cut you down. It will devour you like the young locust. Multiply yourselves like the young locust
Nahum3:16 You have made your merchants more numerous than the stars of the sky. The young locust strips [the land] and flies away.
Nahum3:17 Your court officials are like the swarming locust
Nahum3:18 King of Assyria
Nahum3:19 There is no remedy for your injury; your wound is severe. All who hear the news about you will clap their hands because of you
Habakkuk1:1 The oracle that Habakkuk the prophet saw.
Habakkuk1:2 How long
Habakkuk1:3 Why do You force me to look at injustice? Why do You tolerate wrongdoing? Oppression and violence are right in front of me. Strife is ongoing
Habakkuk1:4 This is why the law is ineffective and justice never emerges. For the wicked restrict the righteous; therefore
Habakkuk1:5 Look at the nations and observe-- be utterly astounded! For something is taking place in your days that you will not believe when you hear about it.
Habakkuk1:6 Look! I am raising up the Chaldeans
Habakkuk1:7 They are fierce and terrifying; their views of justice and sovereignty stem from themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses are swifter than leopards and more fierce than wolves of the night. Their horsemen charge ahead; their horsemen come from distant [lands]. They fly like an eagle
Habakkuk1:9 All of them come to do violence; their faces are set in determination. They gather prisoners like sand.
Habakkuk1:10 They mock kings
Habakkuk1:11 Then they sweep by like the wind and pass through. They are guilty; their strength is their god.
Habakkuk1:12 Are You not from eternity
Habakkuk1:13 [Your] eyes are too pure to look on evil
Habakkuk1:14 You have made mankind like the fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 The Chaldeans pull them all up with a hook
Habakkuk1:16 That is why they sacrifice to their dragnet and burn incense to their fishing net
Habakkuk1:17 Will they therefore empty their net and continually slaughter nations without mercy?
Habakkuk2:1 I will stand at my guard post and station myself on the lookout tower. I will watch to see what He will say to me and what I should reply about my complaint.
Habakkuk2:2 The LORD answered me: Write down this vision; clearly inscribe it on tablets so one may easily read it.
Habakkuk2:3 For the vision is yet for the appointed time; it testifies about the end and will not lie. Though it delays
Habakkuk2:4 Look
Habakkuk2:5 Moreover
Habakkuk2:6 Won't all of these take up a taunt against him
Habakkuk2:7 Won't your creditors suddenly arise
Habakkuk2:8 Since you have plundered many nations
Habakkuk2:9 Woe to him who unjustly gains wealth for his house to place his nest on high
Habakkuk2:10 You have planned shame for your house by wiping out many peoples and sinning against your own self.
Habakkuk2:11 For the stones will cry out from the wall
Habakkuk2:12 Woe to him who builds a city with bloodshed and founds a town with injustice!
Habakkuk2:13 Is it not from the LORD of Hosts
Habakkuk2:14 For the earth will be filled with the knowledge of the LORD's glory
Habakkuk2:15 Woe to him who gives his neighbors drink
Habakkuk2:16 You will be filled with disgrace instead of glory. You also-- drink
Habakkuk2:17 For [your] violence against Lebanon will overwhelm you; the destruction of animals will terrify you
Habakkuk2:18 What use is a carved idol after its craftsman carves it? It is [only] a cast image
Habakkuk2:19 Woe to him who says to wood: Wake up! or to mute stone: Come alive! Can it teach? Look! It may be plated with gold and silver
Habakkuk2:20 But the LORD is in His holy temple; let everyone on earth be silent in His presence.
Habakkuk3:1 A prayer of Habakkuk the prophet. According to Shigionoth.
Habakkuk3:2 LORD
Habakkuk3:3 God comes from Teman
Habakkuk3:4 [His] brilliance is like light; rays are flashing from His hand. This is where His power is hidden.
Habakkuk3:5 Plague goes before Him
Habakkuk3:6 He stands and shakes the earth; He looks and startles the nations. The age-old mountains break apart; the ancient hills sink down. His pathways are ancient.
Habakkuk3:7 I see the tents of Cushan in distress; the tent curtains of the land of Midian tremble.
Habakkuk3:8 Are You angry at the rivers
Habakkuk3:9 You took the sheath from Your bow; the arrows are ready to be used with an oath. Selah You split the earth with rivers.
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains see You and shudder; a downpour of water sweeps by. The deep roars with its voice and lifts its waves high.
Habakkuk3:11 Sun and moon stand still in [their] lofty residence
Habakkuk3:12 You march across the earth with indignation; You trample down the nations in wrath.
Habakkuk3:13 You come out to save Your people
Habakkuk3:14 You pierce his head with his own spears; his warriors storm out to scatter us
Habakkuk3:15 You tread the sea with Your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I heard
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree does not bud and there is no fruit on the vines
Habakkuk3:18 yet I will triumph in the LORD; I will rejoice in the God of my salvation!
Habakkuk3:19 Yahweh my Lord is my strength; He makes my feet like those of a deer and enables me to walk on mountain heights! [For the choir director: on stringed instruments.]

Zephaniah1:2 I will completely sweep away everything from the face of the earth-- [this is] the LORD's declaration.
Zephaniah1:3 I will sweep away man and animal; I will sweep away the birds of the sky and the fish of the sea
Zephaniah1:4 I will stretch out My hand against Judah and against all the residents of Jerusalem. I will cut off from this place every vestige of Baal

Zephaniah1:15 That day is a day of wrath

Zephaniah1:17 I will bring distress on mankind
Zephaniah1:18 Their silver and their gold will not be able to rescue them on the day of the LORD's wrath. The whole earth will be consumed by the fire of His jealousy. For He will make a complete

Zephaniah3:1 Woe to the city that is rebellious and defiled
Zephaniah3:2 She has not obeyed; she has not accepted discipline. She has not trusted in the LORD; she has not drawn near to her God.
Zephaniah3:3 The princes within her are roaring lions; her judges are wolves of the night
Zephaniah3:4 Her prophets are reckless-- treacherous men. Her priests profane the sanctuary; they do violence to instruction.
Zephaniah3:5 The righteous LORD is in her; He does no wrong. He applies His justice morning by morning; He does not fail at dawn
Zephaniah3:6 I have cut off nations; their corner towers are destroyed. I have laid waste their streets
Zephaniah3:7 I thought: You will certainly fear Me and accept correction. Then her dwelling place would not be cut off [based on] all that I had allocated to her. However
Zephaniah3:8 Therefore
Zephaniah3:9 For I will then restore pure speech to the peoples so that all of them may call on the name of Yahweh and serve Him with a single purpose.
Zephaniah3:10 From beyond the rivers of Cush My supplicants
Zephaniah3:11 On that day you will not be put to shame because of everything you have done in rebelling against Me. For then I will remove your boastful braggarts from among you
Zephaniah3:12 I will leave a meek and humble people among you
Zephaniah3:13 The remnant of Israel will no longer do wrong or tell lies; a deceitful tongue will not be found in their mouths. But they will pasture and lie down
Zephaniah3:14 Sing for joy
Zephaniah3:15 The LORD has removed your punishment; He has turned back your enemy. The King of Israel
Zephaniah3:16 On that day it will be said to Jerusalem: Do not fear; Zion
Zephaniah3:17 The LORD your God is among you
Zephaniah3:18 I will gather those who have been driven from the appointed festivals; [They will be] a tribute from you
Zephaniah3:19 Yes
Zephaniah3:20 At that time I will bring you back
Haggai1:1 In the second year of King Darius
Haggai1:2 The LORD of Hosts says this: These people say: The time has not come for the house of the LORD to be rebuilt.

Haggai1:4 Is it a time for you yourselves to live in your paneled houses
Haggai1:5 Now
Haggai1:6 You have planted much but harvested little. You eat but never have enough to be satisfied. You drink but never have enough to become drunk. You put on clothes but never have enough to get warm. The wage earner [puts his] wages into a bag with a hole in it.
Haggai1:7 The LORD of Hosts says this: Think carefully about your ways.
Haggai1:8 Go up into the hills
Haggai1:9 You expected much
Haggai1:10 So on your account
Haggai1:11 I have summoned a drought on the fields and the hills
Haggai1:12 Then Zerubbabel son of Shealtiel
Haggai1:13 Haggai

Haggai2:6 For the LORD of Hosts says this: Once more
Haggai2:7 I will shake all the nations so that the treasures of all the nations will come
Haggai2:8 The silver and gold belong to Me-- the declaration of the LORD of Hosts.
Haggai2:9 The final glory of this house will be greater than the first
Haggai2:10 On the twenty-fourth day of the ninth [month]

Zechariah1:1 In the eighth month
Zechariah1:2 The LORD was extremely angry with your ancestors.
Zechariah1:3 So tell the people: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Return to Me-- [this is] the declaration of the LORD of Hosts-- and I will return to you
Zechariah1:4 Do not be like your ancestors; the earlier prophets proclaimed to them: This is what the LORD of Hosts says: Turn from your evil ways and your evil deeds. But they did not listen or pay attention to Me-- the LORD's declaration.
Zechariah1:5 Where are your ancestors now? And do the prophets live forever?
Zechariah1:6 But didn't My words and My statutes that I commanded My servants the prophets overtake your ancestors? They repented and said: As the LORD of Hosts purposed to deal with us for our ways and deeds

Zechariah1:12 Then the Angel of the LORD responded
Zechariah1:13 The LORD replied with kind and comforting words to the angel who was speaking with me.

Zechariah1:15 I am fiercely angry with the nations that are at ease
Zechariah1:16 Therefore

Zechariah1:19 So I asked the angel who was speaking with me

Zechariah7:2 Now [the people of] Bethel had sent Sharezer
Zechariah7:3 by asking the priests who were at the house of the LORD of Hosts as well as the prophets

Zechariah7:5 Ask all the people of the land and the priests: When you fasted and lamented in the fifth and in the seventh [months] for these 70 years
Zechariah7:6 When you eat and drink
Zechariah7:7 Aren't [these] the words that the LORD proclaimed through the earlier prophets when Jerusalem was inhabited and secure

Zechariah7:9 The LORD of Hosts says this: Render true justice. Show faithful love and compassion to one another.
Zechariah7:10 Do not oppress the widow or the fatherless
Zechariah7:11 But they refused to pay attention and turned a stubborn shoulder; they closed their ears so they could not hear.

Zechariah8:1 The word of the LORD of Hosts came:
Zechariah8:2 The LORD of Hosts says this: I am extremely jealous for Zion; I am jealous for her with great wrath.
Zechariah8:3 The LORD says this: I will return to Zion and live in Jerusalem. Then Jerusalem will be called the Faithful City
Zechariah8:4 The LORD of Hosts says this: Old men and women will again sit along the streets of Jerusalem
Zechariah8:5 The streets of the city will be filled with boys and girls playing in them.
Zechariah8:6 The LORD of Hosts says this: Though it may seem incredible to the remnant of this people in those days
Zechariah8:7 The LORD of Hosts says this: I will save My people from the land of the east and the land of the west.
Zechariah8:8 I will bring them [back] to live in Jerusalem. They will be My people
Zechariah8:9 The LORD of Hosts says this: Let your hands be strong
Zechariah8:10 For prior to those days neither man nor beast had wages. There was no safety from the enemy for anyone who came or went
Zechariah8:11 But now
Zechariah8:12 For they will sow in peace: the vine will yield its fruit
Zechariah8:13 As you have been a curse among the nations
Zechariah8:14 For the LORD of Hosts says this: As I resolved to treat you badly when your fathers provoked Me to anger
Zechariah8:15 so I have resolved again in these days to do what is good to Jerusalem and the house of Judah. Don't be afraid.
Zechariah8:16 These are the things you must do: Speak truth to one another; render honest and peaceful judgments in your gates.
Zechariah8:17 Do not plot evil in your hearts against your neighbor
Zechariah8:18 Then the word of the LORD of Hosts came to me:
Zechariah8:19 The LORD of Hosts says this: The fast of the fourth [month]
Zechariah8:20 The LORD of Hosts says this: Peoples will yet come
Zechariah8:21 the residents of one city will go to another
Zechariah8:22 Many peoples and strong nations will come to seek the LORD of Hosts in Jerusalem and to plead for the LORD's favor.
Zechariah8:23 The LORD of Hosts says this: In those days

Zechariah9:2 and also against Hamath
Zechariah9:3 Tyre has built herself a fortress; she has heaped up silver like dust and gold like the dirt of the streets.

Zechariah9:5 Ashkelon will see it and be afraid; Gaza too
Zechariah9:6 A mongrel people will live in Ashdod
Zechariah9:7 I will remove the blood from their mouths and the detestable things from between their teeth. Then they too will become a remnant for our God; they will become like a clan in Judah and Ekron like the Jebusites.

Zechariah9:9 Rejoice greatly
Zechariah9:10 I will cut off the chariot from Ephraim and the horse from Jerusalem. The bow of war will be removed
Zechariah9:11 As for you
Zechariah9:12 Return to a stronghold

Zechariah9:14 Then the LORD will appear over them

Zechariah9:16 The LORD their God will save them on that day as the flock of His people; for they are like jewels in a crown
Zechariah9:17 How lovely and beautiful they will be! Grain will make the young men flourish

Zechariah10:2 For the idols speak falsehood
Zechariah10:3 My anger burns against the shepherds

Zechariah10:5 they will be like warriors in battle trampling down the mud of the streets. They will fight because the LORD is with them

Zechariah11:2 Wail

Zechariah11:12 Then I said to them
Zechariah11:13 Throw it to the potter
Zechariah11:14 Then I cut in two my second staff
Zechariah11:15 The LORD also said to me: Take the equipment of a foolish shepherd.
Zechariah11:16 I am about to raise up a shepherd in the land who will not care for those who are going astray

Zechariah12:1 An Oracle The word of the LORD concerning Israel. A declaration of the LORD
Zechariah12:2 Look
Zechariah12:3 On that day I will make Jerusalem a heavy stone for all the peoples; all who try to lift it will injure themselves severely when all the nations of the earth gather against her.
Zechariah12:4 On that day-- the LORD's declaration-- I will strike every horse with panic and its rider with madness. I will keep a watchful eye on the house of Judah but strike all the horses of the nations with blindness.
Zechariah12:5 Then [each of] the leaders of Judah will think to himself: The residents of Jerusalem are my strength through the LORD of Hosts
Zechariah12:6 On that day I will make the leaders of Judah like a firepot in a woodpile
Zechariah12:7 The LORD will save the tents of Judah first
Zechariah12:8 On that day the LORD will defend the inhabitants of Jerusalem
Zechariah12:9 On that day I will set out to destroy all the nations that come against Jerusalem.
Zechariah12:10 Then I will pour out a spiritof grace and prayer on the house of David and the residents of Jerusalem
Zechariah12:11 On that day the mourning in Jerusalem will be as great as the mourning of Hadad-rimmon in the plain of Megiddo.
Zechariah12:12 The land will mourn
Zechariah12:13 the family of Levi's house by itself and their women by themselves; the family of Shimei by itself and their women by themselves;
Zechariah12:14 all the remaining families
Zechariah13:1 On that day a fountain will be opened for the house of David and for the residents of Jerusalem
Zechariah13:2 On that day-- the declaration of the LORD of Hosts-- I will erase the names of the idols from the land
Zechariah13:3 If a man still prophesies
Zechariah13:4 On that day every prophet will be ashamed of his vision when he prophesies; they will not put on a hairy cloak in order to deceive.
Zechariah13:5 He will say: I am not a prophet; I am a tiller of the soil
Zechariah13:6 If someone asks him: What are these wounds on your chest?-- then he will answer: The wounds I received in the house of my friends.
Zechariah13:7 Sword
Zechariah13:8 In the whole land-- the LORD's declaration-- two-thirds will be cut off and die
Zechariah13:9 I will put this third through the fire; I will refine them as silver is refined and test them as gold is tested. They will call on My name
Zechariah14:1 A day of the LORD is coming when your plunder will be divided in your presence.
Zechariah14:2 I will gather all the nations against Jerusalem for battle. The city will be captured
Zechariah14:3 Then the LORD will go out to fight against those nations as He fights on a day of battle.
Zechariah14:4 On that day His feet will stand on the Mount of Olives
Zechariah14:5 You will flee by My mountain valley
Zechariah14:6 On that day there will be no light; the sunlight and moonlight will diminish.
Zechariah14:7 It will be a day known [only] to Yahweh
Zechariah14:8 On that day living water will flow out from Jerusalem
Zechariah14:9 On that day Yahweh will become king over all the earth-- Yahweh alone
Zechariah14:10 All the land from Geba to Rimmon south of Jerusalem will be changed into a plain. But [Jerusalem] will be raised up and will remain on its site from the Benjamin Gate to the place of the First Gate
Zechariah14:11 People will live there
Zechariah14:12 This will be the plague the LORD strikes all the peoples with
Zechariah14:13 On that day a great panic from the LORD will be among them
Zechariah14:14 Judah will also fight at Jerusalem
Zechariah14:15 The same plague as the previous one will strike the horses
Zechariah14:16 Then all the survivors from the nations that came against Jerusalem will go up year after year to worship the King
Zechariah14:17 Should any of the families of the earth not go up to Jerusalem to worship the King
Zechariah14:18 And if the people of Egypt will not go up and enter
Zechariah14:19 This will be the punishment of Egypt and all the nations that do not go up to celebrate the Festival of Booths.
Zechariah14:20 On that day
Zechariah14:21 Every pot in Jerusalem and in Judah will be holy to the LORD of Hosts. Everyone who sacrifices will come and take some of the pots to cook in. And on that day there will no longer be a Canaanite in the house of the LORD of Hosts.
Malachi1:1 An oracle: The word of the LORD to Israel through Malachi.
Malachi1:2 I have loved you
Malachi1:3 but I hated Esau. I turned his mountains into a wasteland
Malachi1:4 Though Edom says: We have been devastated
Malachi1:5 Your own eyes will see this
Malachi1:6 A son honors [his] father
Malachi1:7 By presenting defiled food on My altar. You ask: How have we defiled You? When you say: The LORD's table is contemptible.
Malachi1:8 When you present a blind [animal] for sacrifice
Malachi1:9 And now ask for God's favor. Will He be gracious to us? [Since] this has come from your hands
Malachi1:10 I wish one of you would shut the [temple] doors
Malachi1:11 For My name will be great among the nations
Malachi1:12 But you are profaning itwhen you say: The Lord's table is defiled
Malachi1:13 You also say: Look
Malachi1:14 The deceiver is cursed who has an [acceptable] male in his flock and makes a vow but sacrifices a defective [animal] to the Lord. For I am a great King

Malachi2:7 For the lips of a priest should guard knowledge
Malachi2:8 You
Malachi2:9 So I in turn have made you despised and humiliated before all the people because you are not keeping My ways but are showing partiality in [your] instruction.

Malachi2:11 Judah has acted treacherously
Malachi2:12 To the man who does this
Malachi2:13 And this is another thing you do: you cover the LORD's altar with tears
Malachi2:14 Yet you ask

Malachi2:16 If he hates and divorces [his wife]

Malachi3:2 But who can endure the day of His coming? And who will be able to stand when He appears? For He will be like a refiner's fire and like cleansing lye.
Malachi3:3 He will be like a refiner and purifier of silver; He will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and silver. Then they will present offerings to the LORD in righteousness.
Malachi3:4 And the offerings of Judah and Jerusalem will please the LORD as in days of old and years gone by.
Malachi3:5 I will come to you in judgment
Malachi3:6 Because I
Malachi3:7 Since the days of your fathers
Malachi3:8 Will a man rob God? Yet you are robbing Me! You ask: How do we rob You? [By not making the payments] of 10 percent and the contributions.
Malachi3:9 You are suffering under a curse
Malachi3:10 Bring the full 10 percent into the storehouse so that there may be food in My house. Test Me in this way
Malachi3:11 I will rebuke the devourer for you
Malachi3:12 Then all the nations will consider you fortunate
Malachi3:13 Your words against Me are harsh
Malachi3:14 You have said: It is useless to serve God. What have we gained by keeping His requirements and walking mournfully before the LORD of Hosts?
Malachi3:15 So now we consider the arrogant to be fortunate. Not only do those who commit wickedness prosper
Malachi3:16 At that time those who feared the LORD spoke to one another. The LORD took notice and listened. So a book of remembrance was written before Him for those who feared Yahweh and had high regard for His name.
Malachi3:17 They will be Mine
Malachi3:18 So you will again see the difference between the righteous and the wicked

Mark1:1 The beginning of the gospel of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 As it is written in Isaiah the prophet: Look

Mark1:4 John came baptizing in the wilderness and preaching a baptism of repentance for the forgiveness of sins.
Mark1:5 The whole Judean countryside and all the people of Jerusalem were flocking to him
Mark1:6 John wore a camel-hair garment with a leather belt around his waist and ate locusts and wild honey.
Mark1:7 He was preaching: Someone more powerful than I will come after me. I am not worthy to stoop down and untie the strap of His sandals.
Mark1:8 I have baptized you with water

Luke12:19 Then I'll say to myself
Luke12:20 But God said to him
Luke12:21 That's how it is with the one who stores up treasure for himself and is not rich toward God.
Luke12:22 Then He said to His disciples: Therefore I tell you
Luke12:23 For life is more than food and the body more than clothing.
Luke12:24 Consider the ravens: they don't sow or reap; they don't have a storeroom or a barn; yet God feeds them. Aren't you worth much more than the birds?
Luke12:25 Can any of you add a cubit to his height by worrying?
Luke12:26 If then you're not able to do even a little thing
Luke12:27 Consider how the wildflowers grow: they don't labor or spin thread. Yet I tell you

Luke12:29 Don't keep striving for what you should eat and what you should drink
Luke12:30 For the Gentile world eagerly seeks all these things
Luke12:31 But seek His kingdom
Luke12:32 Don't be afraid
Luke12:33 Sell your possessions and give to the poor. Make money-bags for yourselves that won't grow old
Luke12:34 For where your treasure is
Luke12:35 Be ready for service and have your lamps lit.

Luke12:37 Those slaves the master will find alert when he comes will be blessed. I assure you: He will get ready
Luke12:38 If he comes in the middle of the night
Luke12:39 But know this: if the homeowner had known at what hour the thief was coming
Luke12:40 You also be ready
Luke12:41 Lord
Luke12:42 The Lord said: Who then is the faithful and sensible manager his master will put in charge of his household servants to give them their allotted food at the proper time?
Luke12:43 That slave whose master finds him working when he comes will be rewarded.
Luke12:44 I tell you the truth: he will put him in charge of all his possessions.
Luke12:45 But if that slave says in his heart
Luke12:46 that slave's master will come on a day he does not expect him and at an hour he does not know. He will cut him to pieces and assign him a place with the unbelievers.
Luke12:47 And that slave who knew his master's will and didn't prepare himself or do it will be severely beaten.
Luke12:48 But the one who did not know and did things deserving of blows will be beaten lightly. Much will be required of everyone who has been given much. And even more will be expected of the one who has been entrusted with more.

Luke12:50 But I have a baptism to be baptized with

Luke12:52 From now on
Luke12:53 They will be divided

John2:5 Do whatever He tells you

John2:10 and told him

John2:13 The Jewish Passover was near

John3:6 Whatever is born of the flesh is flesh
John3:7 Do not be amazed that I told you that you must be born again.

John5:26 For just as the Father has life in Himself

John5:29 and come out-- those who have done good things

John5:34 I don't receive man's testimony

John5:37 The Father who sent Me has Himself testified about Me. You have not heard His voice at any time
John5:38 You don't have His word living in you
John5:39 You pore over the Scriptures because you think you have eternal life in them
John5:40 And you are not willing to come to Me that you may have life.
John5:41 I do not accept glory from men
John5:42 but I know you-- that you have no love for God within you.

John6:46 not that anyone has seen the Father except the One who is from God. He has seen the Father.
John6:47 I assure you: Anyone who believes has eternal life.
John6:48 I am the bread of life.
John6:49 Your fathers ate the manna in the wilderness
John6:50 This is the bread that comes down from heaven so that anyone may eat of it and not die.
John6:51 I am the living bread that came down from heaven. If anyone eats of this bread he will live forever. The bread that I will give for the life of the world is My flesh.
John6:52 At that
John6:53 So Jesus said to them
John6:54 Anyone who eats My flesh and drinks My blood has eternal life
John6:55 because My flesh is real food and My blood is real drink.
John6:56 The one who eats My flesh and drinks My blood lives in Me
John6:57 Just as the living Father sent Me and I live because of the Father
John6:58 This is the bread that came down from heaven; it is not like the manna your fathers ate-- and they died. The one who eats this bread will live forever.
John6:59 He said these things while teaching in the synagogue in Capernaum.
John6:60 Therefore
John6:61 Jesus
John6:62 Then what if you were to observe the Son of Man ascending to where He was before?
John6:63 The Spirit is the One who gives life. The flesh doesn't help at all. The words that I have spoken to you are spirit and are life.

Romans1:1 Paul
Romans1:2 which He promised long ago through His prophets in the Holy Scriptures--
Romans1:3 concerning His Son
Romans1:4 and was established as the powerful Son of God by the resurrection from the dead according to the Spirit of holiness.
Romans1:5 We have received grace and apostleship through Him to bring about the obedience of faith among all the nations
Romans1:6 including yourselves who are also Jesus Christ's by calling:
Romans1:7 To all who are in Rome
Romans1:8 First
Romans1:9 For God
Romans1:10 always asking in my prayers that if it is somehow in God's will
Romans1:11 For I want very much to see you
Romans1:12 that is
Romans1:13 Now I want you to know
Romans1:14 I am obligated both to Greeks and barbarians
Romans1:15 So I am eager to preach the good news to you also who are in Rome.
Romans1:16 For I am not ashamed of the gospel
Romans1:17 For in it God's righteousness is revealed from faith to faith
Romans1:18 For God's wrath is revealed from heaven against all godlessness and unrighteousness of people who by their unrighteousness suppress the truth
Romans1:19 since what can be known about God is evident among them
Romans1:20 From the creation of the world His invisible attributes
Romans1:21 For though they knew God
Romans1:22 Claiming to be wise
Romans1:23 and exchanged the glory of the immortal God for images resembling mortal man
Romans1:24 Therefore God delivered them over in the cravings of their hearts to sexual impurity
Romans1:25 They exchanged the truth of God for a lie
Romans1:26 This is why God delivered them over to degrading passions. For even their females exchanged natural sexual intercourse for what is unnatural.
Romans1:27 The males in the same way also left natural sexual intercourse with females and were inflamed in their lust for one another. Males committed shameless acts with males and received in their own persons the appropriate penalty for their perversion.
Romans1:28 And because they did not think it worthwhile to have God in their knowledge
Romans1:29 They are filled with all unrighteousness
Romans1:30 slanderers
Romans1:31 undiscerning
Romans1:32 Although they know full well God's just sentence-- that those who practice such things deserve to die-- they not only do them
Romans2:1 Therefore
Romans2:2 We know that God's judgment on those who do such things is based on the truth.
Romans2:3 Do you really think-- anyone of you who judges those who do such things yet do the same-- that you will escape God's judgment?
Romans2:4 Or do you despise the riches of His kindness
Romans2:5 But because of your hardness and unrepentant heart you are storing up wrath for yourself in the day of wrath
Romans2:6 He will repay each one according to his works:
Romans2:7 eternal life to those who by patiently doing good seek for glory
Romans2:8 but wrath and indignation to those who are self-seeking and disobey the truth
Romans2:9 affliction and distress for every human being who does evil
Romans2:10 but glory
Romans2:11 There is no favoritism with God.
Romans2:12 All those who sinned without the law will also perish without the law
Romans2:13 For the hearers of the law are not righteous before God
Romans2:14 So
Romans2:15 They show that the work of the law is written on their hearts. Their consciences testify in support of this
Romans2:16 on the day when God judges what people have kept secret
Romans2:17 Now if you call yourself a Jew
Romans2:18 and know His will

Ephesians1:15 This is why

Ephesians1:19 and what is the immeasurable greatness of His power to us who believe
Ephesians1:20 He demonstrated [this power] in the Messiah by raising Him from the dead and seating Him at His right hand in the heavens--
Ephesians1:21 far above every ruler and authority
Ephesians1:22 And He put everything under His feet and appointed Him as head over everything for the church

Hebrews9:8 The Holy Spirit was making it clear that the way into the holy of holies had not yet been disclosed while the first tabernacle was still standing.
Hebrews9:9 This is a symbol for the present time
Hebrews9:10 They are physical regulations and only deal with food

Hebrews9:14 how much more will the blood of the Messiah
Hebrews9:15 Therefore He is the mediator of a new covenant
Hebrews9:16 Where a will exists
Hebrews9:17 For a will is valid only when people die
Hebrews9:18 That is why even the first covenant was inaugurated with blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when every commandment had been proclaimed by Moses to all the people according to the law

1John3:2 Dear friends
1John3:3 And everyone who has this hope in Him purifies himself just as He is pure.
1John3:4 Everyone who commits sin also breaks the law; sin is the breaking of law.
1John3:5 You know that He was revealed so that He might take away sins
1John3:6 Everyone who remains in Him does not sin; everyone who sins has not seen Him or known Him.
1John3:7 Little children
1John3:8 The one who commits sin is of the Devil
1John3:9 Everyone who has been born of God does not sin
1John3:10 This is how God's children-- and the Devil's children-- are made evident. Whoever does not do what is right is not of God
1John3:11 For this is the message you have heard from the beginning: we should love one another
1John3:12 unlike Cain
1John3:13 Do not be surprised
1John3:14 We know that we have passed from death to life because we love our brothers. The one who does not love remains in death.
1John3:15 Everyone who hates his brother is a murderer
1John3:16 This is how we have come to know love: He laid down His life for us. We should also lay down our lives for our brothers.
1John3:17 If anyone has this world's goods and sees his brother in need but shuts off his compassion from him-- how can God's love reside in him?
1John3:18 Little children
1John3:19 that is how we will know we are of the truth
1John3:20 because if our hearts condemn us

1John4:1 Dear friends
1John4:2 This is how you know the Spirit of God: Every spirit who confesses that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh is from God.
1John4:3 But every spirit who does not confess Jesus is not from God. This is the spirit of the antichrist; you have heard that he is coming
1John4:4 You are from God
1John4:5 They are from the world. Therefore what they say is from the world
1John4:6 We are from God. Anyone who knows God listens to us; anyone who is not from God does not listen to us. From this we know the Spirit of truth and the spirit of deception.
1John4:7 Dear friends
1John4:8 The one who does not love does not know God
1John4:9 God's love was revealed among us in this way: God sent His One and Only Son into the world so that we might live through Him.
1John4:10 Love consists in this: not that we loved God
1John4:11 Dear friends

Revelation16:4 The third poured out his bowl into the rivers and the springs of water
Revelation16:5 I heard the angel of the waters say: You are righteous
Revelation16:6 Because they poured out the blood of the saints and the prophets
Revelation16:7 Then I heard someone from the altar say: Yes
Revelation16:8 The fourth poured out his bowl on the sun. He was given the power to burn people with fire
Revelation16:9 and people were burned by the intense heat. So they blasphemed the name of God who had the power over these plagues
Revelation16:10 The fifth poured out his bowl on the throne of the beast
Revelation16:11 and blasphemed the God of heaven because of their pains and their sores

Revelation16:14 For they are spirits of demons performing signs

Revelation16:18 There were lightnings
Revelation16:19 The great city split into three parts
Revelation16:20 Every island fled
Revelation16:21 Enormous hailstones

Revelation17:11 The beast that was and is not

Revelation17:13 These have one purpose
Revelation17:14 These will make war against the Lamb

Revelation17:17 For God has put it into their hearts to carry out His plan by having one purpose

Revelation18:1 After this I saw another angel with great authority coming down from heaven
Revelation18:2 He cried in a mighty voice: It has fallen
Revelation18:3 For all the nations have drunk the wine of her sexual immorality
Revelation18:4 Then I heard another voice from heaven: Come out of her
Revelation18:5 For her sins are piled up to heaven
Revelation18:6 Pay her back the way she also paid
Revelation18:7 As much as she glorified herself and lived luxuriously
Revelation18:8 therefore her plagues will come in one day-- death
Revelation18:9 The kings of the earth who have committed sexual immorality and lived luxuriously with her will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke of her burning.
Revelation18:10 They stand far off in fear of her torment
Revelation18:11 The merchants of the earth will also weep and mourn over her
Revelation18:12 merchandise of gold
Revelation18:13 cinnamon
Revelation18:14 The fruit you craved has left you. All your splendid and glamorous things are gone; they will never find them again.
Revelation18:15 The merchants of these things
Revelation18:16 saying: Woe
Revelation18:17 because in a single hour such fabulous wealth was destroyed! And every shipmaster
Revelation18:18 as they watched the smoke from her burning and kept crying out: Who is like the great city?
Revelation18:19 They threw dust on their heads and kept crying out
Revelation18:20 Rejoice over her

Revelation21:15 The one who spoke with me had a gold measuring rod to measure the city

Revelation21:17 Then he measured its wall
Revelation21:18 The building material of its wall was jasper
Revelation21:19 The foundations of the city wall were adorned with every kind of precious stone: the first foundation jasper
Revelation21:20 the fifth sardonyx
Revelation21:21 The 12 gates are 12 pearls; each individual gate was made of a single pearl. The broad street of the city was pure gold
Revelation21:22 I did not see a sanctuary in it
Revelation21:23 The city does not need the sun or the moon to shine on it
Revelation21:24 The nations will walk in its light

Revelation21:26 They will bring the glory and honor of the nations into it.
Revelation21:27 Nothing profane will ever enter it: no one who does what is vile or false
